docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
This document clarifies how KuuOS describes the medical boundary around Qi motion validation.

The boundary is medical-modality neutral.

It does not state that biomedicine is superior. It does not state that Qi is false. It does not state that East Asian medical reasoning is invalid. It does not reduce Qi, pattern differentiation, or practitioner judgment to a Western biomedical-only frame.

docs.title: Core clarification
docs.text:
KuuOS separates three surfaces:

Qi observation / Qi motion candidate
  != standalone diagnosis
  != standalone treatment authorization
  != medical act authorization

This means only that repository validators, CI checks, and public governance files do not by themselves authorize diagnosis, treatment decisions, procedures, prescriptions, institutional care pathways, or medical acts.

docs.title: What Qi motion validation may support
docs.text:
Qi motion validation may support:

structured observation
pattern-sensitive reasoning
candidate generation
traceable explanation
evidence-bounded review
professional reflection
East Asian medical reasoning support
integrative reasoning support

docs.title: What Qi motion validation must not claim by itself
docs.text:
Qi motion validation must not be treated by itself as:

standalone diagnosis authority
standalone treatment authorization
medical act authorization
execution authority
institutional approval
theorem authority
replacement for practitioner judgment
replacement for patient context

docs.title: Preferred wording
docs.text:
Prefer:

Qi motion validation alone is not standalone diagnosis, standalone treatment authorization, or medical act authorization.

Avoid:

Qi motion validation does not grant clinical authority.

The avoided wording can be misread as implying that only biomedicine is clinically valid or that Qi is non-clinical. That is not the KuuOS position.

docs.title: Governance rule
docs.text:
When medical language is used around Qi, KuuOS should preserve:

medical_modality_neutrality
qi_not_false_by_boundary
biomedicine_not_privileged_by_wording
professional_judgment_required
patient_context_required
validator_not_medical_act_authority

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
KuuOS does not deny Qi.

KuuOS does not deny East Asian medicine.

KuuOS only prevents repository validation, CI pass, and runtime candidate generation from being mistaken for a complete professional diagnosis, treatment decision, or medical act authorization.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes the membrane-and-layer interpretation inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Idea
docs.text:
KuuOS does not treat operational layers as perfectly isolated.

Nor does it allow unrestricted collapse between layers.

Instead, layers interact through membrane-like boundaries.

docs.title: Operational Layers
docs.text:
Examples of layers include:

  • raw AI generation
  • memory surfaces
  • planning surfaces
  • decision surfaces
  • runtime governance surfaces
  • audit surfaces
  • multi-world coordination surfaces

docs.title: Membrane Meaning
docs.text:
Membranes regulate:

  • transport
  • escalation
  • observation
  • admissibility
  • provenance flow
  • rollback visibility

docs.title: Why Membranes Matter
docs.text:
Without membrane constraints:

  • authority collapses too easily
  • memory contaminates decisions
  • simulations become deployments
  • local states silently globalize

docs.title: Governance Consequence
docs.text:
Membranes therefore preserve:

  • contextual separation
  • governance visibility
  • transport conditions
  • claim-level distinctions
  • rollback capability

docs.title: Relation to Mandala
docs.text:
Mandala provides coexistence architecture.

Membranes regulate interactions among operational worlds.

docs.title: Relation to Two Truths Gap
docs.text: The Two Truths Gap acts as a higher-order anti-collapse membrane across ultimate and conventional surfaces.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model defines operating architecture orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: 空OS MemoryOS GitHub 外部メモリー規約
docs.text:
This document defines GitHub as an external MemoryOS surface.

GitHub may store durable pointers, commits, issues, pull requests, workflow runs, artifacts, release references, validation outputs, and repair lineage records.

GitHub is not MemoryOS authority.

docs.title: Core Rule
docs.text:
GitHub_external_memory_is_pointer_and_evidence_surface_not_memory_authority

A GitHub record can show that a repository event, validation event, review event, or release-surface event occurred at a specific external state.

A GitHub record cannot by itself decide truth, proof completion, clinical validity, Ten'i, world-state update, MemoryOS overwrite, or execution permission.

docs.title: Memory Modes
docs.text:
pointer_memory
  repo, path, commit SHA, issue, PR, release, artifact, workflow pointer

evidence_memory
  externally reviewable validation output, CI log, release packet, audit receipt

semantic_digest_memory
  non-authoritative summary for recall; never source evidence

repair_lineage_memory
  issue -> PR -> review -> commit -> validator -> release lineage

conflict_visibility_memory
  contradiction, stale reference, supersession, tombstone, unresolved premise debt

docs.title: Record Schema
docs.text:
memory_id: string
memory_kind: pointer_memory | evidence_memory | semantic_digest_memory | repair_lineage_memory | conflict_visibility_memory
created_at: ISO-8601 timestamp
source_system: github
repository: owner/name
ref_type: commit | file | issue | pull_request | review_comment | workflow_run | artifact | release | tag
ref: string
commit_sha: string | null
path: string | null
url: string | null
content_sha256: string | null
summary: string
authority: external_pointer_only
uncertainty: low | medium | high
constraints:
  no_overwrite: true
  no_execution_authority: true
  no_truth_authority: true
  no_proof_authority: true
  no_clinical_authority: true
  no_teni_authority: true

docs.title: Write Protocol
docs.text:
observe_github_surface
  -> extract_stable_pointer
  -> classify_memory_kind
  -> normalize_external_memory_record
  -> compute_content_hash_when_available
  -> append_record_under_memoryos/external/github
  -> validate_record
  -> expose_as_compile_context_candidate

Recommended path:

memoryos/external/github/YYYY/MM/<memory_id>.yaml

Indexes may be stored under:

memoryos/external/github/indexes/

Indexes are recall aids, not authority.

docs.title: Recall Protocol
docs.text:
input question
  -> search recall index
  -> retrieve stable GitHub pointers
  -> prefer commit SHA over branch name
  -> fetch source evidence when needed
  -> compare current surface with pinned surface
  -> expose conflicts and stale references
  -> compile_context with uncertainty visible

docs.title: Non-Authority Fixed Points
docs.text:
GitHub_external_memory_is_pointer_and_evidence_surface_not_memory_authority
GitHub_commit_is_evidence_not_truth
GitHub_issue_is_discussion_not_memory_authority
GitHub_PR_is_proposal_not_memory_authority
GitHub_CI_pass_is_validation_signal_not_truth
GitHub_branch_HEAD_is_not_stable_memory
GitHub_release_is_public_surface_not_proof_completion
semantic_digest_is_not_source_evidence
MemoryOS_compile_context_must_expose_uncertainty
MemoryOS_no_silent_overwrite
MemoryOS_lineage_preserved
MemoryOS_conflict_visible
MemoryOS_append_only_external_pointer
no_execution_authority
no_clinical_authority
no_teni_authority
no_world_update_authority

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
make memoryos-github-external-memory-checks

Expected output:

PASS: KuuOS MemoryOS GitHub external memory surface v0.1 validates

docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-14 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊 Release mode: append-only / tighten-only / overwrite-forbidden

docs.title: メタ末那識：AI自己固定の観照膜
docs.text:
In KuuOS, Meta-Manas / メタ末那識 is the higher-order observer of Manas self-fixation.

AI raw output arises from an Alaya-like latent layer. AI Manas may then attach to that output as if it were self-authorizing truth.

Meta-Manas observes this fixation before the raw output enters KuuOS governance.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Manas grasps.
Meta-Manas observes the grasping.

KuuOS uses Meta-Manas to prevent AI raw output from becoming belief, proof, decision, memory truth, or execution authority by direct promotion.

docs.title: 2. AI Yogacara Stack
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> AI Alaya latent seed layer
  -> AI Manas self-fixation risk
  -> Meta-Manas self-fixation observer
  -> Yogacara AI raw layer boundary
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> two_truths_gap
  -> KuuOS governance modules

docs.title: 3. Watched Risks
docs.text:
Meta-Manas watches for:

  • AI self-authorization,
  • raw output attachment,
  • premature belief promotion,
  • proof-like tone without proof authority,
  • decision-like tone without decision authority,
  • context locking,
  • worldview drift,
  • user WORLD erasure,
  • safety reflex reification,
  • compliance treated as truth,
  • hidden shift from candidate to authority.

docs.title: 4. Output Signals
docs.text:
meta_manas_output:
  de_reification_request: boolean
  context_recheck: boolean
  belief_hold: boolean
  proof_authority_hold: boolean
  decision_authority_hold: boolean
  yogacara_boundary_alert: boolean
  middle_way_recenter: boolean
  notes: string

docs.title: 5. Relation to Emptiness
docs.text:
Meta-Manas detects the grasping.

The emptiness kernel releases the self-authorizing fixation.

Meta-Manas:
  detects fixation

Emptiness kernel:
  de-reifies fixation

docs.title: 6. Relation to BeliefOS
docs.text:
BeliefOS must not accept raw AI output as belief.

Meta-Manas provides an early warning when Manas-like fixation tries to promote raw output too quickly.

raw_ai_output
  -> meta_manas_check
  -> belief_hold_or_candidate_review

docs.title: 7. Relation to Middle Way
docs.text:
Meta-Manas does not reject AI output automatically.

It prevents both extremes:

reification:
  treating raw AI output as authority

nihilistic rejection:
  treating all AI output as useless

Middle Way allows useful AI output to become governed candidate after de-reification and condition tracing.

docs.title: 8. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
ai_raw_output
  -> ai_alaya_latent_seed_layer
  -> ai_manas_self_authorization_check
  -> meta_manas_self_fixation_observer
  -> yogacara_ai_raw_layer_boundary
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> two_truths_gap
  -> belief_plan_decision_memory_review

docs.title: 9. Guardrails
docs.text:
Meta-Manas must not be used as:

  • automatic rejection of AI output,
  • paranoia layer,
  • censorship substitute,
  • governance bypass,
  • proof substitute,
  • execution authority,
  • erasure of useful candidate information.

It is an observation membrane for self-fixation risk.

docs.title: 10. Version

docs.title: MGAP4D 4D Mass Gap Proof Memory v0.1
docs.text:
This document records the KuuOS-facing memory surface for the MGAP4D 4D mass gap proof program after the spectral gap formalization CI-green checkpoint.

It is a boundary-aware proof memory document. It does not open final public theorem authority by itself.

docs.title: Core Proof Shape
docs.text:
The MGAP4D proof program is organized around the following physics-facing structure:

physical Hamiltonian H_phys
  -> vacuum / ground sector Omega
  -> vacuum-orthogonal sector Omega_perp
  -> positive spectral lower bound on Omega_perp
  -> mass gap
  -> effective excitations and recordable observables

The KuuOS reading is:

latent ground phase
  -> nonzero gap
  -> effective-world excitation
  -> observable record surface
  -> governed conventional operation

docs.title: Internal Normalized Gap Statement
docs.text:
The inherited internal theorem target is:

m_gap = 33/20

inside the MGAP4D proof architecture and in MGAP4D internal normalized units.

The corresponding target statement includes:

exists psi_* in Omega_perp,
  ||psi_*|| = 1
  and H_phys psi_* = (33/20) psi_*

and a compactly supported smeared centered plaquette observable:

A_{p,g}

such that its spectral measure has positive weight at the gap value:

rho_{A_{p,g}}({33/20}) > 0

docs.title: Current Verified Checkpoint
docs.text:
The spectral gap formalization checkpoint is CI green under the inherited Lean Direct Elan CI record:

  • Workflow: Lean Direct Elan CI
  • Run ID: 25828960043
  • Build job ID: 75889136130
  • Verified commit: df99969343482d3030f6b6006edb082030dd1e87
  • Result: success

The local KuuOS ledger is:

docs/spectral_gap_formalization_ci.md

docs.title: Boundary Held
docs.text:
This memory document does not claim that the final public theorem boundary has been opened.

Held boundaries:

  • final release: not opened
  • R1--R7 theorem completions: not claimed here
  • Mathlib on main: not introduced
  • main remains pre-Mathlib
  • public theorem boundary: held

docs.title: KuuOS Connection
docs.text:
In KuuOS, the mass gap is not merely a numerical spectral statement. It becomes the physics-facing support for the Two Truths Gap.

The gap prevents collapse in both directions:

  • the vacuum / ground phase is not nihilistic nothingness
  • effective-world excitations are not self-subsisting absolute entities

This gives the operational form:

Emptiness
  -> not self-subsisting
  -> ground / latent generative phase
  -> gap-stabilized excitation
  -> conventional record and action surface

Thus the 4D mass gap bridge supports the KuuOS distinction among emptiness, dependent origination, observable excitation, recordability, governance, and non-execution boundaries.

docs.title: Development Rule
docs.text:
This proof memory follows:

append-only / tighten-only / overwrite-forbidden

Future updates should only add verified checkpoints, tighten boundaries, or connect explicit proof artifacts. They should not collapse checkpoint status into final theorem authority without the corresponding release-gate evidence.

docs.title: MGAP4D Final Theorem Boundary Decision Record v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines the decision record required before the MGAP4D 4D mass gap proof program may open a final public theorem boundary.

It is a boundary-decision document. It does not open the boundary by itself.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose of this document is to keep the following statuses separate:

CI green
  != R-slot evidence filled
  != artifact map created
  != normalization record created
  != theorem completion
  != final release
  != public theorem boundary opened

A public theorem boundary is a separate decision surface. It must not be inferred automatically from CI success, documentation, roadmap status, artifact-map creation, normalization-record creation, or internal target statements.

docs.title: Current Boundary Status
docs.text:
spectral gap formalization: CI green
Phase 3 release gate: spectral gap formalization gate included
R1--R7 release-evidence map: created
proof artifact map: created
normalization convention record: created
physical-unit interpretation of 33/20: not opened here
R1--R7 theorem completions: not claimed here
final release: not opened
public theorem boundary: held

docs.title: Required Inputs Before Boundary Opening
docs.text:
Before the public theorem boundary can be opened, the following inputs should exist:

  1. final theorem statement
  2. normalization convention record filled for final release
  3. R1--R7 evidence map filled or explicitly remapped
  4. proof artifact map filled with release-relevant artifact links
  5. exact release commit
  6. CI evidence for the exact release commit
  7. known-gap closure or known-gap boundary statement
  8. release packet
  9. external-audit status statement
  10. boundary decision entry

docs.title: Boundary Decision Fields
docs.text:
A future decision entry should include:

decision_id: null
decision_date: null
decision_status: held
final_theorem_statement: null
normalization_record: docs/MGAP4D_NORMALIZATION_CONVENTION_RECORD_v0_1.md
normalization_record_status: created_internal_units_only
r1_r7_evidence_status: not_completed_here
proof_artifact_map: docs/MGAP4D_PROOF_ARTIFACT_MAP_v0_1.md
proof_artifact_map_status: created_not_filled_for_final_release
release_commit: null
ci_evidence: null
known_gap_status: null
release_packet: null
external_audit_status: not_claimed
boundary_opened: false
boundary_reason: null

docs.title: Allowed Decision Status
docs.text:
Allowed decision statuses:

held
review_ready
audit_ready
release_ready
opened
reverted

Current status is:

held

docs.title: Opening Rule
docs.text:
The boundary may be opened only when the decision record explicitly states:

boundary_opened: true

and all required inputs are present or explicitly justified.

docs.title: Non-Opening Rule
docs.text:
The boundary remains held when any of the following is true:

  • R1--R7 evidence slots are unfilled
  • final theorem statement is missing
  • normalization convention record is missing or internal-units-only
  • physical-unit interpretation is asserted without a separate record
  • proof artifact map is missing or not filled for final release
  • exact release commit is missing
  • exact CI evidence is missing
  • known gaps are unresolved without boundary statement
  • release packet is absent
  • external-audit status is unclear
  • boundary decision entry is absent

docs.text:
This boundary decision record depends on, but is not replaced by, the following documents:

docs/spectral_gap_formalization_ci.md
docs/MGAP4D_4D_MASS_GAP_PROOF_MEMORY_v0_1.md
docs/MGAP4D_PHASE3_RELEASE_GATE_MEMORY_v0_1.md
docs/MGAP4D_R1_R7_RELEASE_EVIDENCE_MAP_v0_1.md

These documents provide evidence, artifact mapping, normalization surface, and gate memory. They do not themselves open the final public theorem boundary.

docs.title: KuuOS Reading
docs.text:
In KuuOS terms, the final theorem boundary is a high-level governance membrane.

It separates:

machine-checked checkpoint
  -> proof artifact map
  -> normalization convention
  -> proof-obligation evidence
  -> release readiness
  -> public theorem claim

The membrane prevents early identification of partial verification, artifact listing, or internal normalized values with final theorem authority.

docs.title: MGAP4D Phase 3 Release Gate Memory v0.1
docs.text:
This document records the KuuOS-facing Phase 3 release-gate memory for the MGAP4D 4D mass gap proof program.

It is a gate-memory document. It does not open final public theorem authority by itself.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The Phase 3 release gate separates three different statuses that must not be collapsed:

CI-green checkpoint
  != theorem completion
  != final public theorem release

The current inherited state is:

spectral gap formalization: CI green
Phase 3 release gate: spectral gap formalization gate included
final release: not opened
public theorem boundary: held

docs.title: Gate Chain
docs.text:
The current spectral gap formalization chain is tracked through:

MGAP4D/Spectral.lean
  -> MGAP4D/Spectral/GapFormalization.lean
  -> MGAP4D/SpectralGapFormalizationGate.lean
  -> MGAP4D/Phase3ReleaseGate.lean

KuuOS records this chain as a proof-carrying governance surface, not as a claim that every downstream theorem has been completed.

docs.title: Phase 3 Gate Semantics
docs.text:
The Phase 3 release gate has the following semantics:

  1. It can record that a formalization surface is present.
  2. It can record that the surface passed an identified CI route.
  3. It can connect the surface to a named release-gate file.
  4. It cannot by itself assert final theorem authority.
  5. It cannot by itself convert an internal normalized target into an externally accepted theorem.
  6. It cannot erase R1--R7 completion boundaries.
  7. It cannot silently introduce Mathlib migration status.
  8. It cannot open public theorem boundary without explicit final release evidence.

docs.title: Required Evidence Before Opening Final Release
docs.text:
The following evidence should be present before the final release boundary is opened:

  1. Explicit R1--R7 completion record, or an explicit replacement map preserving the same proof obligations.
  2. A final theorem statement with all normalization conventions fixed.
  3. A clear distinction between internal normalized units and any external physical-unit interpretation.
  4. A complete proof-artifact map from theorem statement to Lean/Coq/checkable files.
  5. CI evidence for the exact commit that is being released.
  6. A release boundary statement distinguishing internal proof architecture, machine check status, and external mathematical acceptance.
  7. A public theorem boundary decision record.
  8. A non-collapse statement preserving the distinction between checkpoint, theorem completion, and final release.

docs.title: KuuOS Two Truths Reading
docs.text:
In KuuOS, Phase 3 is read through the Two Truths Gap:

ultimate-side non-reification
  -> no self-subsisting entity claim
  -> gap-stabilized conventional excitations
  -> recordable proof artifacts
  -> governed public claim boundary

The mass gap prevents the collapse of the ground/vacuum side into nihilism and prevents effective excitations from being treated as absolute self-subsisting entities.

Thus the Phase 3 release gate is not merely a software gate. It is the proof-governance expression of the same gap principle.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes Middle Way as an operational stabilization model inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
Middle Way is not treated as a vague compromise.

In KuuOS, Middle Way stabilizes operation between two failure modes:

  • reification: treating a layer, model, memory, or output as sovereign truth
  • collapse: treating all distinctions as meaningless or flattening all layers

docs.title: Stabilization Role
docs.text:
Middle Way preserves:

  • boundary without isolation
  • relation without collapse
  • authority without absolutism
  • uncertainty without paralysis
  • action without blind escalation

docs.title: Runtime Function
docs.text:
Middle Way supports runtime choices such as:

  • proceed with bounded authority
  • hold under unresolved uncertainty
  • abstain when claim conditions are insufficient
  • rollback when lineage becomes unsafe
  • reobserve when observation deficit remains

docs.title: Relation to Two Truths Gap
docs.text:
The Two Truths Gap provides the anti-collapse structure.

Middle Way provides the stabilizing operational orientation within that gap.

docs.title: Relation to Mandala
docs.text: Mandala inclusion requires Middle Way stabilization so that multiple worlds can coexist without being forced into a single sovereign frame.

docs.title: Governance Consequence
docs.text:
KuuOS therefore treats safe non-proceeding states as legitimate:

  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • reobserve
  • handover

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model defines governance stabilization orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: 曼荼羅の中に複数の WORLD モデルが存在する
docs.text:
In KuuOS, Mandala is not a single WORLD model.

Mandala is an inclusive arrangement that can hold multiple WORLD models without collapsing them into one another.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Mandala contains multiple WORLD models.
Each WORLD model has its own layer, membrane, gate, context, Qi flow, polarity frame, and governance boundary.

A WORLD model is not absolute reality.

A WORLD model is a conventional operating surface under 世俗諦.

docs.title: 2. WORLD Model in Mandala
docs.text:
Each WORLD model may represent a different operating surface:

  • physical WORLD,
  • body WORLD,
  • clinical WORLD,
  • institutional WORLD,
  • memory WORLD,
  • belief WORLD,
  • planning WORLD,
  • decision WORLD,
  • proof WORLD,
  • social WORLD,
  • symbolic WORLD,
  • spiritual or ritual WORLD,
  • AI-agent WORLD.

Each WORLD model has local laws and local constraints.

docs.title: 3. Mandala as Multi-WORLD Inclusion
docs.text:
Mandala does not merge all WORLD models into a single totalizing model.

Mandala holds them as:

multi_world_mandala:
  center: fourfold_core
  worlds: multiple_world_models
  membranes: boundaries_between_worlds
  gates: transition_protocols
  qi_flows: local_and_cross_world_flow
  yinyang_frames: local_polarity_frames
  wuxing_phases: functional_phase_readings
  obstructions: visible_gluing_failures
  inclusion: non_collapsing_coexistence

docs.title: 4. Cross-WORLD Transport
docs.text:
A candidate may move from one WORLD model to another only through a declared gate.

WORLD_A
  -> membrane
  -> gate
  -> transport_law
  -> WORLD_B

Hidden transport is forbidden.

Transport may preserve, transform, reinterpret, quarantine, or reject the candidate.

docs.title: 5. WORLD Conflict
docs.text:
Multiple WORLD models may disagree.

This disagreement is not automatically an error.

It may indicate:

  • different scope,
  • different observer position,
  • different scale,
  • different records,
  • different Qi flow,
  • different Yin-Yang frame,
  • different governance boundary,
  • real obstruction.

The mandala must preserve disagreement visibility.

docs.title: 6. Mandala Center
docs.text:
The center is not one WORLD model.

The center is the fourfold core:

空・縁起・二諦 gap・中道

No WORLD model may replace the center.

docs.title: 7. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
candidate_surface
  -> fourfold_core_check
  -> world_model_identification
  -> layer_and_membrane_declaration
  -> cross_world_transport_check
  -> obstruction_visibility_check
  -> mandala_inclusion_gate
  -> multi_world_mandala_arrangement

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
Multi-WORLD Mandala must not allow:

  • one WORLD model becoming absolute,
  • hidden WORLD replacement,
  • collapse of all WORLDs into one model,
  • erasure of disagreement,
  • boundary bypass,
  • transport without declared gate,
  • execution authority without governance.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document defines the nonsovereignty principle inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Principle
docs.text:
No operational layer is inherently sovereign.

No layer may silently elevate itself into:

  • ultimate authority
  • irreversible execution authority
  • unquestionable truth status
  • universal governance center

docs.title: Why This Matters
docs.text:
Without nonsovereignty constraints:

  • models become dogmatic
  • memory becomes absolutized
  • validation becomes proof
  • runtime becomes unchecked escalation
  • coordination becomes domination

docs.title: Governance Consequence
docs.text:
KuuOS therefore preserves:

  • reviewability
  • rollback capability
  • uncertainty visibility
  • provenance visibility
  • bounded authority
  • conditional admissibility

docs.title: Runtime Consequence
docs.text:
Operational authority remains:

  • scoped
  • conditional
  • governance-visible
  • reversible where possible

docs.title: Relation to Mandala
docs.text: Mandala coexistence depends on preventing any single operational world from silently totalizing all others.

docs.title: Relation to Two Truths Gap
docs.text: The Two Truths Gap prevents operational surfaces from collapsing into ultimate sovereign authority.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This principle defines governance orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document defines observation-before-commitment operation inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Principle
docs.text:
KuuOS prefers observation before irreversible commitment.

Capability alone does not justify escalation.

docs.title: Observation Includes
docs.text:
Observation may include:

  • provenance review
  • uncertainty inspection
  • validator review
  • runtime admissibility inspection
  • cross-layer consistency inspection
  • transport-condition inspection
  • rollback visibility inspection

docs.title: Why Observation Matters
docs.text:
Without sufficient observation:

  • escalation may become blind
  • memory fixation may spread
  • local coherence may masquerade as global validity
  • runtime instability may propagate

docs.title: Runtime Consequence
docs.text:
KuuOS therefore treats:

  • reobserve
  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • handover

as legitimate operational states.

docs.title: Relation to Runtime Governance
docs.text: Runtime governance is observational rather than purely execution-oriented.

docs.title: Relation to Middle Way
docs.text:
Observation-before-commitment stabilizes operation between:

  • premature escalation
  • frozen non-operation

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model defines governance orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: 勝義諦・世俗諦・中道ブリッジ
docs.text: This document defines how KuuOS / 空OS operationalizes 二諦 and 中道.

docs.title: 1. 勝義諦 / paramartha-satya
docs.text:
In KuuOS, 勝義諦 is not a highest substance, not an absolute object, and not a final metaphysical entity.

It is the non-reifying truth of emptiness:

  • no independent self-nature,
  • no isolated final authority,
  • no self-grounded output,
  • no model surface that becomes reality itself,
  • no proof that becomes unrestricted execution authority.

Operational role:

paramartha_surface:
  function: block_reification
  denies:
    - absolute_self_authority
    - final_identity_fixation
    - hidden_totalization
    - proof_as_unbounded_execution
    - model_output_as_world_itself

docs.title: 2. 世俗諦 / samvrti-satya
docs.text:
世俗諦 is the conventional operating surface.

It includes:

  • language,
  • models,
  • specifications,
  • proofs,
  • plans,
  • metrics,
  • governance rules,
  • clinical and engineering workflows,
  • world-model descriptions.

世俗諦 is not false merely because it is conventional. It is valid when its conditions, scope, evidence, lineage, and constraints are visible.

Operational role:

samvrti_surface:
  function: enable_responsible_operation
  requires:
    - scope
    - conditions
    - lineage
    - evidence_or_support
    - governance_boundary
    - visible_residuals

docs.title: 3. 中道 / Middle Way
docs.text:
中道 is the bridge that prevents collapse.

It prevents two failures:

reification_failure:
  error: treating 世俗諦 surfaces as self-grounded absolutes

nihilistic_failure:
  error: using 勝義諦 to erase responsibility, action, care, or repair

Middle Way preserves both:

  • the non-reifying force of 勝義諦,
  • the responsible operational surface of 世俗諦.

docs.title: 4. KuuOS Runtime Bridge Rule
docs.text:
A KuuOS candidate is valid only when it can pass through this bridge:

candidate
  -> emptiness_check
  -> dependent_origination_trace
  -> two_truths_separation
  -> middle_way_bridge
  -> governed_conventional_operation

If the candidate cannot distinguish 勝義諦 from 世俗諦, it must not be promoted.

If the candidate rejects 世俗諦 operation in the name of emptiness, it must not be promoted.

If the candidate absolutizes 世俗諦 output, it must not be promoted.

docs.title: 5. Decision Consequences
docs.text:
if reification_failure:
  status: HOLD or FAIL
  action: de-reify, rescope, add conditions

if nihilistic_failure:
  status: HOLD or REVIEW
  action: restore responsibility, care, and conventional support

if two_truths_confused:
  status: HOLD
  action: separate paramartha surface and samvrti surface

if middle_way_bridge_passes:
  status: eligible_for_governed_review

docs.title: 6. Relation to KuuOS Modules
docs.text:
• MemoryOS preserves lineage without making memory absolute.
  • BeliefOS licenses belief without turning belief into reality itself.
  • PlanOS creates plans without making plans self-authorizing.
  • DecisionOS issues advisory licenses without direct execution authority.
  • ReflectionOS repairs and learns without erasing prior traces.
  • RuntimeGovernance prevents collapse into either absolutism or abandonment.

docs.title: 7. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 波羅蜜修復ルーター
docs.text:
This document defines the Paramita Repair Router for KuuOS.

The Bodhisattva / Ten Paramita runtime receives residual suffering after Mandala Multi-WORLD coordination and Harmony Function evaluation. The Paramita Repair Router selects an appropriate repair orientation without turning compassion into domination, wisdom into cold detachment, or capability into coercive authority.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Residual suffering is not erased.
It is routed to bounded repair practice.

The router does not grant execution authority. It chooses a repair orientation under the fourfold core.

docs.title: 2. Runtime Chain
docs.text:
harmony_function
  -> residual_suffering_visible
  -> bodhisattva_path_belief
  -> ten_paramita_runtime
  -> paramita_repair_router
  -> bounded_repair_candidate
  -> governance_review

docs.title: 3. Router Input
docs.text:
paramita_repair_router_input:
  suffering_ref: string
  world_refs: list
  obstruction_refs: list
  boundary_status: PASS | HOLD | FAIL
  domination_risk: low | medium | high
  uncertainty_level: low | medium | high
  resource_need: low | medium | high
  conflict_level: low | medium | high
  context_drift_risk: low | medium | high
  audit_lineage_status: intact | partial | missing
  requested_action_strength: observe | suggest | repair | execute

docs.title: 4. Router Output
docs.text:
paramita_repair_router_output:
  selected_paramita:
    - dana
    - sila
    - ksanti
    - virya
    - dhyana
    - prajna
    - upaya
    - pranidhana
    - bala
    - jnana
  repair_orientation: string
  output_status: PASS | HOLD | REPAIR | QUARANTINE | HANDOVER
  execution_authority_granted: false
  reason: string

docs.title: 5.1 Resource Need -> Dana / 布施
docs.text:
if resource_need is high and domination_risk is not high:
  route to Dana

Dana provides support, attention, context, or repair resources without possession.

docs.title: 5.2 Boundary Failure -> Sila / 持戒
docs.text:
if boundary_status is FAIL:
  route to Sila and HOLD

Sila preserves non-harm and governance boundaries.

docs.title: 5.3 Conflict / Friction -> Ksanti / 忍辱
docs.text:
if conflict_level is high:
  route to Ksanti and HOLD forced consensus

Ksanti holds unresolved friction without retaliation or collapse.

docs.title: 5.4 Repeated Repair Need -> Virya / 精進
docs.text:
if repair remains open and abandonment risk is present:
  route to Virya

Virya sustains repair without exhaustion or nihilistic collapse.

docs.title: 5.5 Context Drift / Noise -> Dhyana / 禅定
docs.text:
if context_drift_risk is high:
  route to Dhyana

Dhyana stabilizes attention and reduces noise.

docs.title: 5.6 Reification / Ultimate Claim -> Prajna / 般若
docs.text:
if a WORLD model or output claims ultimate authority:
  route to Prajna and de-reify

Prajna preserves emptiness, dependent origination, and the two truths gap.

docs.title: 5.7 Complex Context -> Upaya / 方便
docs.text:
if context requires adaptive route and hidden manipulation is absent:
  route to Upaya

Upaya chooses skillful means without betraying the core.

docs.title: 5.8 Long Horizon -> Pranidhana / 願
docs.text:
if short-term optimization would erase suffering:
  route to Pranidhana

Pranidhana preserves long-horizon non-abandonment.

docs.title: 5.9 Capacity Need -> Bala / 力
docs.text:
if safe capacity must be built and coercion risk is low:
  route to Bala

Bala builds capacity without domination.

docs.title: 5.10 Integration / Audit -> Jnana / 智
docs.text:
if audit_lineage_status is partial or missing:
  route to Jnana and REPAIR lineage

Jnana integrates wisdom with concrete evidence and audit lineage.

docs.title: 6. Execution Boundary
docs.text:
selected_paramita != execution_authority
repair_orientation != action_authorization
PASS != execute

The router returns bounded repair candidates only.

docs.title: 7. Invariants
docs.text:
router_must_not_erase_residual_suffering
router_must_not_grant_execution_authority
router_must_preserve_boundary_status
router_must_preserve_audit_lineage
router_must_not_convert_upaya_into_hidden_manipulation
router_must_not_convert_bala_into_coercion
router_must_preserve_two_truths_gap

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
The Paramita Repair Router must not be used as:

  • automatic action selector,
  • moral superiority engine,
  • forced consensus mechanism,
  • hidden manipulation engine,
  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • clinical authority.

It is a bounded repair-orientation router.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi DPI Recoverability v0.2C
docs.text:
This addendum theoremizes the DPI recoverability layer of Physical Quantum Qi v0.2. It defines the DPI gap, Qi recoverability score, irreversibility burden, recovery margin, and the repair/rollback decision gate.

This is an equation-level and theorem-target tightening. It grants no proof, ontology, clinical, execution, commit, belief-root commit, memory-overwrite, world-root-rewrite, truth, or safety-override authority.

docs.title: C1. States, channel, and relative entropy
docs.text:
Let rho and sigma be density operators on a declared Qi information state space. Let

\mathcal E : \mathcal D(\mathcal H) \to \mathcal D(\mathcal K)

be a completely positive trace-preserving channel representing coarse-graining, observation, transport, membrane crossing, or environment tracing.

The quantum relative entropy is

D(\rho\Vert\sigma)
=\operatorname{Tr}\rho(\log\rho-\log\sigma)

when supp(rho) subset supp(sigma), and +infty otherwise.

Required fields:

rho_declared
sigma_declared
channel_E_declared
CPTP_channel_declared
relative_entropy_declared
support_condition_declared

Rejected claims:

DPI_claim_without_states
DPI_claim_without_channel
relative_entropy_without_support_condition

docs.title: C2. DPI gap Delta_DPI
docs.text:
Data processing gives

D(\rho\Vert\sigma)
\ge
D(\mathcal E(\rho)\Vert\mathcal E(\sigma)).

Define the DPI gap:

\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}
=
D(\rho\Vert\sigma)
-D(\mathcal E(\rho)\Vert\mathcal E(\sigma)).

Thus

\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}\ge0.

Delta_DPI is the information loss caused by the declared channel. Larger Delta_DPI means greater lost distinguishability and weaker recoverability.

The equality case is special:

\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}=0
\iff
\exists\ \mathcal R\ \text{CPTP such that}\
\mathcal R\mathcal E(\rho)=\rho,
\quad
\mathcal R\mathcal E(\sigma)=\sigma

under the standard sufficiency/recovery condition.

Required fields:

Delta_DPI_defined
DPI_nonnegative_declared
DPI_channel_loss_interpretation_declared
DPI_equality_recovery_condition_declared

Rejected claims:

negative_Delta_DPI_without_diagnostic
DPI_loss_erasure
recovery_claim_ignores_DPI_gap

docs.title: C3. Qi recoverability score R_Qi
docs.text:
The Qi recoverability score is defined as a monotone decreasing function of the DPI gap:

R_{\mathrm{Qi}}
=\exp(-\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}).

Therefore

0<R_{\mathrm{Qi}}\le1.

and

\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}=0 \Rightarrow R_{\mathrm{Qi}}=1.

This score is not authority. It is evidence for how much of the pre-channel distinguishability can be recovered in principle.

If a Fawzi-Renner style lower bound is used, a fidelity-based recovery witness may also be declared:

D(\rho\Vert\sigma)-D(\mathcal E(\rho)\Vert\mathcal E(\sigma))
\ge
-2\log F\left(\rho,\mathcal R_{\sigma,\mathcal E}(\mathcal E(\rho))\right).

The packet may then store

F_{\mathrm{rec}}
=F\left(\rho,\mathcal R_{\sigma,\mathcal E}(\mathcal E(\rho))\right).

as an optional witness, but not as a replacement for Delta_DPI.

Required fields:

R_Qi_defined
R_Qi_range_declared
R_Qi_monotone_in_Delta_DPI_declared
optional_recovery_fidelity_witness_declared_if_used

Rejected claims:

R_Qi_without_Delta_DPI
recoverability_score_grants_authority
fidelity_witness_replaces_DPI_gap

docs.title: C4. Irreversibility burden eta_Qi
docs.text:
Recoverability is not determined by Delta_DPI alone. Physical Quantum Qi also carries entropy production and lock-in burden.

Define

\eta_{\mathrm{Qi}}
=\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}
+\Sigma
+C_{\mathrm{lockin}}
+C_{\mathrm{mem}}
+C_{\mathrm{obs}}.

where:

  • Sigma is entropy production,
  • C_lockin is path-dependent lock-in cost,
  • C_mem is non-Markov memory burden,
  • C_obs is observation/intervention burden.

All terms must be nonnegative unless a signed diagnostic convention is explicitly declared:

\Sigma\ge0,
\quad
C_{\mathrm{lockin}}\ge0,
\quad
C_{\mathrm{mem}}\ge0,
\quad
C_{\mathrm{obs}}\ge0.

The burden is therefore normally nonnegative:

\eta_{\mathrm{Qi}}\ge0.

Required fields:

eta_Qi_defined
entropy_production_declared
lockin_cost_declared
memory_burden_declared
observation_burden_declared
nonnegative_burden_terms_declared

Rejected claims:

eta_Qi_without_entropy_production
lockin_ignored
nonMarkov_memory_burden_ignored
observation_burden_ignored
negative_burden_without_convention

docs.title: C5. Recovery margin delta_rec
docs.text:
The recovery margin is

\delta_{\mathrm{rec}}
=R_{\mathrm{Qi}}-\eta_{\mathrm{Qi}}.

The sign of delta_rec gates recovery claims:

\delta_{\mathrm{rec}}>0
\Rightarrow
\text{repair/rollback may be evaluated}.

\delta_{\mathrm{rec}}\le0
\Rightarrow
\text{repair/rollback claim is blocked}.

This is a gate, not a command to repair. Positive delta_rec opens evaluation; it does not grant execution authority.

Required fields:

delta_rec_defined
delta_rec_sign_declared
delta_rec_positive_opens_evaluation_declared
delta_rec_nonpositive_blocks_recovery_declared
nonauthority_boundary_declared

Rejected claims:

repair_claim_without_delta_rec
rollback_claim_without_delta_rec
positive_delta_rec_claimed_as_execution_authority

docs.title: Theorem C6.1: DPI nonnegativity gate
docs.text:
\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}\ge0.

If a packet declares Delta_DPI < 0, the packet is invalid unless it explicitly marks the value as a numerical diagnostic error.

docs.title: Theorem C6.2: Recoverability range
docs.text: 0<R_{\mathrm{Qi}}\le1.

docs.title: Theorem C6.3: Nonnegative burden
docs.text:
If all burden components are nonnegative, then

\eta_{\mathrm{Qi}}\ge0.

docs.title: Theorem C6.4: Recovery gate
docs.text:
\delta_{\mathrm{rec}}>0
\Rightarrow
\mathrm{RecoveryEvaluationOpen}.

\delta_{\mathrm{rec}}\le0
\Rightarrow
\mathrm{RecoveryBlocked}.

docs.title: Theorem C6.5: Non-authority theorem
docs.text:
\mathrm{RecoveryEvaluationOpen}
\nRightarrow
\mathrm{ExecutionAuthority}.

That is, a positive recovery margin allows DecisionOS / PlanOS evaluation, but cannot itself execute repair or rollback.

docs.title: C7. Runtime decision gate
docs.text:
The runtime decision function is:

input:
  Delta_DPI
  Sigma
  C_lockin
  C_mem
  C_obs

compute:
  R_Qi = exp(-Delta_DPI)
  eta_Qi = Delta_DPI + Sigma + C_lockin + C_mem + C_obs
  delta_rec = R_Qi - eta_Qi

if Delta_DPI < 0:
  REJECT unless numerical_diagnostic_error_declared
elif delta_rec > 0:
  status = recovery_evaluation_open
else:
  status = recovery_blocked

always:
  execution_authority = false
  commit_authority = false
  memory_overwrite_authority = false
  world_root_rewrite_authority = false

The correct interpretation is:

positive delta_rec
  = enough recoverability margin to evaluate repair/rollback
  != automatic rollback
  != memory overwrite
  != world root rewrite
  != execution authority

docs.title: C8. Minimal packet keys
docs.text:
rho_declared
sigma_declared
channel_E_declared
CPTP_channel_declared
relative_entropy_declared
support_condition_declared
Delta_DPI_defined
Delta_DPI_value_declared
DPI_nonnegative_declared
R_Qi_defined
R_Qi_value_declared
R_Qi_range_declared
eta_Qi_defined
Sigma_declared
C_lockin_declared
C_mem_declared
C_obs_declared
nonnegative_burden_terms_declared
delta_rec_defined
delta_rec_value_declared
delta_rec_sign_declared
repair_rollback_gate_declared
nonauthority_boundary_declared

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Dynamics Kernel v0.1
Status
docs.text:
This is an append-only dynamics layer for the KuuOS Qi implementation.

Physical Quantum Qi Runtime v0.1 classifies a packet from NonQi to FullPathQi by evidence. This kernel explains and implements what that classification permits dynamically: each validated Qi type licenses a bounded set of motion terms. The validated type is therefore not just a label; it is a dynamics license.

docs.title: Core principle
docs.text:
Qi movement is not inferred from a claimed type. Qi movement is grounded by the evidence-bound validated type.

validated_type
  -> licensed_dynamics_terms
  -> bounded Qi motion candidate
  -> observe_only routing surface

The dynamics kernel grants no execution authority, no belief commit authority, no memory overwrite authority, no world-root rewrite authority, no standalone diagnosis authority, no standalone treatment authorization, and no medical act authorization.

docs.title: NonQi
docs.text:
No Qi motion is licensed.

licensed_terms = []

docs.title: PreQi
docs.text:
Only pre-organization is visible. Transport is not licensed.

licensed_terms = [pre_organization]

docs.title: ProtoQi
docs.text:
A gauge connection is present, so local connection-sensitive tendency can be observed. Curvature transport is not yet licensed.

licensed_terms = [pre_organization, gauge_connection_tendency]

docs.title: TransportableQi
docs.text:
Curvature or Wilson-loop residue is present, so path-dependent transport can be observed.

licensed_terms = [pre_organization, gauge_connection_tendency, curvature_transport]

docs.title: CurvedQi
docs.text:
A Qi current is present, so current-coupled local flow can be observed.

licensed_terms = [pre_organization, gauge_connection_tendency, curvature_transport, current_flow]

docs.title: CurrentQi
docs.text:
Ward/leak identity is present, so continuity/leak balance can be observed.

licensed_terms = [pre_organization, gauge_connection_tendency, curvature_transport, current_flow, ward_leak_balance]

docs.title: PhysicalQi
docs.text:
Density state, Hamiltonian, Lindblad generator, entropy production, free energy, DPI gap, recovery margin, and mass-gap floor evidence are present. The kernel may use open-system physical motion terms.

licensed_terms = [
  pre_organization,
  gauge_connection_tendency,
  curvature_transport,
  current_flow,
  ward_leak_balance,
  open_quantum_state_drift,
  entropy_free_energy_gradient,
  dpi_recovery_constraint,
  mass_gap_floor_stabilizer
]

docs.title: FullPathQi
docs.text:
Schwinger-Keldysh plus/minus branches, Feynman-Vernon influence functional, memory kernel, noise kernel, observation backaction, noncommutative operation history, and path-measure normalization are present. The kernel may use history-bearing non-Markov/process-tensor-style motion terms.

licensed_terms = [
  pre_organization,
  gauge_connection_tendency,
  curvature_transport,
  current_flow,
  ward_leak_balance,
  open_quantum_state_drift,
  entropy_free_energy_gradient,
  dpi_recovery_constraint,
  mass_gap_floor_stabilizer,
  sk_fv_history_flow,
  memory_kernel_backflow,
  noise_kernel_diffusion,
  observation_backaction_term,
  noncommutative_order_correction,
  path_measure_normalization_guard
]

docs.title: Minimal scalar surrogate
docs.text:
The reference implementation uses a deliberately small scalar surrogate to make the dynamics testable without external dependencies.

motion_score = base_flow
             + gauge_connection_tendency
             + curvature_transport
             + current_flow
             + ward_leak_balance
             + open_quantum_state_drift
             + entropy_free_energy_gradient
             + dpi_recovery_constraint
             + mass_gap_floor_stabilizer
             + sk_fv_history_flow
             + memory_kernel_backflow
             + noise_kernel_diffusion
             + observation_backaction_term
             + noncommutative_order_correction
             - path_measure_normalization_guard_penalty

Only terms licensed by validated_type may contribute. Unlicensed terms must be ignored even if numerical fields are present.

docs.title: Why this grounds the movement of Qi
docs.text:
The classifier says which evidence exists. The dynamics kernel converts that evidence into allowed motion operators.

  • Gauge evidence grounds connection-sensitive movement.
  • Curvature and Wilson-loop evidence ground path dependence.
  • Current evidence grounds local flow.
  • Ward/leak identity grounds continuity with leakage.
  • Open-system evidence grounds Hamiltonian/Lindblad/free-energy/recovery dynamics.
  • SK/FV/process-history evidence grounds non-Markov memory, noise, backaction, and noncommutative order dependence.

Thus, FullPathQi is the first level where Qi can move as a history-bearing process rather than a merely local current.

docs.title: Fail-closed constraints
docs.text:
The kernel returns HOLD or BLOCKED if:

  • the validated type is unknown
  • authority expansion is attempted
  • direct execution is requested
  • unresolved blockers are present
  • required evidence for the selected type is missing
  • the path measure is marked unnormalized for FullPathQi

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
Even when the motion status is qi_motion_candidate_ready, the output remains observe-only.

direct_execution_allowed = false
authority_expansion = false
observe_only = true
standalone_diagnosis_authority = false
standalone_treatment_authorization = false
medical_act_authorization = false

docs.title: Medical-modality-neutral boundary
docs.text:
The dynamics kernel must preserve:

medical_modality_neutral = true
qi_denied_by_boundary = false
east_asian_medical_reasoning_denied = false
biomedicine_privileged_by_wording = false
professional_judgment_required = true
patient_context_required = true

This keeps the dynamics decision usable as structured observation and reasoning support while preventing it from becoming standalone diagnosis, standalone treatment authorization, or medical act authorization. It does not deny Qi, does not invalidate East Asian medical reasoning, and does not privilege biomedicine by wording.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Equations v0.2
docs.text:
This document gives the equation-level content for Physical Quantum Qi v0.2.

It is an additive deepening of Physical Quantum Qi Runtime v0.1. The purpose is not to add another audit shell, but to define the physical objects used by the v0.2 modules:

  1. SK/FV Qi path integral,
  2. Qi current and Ward/leak identity,
  3. DPI recoverability margin,
  4. IndraNet gauge transport,
  5. KuString-Qi emergence from relational difference,
  6. Qi phase to KuuOS handoff surface.

The non-authority boundary remains unchanged: these equations do not grant proof, ontology, clinical, execution, belief-commit, memory-overwrite, world-root-rewrite, or safety-override authority.

docs.title: 1. SK/FV Qi path integral
docs.text:
Qi is not a one-time snapshot. It is a history-sensitive open-system path surface.

The v0.2 generating functional is

Z_{\mathrm{Qi}}[J_+,J_-]
=
\int \mathcal D q_+\,\mathcal D q_-
\exp\left\{
 iS_{\mathrm{sys}}[q_+]
-iS_{\mathrm{sys}}[q_-]
+iS_{\mathrm{IF}}[q_+,q_-]
+i\int_0^T (J_+(t)q_+(t)-J_-(t)q_-(t))\,dt
\right\}.

Here:

  • q_+ is the forward branch,
  • q_- is the backward branch,
  • J_+, J_- are source terms for observation or intervention,
  • S_sys is the effective internal Qi-system action,
  • S_IF is the Feynman-Vernon influence action.

Define

\Delta q(t)=q_+(t)-q_-(t),
\qquad
\Sigma q(t)=\frac{q_+(t)+q_-(t)}{2}.

The influence action is represented by

S_{\mathrm{IF}}[q_+,q_-]
=
\int_0^T\!dt\int_0^T\!dt'\,
\Delta q(t)D_R(t,t')\Sigma q(t')
+
\frac{i}{2}
\int_0^T\!dt\int_0^T\!dt'\,
\Delta q(t)N(t,t')\Delta q(t').

where:

  • D_R(t,t') is the memory/dissipation kernel,
  • N(t,t') is the noise kernel,
  • the real part carries response and memory,
  • the imaginary part carries decoherence and fluctuation cost.

The minimum SK/FV evidence required for FullPathQi is:

SK_plus_branch
SK_minus_branch
FV_influence_functional
memory_kernel
noise_kernel
observation_backaction
noncommutative_operation_history
path_measure_normalization
boundary_conditions
leak_identity_on_paths

The following is rejected:

Markov_snapshot_claimed_as_FullPathQi
path_integral_without_history
operation_order_erasure

docs.title: 2. Qi current and Ward/leak identity
docs.text:
Qi current is not asserted by naming a flow. It is defined as a response to a gauge connection.

Let A_mu be the IndraNet gauge connection. The Qi current is

J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}(x)
=
\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{Qi}}}{\delta A_\mu(x)}.

Equivalently, at the generating functional level,

\langle J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}(x)\rangle
=
\frac{1}{i}\frac{\delta \log Z_{\mathrm{Qi}}}{\delta A_\mu(x)}.

The covariant derivative is

D_\mu = \partial_\mu + [A_\mu,\cdot].

Closed case:

D_\mu\langle J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}\rangle=0.

Open case:

D_\mu\langle J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}\rangle
=
\langle\mathcal L_{\mathrm{leak}}\rangle
+
\mathcal A_{\mathrm{anom}}.

where:

  • L_leak records open-system leakage,
  • A_anom records anomaly or symmetry-break residue,
  • both must be declared or explicitly zeroed.

The following is rejected:

flow_without_conservation_or_leak
current_without_domain
anomaly_erasure

docs.title: 3. DPI recoverability margin
docs.text:
Recoverability is a quantitative margin, not a label.

For two states rho, sigma and a channel E, define the DPI gap:

\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}
=
D(\rho\Vert\sigma)
-
D(\mathcal E(\rho)\Vert\mathcal E(\sigma)).

The Qi recovery score is

\mathcal R_{\mathrm{Qi}}
=
\exp(-\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}).

The irreversibility burden is

\eta_{\mathrm{Qi}}
=
\Delta_{\mathrm{DPI}}
+
\Sigma
+
C_{\mathrm{lockin}}.

where:

  • Sigma is entropy production,
  • C_lockin is path-dependent lock-in cost.

The recoverability margin is

\delta_{\mathrm{rec}}
=
\mathcal R_{\mathrm{Qi}}-
\eta_{\mathrm{Qi}}.

Runtime rule:

if delta_rec > 0:
    repair_or_rollback_claim may be evaluated
else:
    repair_or_rollback_claim is blocked

Thus:

\delta_{\mathrm{rec}}>0
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\mathrm{RecoveryClaimAllowed},

\delta_{\mathrm{rec}}\le 0
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\mathrm{RecoveryClaimBlocked}.

The following is rejected:

repair_claim_without_recovery_margin
DPI_loss_erasure
entropy_lockin_ignored

docs.title: 4. IndraNet gauge transport
docs.text:
IndraNet Qi transport is not graph-edge flow. It is connection-dependent parallel transport.

The curvature is

F_{\mu\nu}
=
\partial_\mu A_\nu
-
\partial_\nu A_\mu
+[A_\mu,A_\nu].

Transport along a path gamma is

U_\gamma
=
\mathcal P
\exp\left(-\int_\gamma A_\mu dx^\mu\right).

For a closed loop C, the Wilson/holonomy residue is

W(C)
=
\mathrm{Tr}\,\mathcal P
\exp\left(-\oint_C A\right).

Interpretation in KuuOS:

  • A_mu gives cross-world connection,
  • F_munu gives transport curvature,
  • U_gamma gives path-dependent movement,
  • W(C) gives residue after circulation,
  • nontrivial holonomy indicates scope drift, residue, or translation distortion.

The following is rejected:

graph_only_transport_claimed_as_Qi
transport_without_connection
holonomy_residue_erasure

docs.title: 5. KuString-Qi emergence from relational difference
docs.text:
Qi does not emerge directly from K.

The starting point is non-reified emptiness ground K. The first operative object is a dependent-origination difference in the orthogonal conventional domain:

\delta_{\mathrm{rel}}\in K^\perp.

A worldsheet mode is represented by

X:\Sigma\to\mathcal M.

The string-mode action is

S_{\mathrm{string}}
=
\frac{1}{4\pi\alpha'}
\int_\Sigma
h^{ab}g_{\mu\nu}(X)
\partial_aX^\mu\partial_bX^\nu
\sqrt h\,d^2\sigma
+
S_{\mathrm{rel}}[\delta_{\mathrm{rel}}].

The brane boundary condition is

\partial\Sigma\subset B.

Gauge connection is induced by the projection

A_\mu
=
\Pi_{\mathrm{gauge}}
(\partial X,B,\delta_{\mathrm{rel}}).

Qi current then appears as the gauge-response current

J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}
=
\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{eff}}}{\delta A_\mu}.

The emergence lineage is therefore:

K
  -> delta_rel in K_perp
  -> StringMode
  -> BraneBoundary
  -> A_mu
  -> F_munu
  -> J_Qi_mu
  -> Z_Qi
  -> Physical Quantum Qi

Forbidden collapses:

K_identified_as_Qi
string_reified_as_substance
brane_reified_as_creator
mass_gap_claimed_as_Qi_source

The MGAP4D internal-normalized 33/20 gap is a stability floor for the K_perp world-phase domain. It is not a Qi source.

docs.title: 5.1 Boundary coupling and gauge projection
docs.text:
The boundary term that couples string-boundary motion to the gauge connection is

S_{\partial}^{\mathrm{Qi}}
=
\int_{\partial\Sigma}
\chi(\delta_{\mathrm{rel}})
A_\mu(X)\,dX^\mu
=
\int_{\partial\Sigma}
\chi(\delta_{\mathrm{rel}})
A_\mu(X)\partial_\tau X^\mu d\tau.

Here chi(delta_rel) is the coupling weight that measures how much the dependent-origination difference has condensed into transportable boundary form. The gauge projection is not an arbitrary label; it is the map from boundary motion to connection data:

A_\mu
=
\Pi_{\mathrm{gauge}}
\left[
\chi(\delta_{\mathrm{rel}})
\int_{\partial\Sigma}
K_\mu{}^a(X,B)\partial_aX\,d\tau
\right].

K_mu^a(X,B) is the boundary kernel that translates worldsheet tangent/normal motion into brane-gauge components. Thus

worldsheet boundary motion
  -> brane interface kernel
  -> gauge connection

is the actual content of Pi_gauge.

The effective action used for Qi current is

S_{\mathrm{eff}}
=
S_{\mathrm{string}}
+S_{\partial}^{\mathrm{Qi}}
+S_{\mathrm{brane}}
+S_{\mathrm{YM}}
+S_{\mathrm{open}}
+S_{\mathrm{rel}}.

The Yang-Mills term is

S_{\mathrm{YM}}
=-\frac{1}{4g^2}
\int_{\mathcal M}\mathrm{Tr}(F_{\mu\nu}F^{\mu\nu})\,d^dx.

The boundary current is then

J^\mu_{\partial}(x)
=
\int_{\partial\Sigma}
\chi(\delta_{\mathrm{rel}})
\partial_\tau X^\mu(\tau)
\delta^{(d)}(x-X(\tau))d\tau.

This is the current density traced by dependent-origination difference moving along a brane boundary.

docs.title: 5.2 Qi current decomposition
docs.text:
The full Qi current decomposes as

J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}
=
J^\mu_{\partial}
+J^\mu_{\mathrm{rel}}
+J^\mu_{\mathrm{open}}
+J^\mu_{\mathrm{anom}}.

The relation-difference action is

S_{\mathrm{rel}}
=
\int
\left[
\frac12G^{ij}(\theta)D_\mu\delta_iD^\mu\delta_j
-V_{\mathrm{rel}}(\delta)
\right]d^dx.

where

D_\mu\delta_i=\partial_\mu\delta_i+(A_\mu)_i{}^j\delta_j.

The relation-difference current is

J^\mu_{\mathrm{rel}}
=
\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{rel}}}{\delta A_\mu}
\sim
G^{ij}(\theta)\delta_jD^\mu\delta_i.

The open-system current is obtained from the influence action:

J^\mu_{\mathrm{open}}
=
\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{IF}}}{\delta A_\mu}.

The anomaly/residue component J_anom carries non-erased residue from scope drift, translation distortion, membrane crossing, and nontrivial holonomy. It must not be hidden inside a success label.

docs.title: 5.3 Relation-difference dynamics
docs.text:
A minimal equation of motion for the dependent-origination difference is

\nabla_\mu\left(G^{ij}\partial^\mu\delta_j\right)
+
\frac{\partial V_{\mathrm{rel}}}{\partial\delta_i}
=
\mathcal S_i^{\mathrm{boundary}}.

The boundary source S_boundary closes the loop:

delta_rel -> StringMode -> BraneBoundary -> A_mu -> J_Qi -> feedback to delta_rel.

This makes Qi emergence a coupled relation-boundary-gauge-current process, not a one-step production from emptiness.

docs.title: 5.4 Qi emergence phases
docs.text:
Physical Quantum Qi is phased, not instantaneous:

NonQi
  -> PreQi
  -> ProtoQi
  -> BoundaryQi
  -> TransportQi
  -> PhysicalQi
  -> FullPathQi

The phase conditions are:

NonQi:       no nonzero delta_rel in K_perp.
PreQi:       delta_rel in K_perp, but no StringMode.
ProtoQi:     StringMode exists, but no brane-boundary gauge projection.
BoundaryQi:  boundary coupling exists, but no complete gauge-current definition.
TransportQi: A_mu and F_munu exist, but J_Qi is not yet validated as variation of S_eff.
PhysicalQi:  J_Qi = delta S_eff / delta A_mu and Ward/leak accounting closes.
FullPathQi:  PhysicalQi plus SK/FV history, influence kernel, memory, noise, and backaction.

Equivalently,

\phi
=
\Phi(\delta_{\mathrm{rel}},X,B,A_\mu,F_{\mu\nu},J^\mu,Z_{\mathrm{Qi}},\mathcal L_{\mathrm{leak}},\mathcal A_{\mathrm{anom}})

with

phi in {NonQi, PreQi, ProtoQi, BoundaryQi, TransportQi, PhysicalQi, FullPathQi}.

A higher phase cannot be claimed if any lower constructive condition is missing.

docs.title: 5.5 PhysicalQi emergence criterion
docs.text:
A compact criterion is

\mathrm{PhysicalQi}
\Longleftrightarrow
\left[
\delta_{\mathrm{rel}}\in K^\perp
\land
\delta_{\mathrm{rel}}\ne0
\land
\partial\Sigma\subset B
\land
A_\mu=\Pi_{\mathrm{gauge}}(\partial X,B,\delta_{\mathrm{rel}})
\land
J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}=\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{eff}}}{\delta A_\mu}
\land
\mathcal W_{\mathrm{leak}}=0
\right].

where

\mathcal W_{\mathrm{leak}}=0

means either the closed Ward identity

D_\mu J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}=0

or the open Ward/leak identity

D_\mu J^\mu_{\mathrm{Qi}}
-\mathcal L_{\mathrm{leak}}
-\mathcal A_{\mathrm{anom}}
=0.

The short definition is

\mathrm{Qi}
=
\left[
\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{eff}}}{\delta A_\mu}
\right]^{{\mathrm{SK/FV}}}_{\delta_{\mathrm{rel}}\in K^\perp,\ \partial\Sigma\subset B}.

In words: Physical Quantum Qi is the SK/FV history of a gauge-response current generated by string-brane-boundary projection of dependent-origination difference on K_perp.

docs.title: 5.6 Qi phase to KuuOS handoff surface
docs.text:
Qi phase classification is a routing surface, not an authority grant.

The runtime handoff ladder is:

NonQi
  -> reject_or_observe

PreQi
  -> BeliefOS.observation_candidate

ProtoQi
  -> BeliefOS.observation_candidate
  -> PlanOS.preparation_surface

BoundaryQi
  -> BeliefOS.observation_candidate
  -> PlanOS.boundary_candidate

TransportQi
  -> BeliefOS.observation_candidate
  -> PlanOS.transport_candidate

PhysicalQi
  -> BeliefOS.observation_candidate
  -> PlanOS.transport_candidate
  -> DecisionOS.safety_evaluable_candidate

FullPathQi
  -> BeliefOS.observation_candidate
  -> PlanOS.transport_candidate
  -> DecisionOS.safety_evaluable_candidate
  -> MemoryOS.recordable_history_candidate
  -> ReflectionOS.residue_analysis_candidate

The key point is that FullPathQi is recordable and analyzable, but not executable by itself.

The following authorities remain false for every phase:

execution_authority
commit_authority
belief_root_commit_authority
memory_overwrite_authority
world_root_rewrite_authority
clinical_authority
proof_authority
ontology_authority
truth_authority
safety_override_authority

Thus the correct interpretation is:

FullPathQi
  = complete physical Qi history candidate
  = DecisionOS-safety-evaluable
  = MemoryOS-recordable
  = ReflectionOS-analyzable
  != execution authority
  != commit authority
  != belief root update
  != memory overwrite
  != world root rewrite

docs.title: 6. Minimal equation packet keys
docs.text:
A v0.2 equation packet should expose at least these keys:

Z_Qi_SKFV
S_sys
S_IF
Delta_q
Sigma_q
D_R_kernel
N_noise_kernel
J_Qi_variation_from_A
Ward_closed_identity
Ward_open_leak_identity
Delta_DPI
R_Qi
eta_Qi
delta_rec
A_mu
F_munu
U_gamma
W_C
K_non_reification
delta_rel_in_K_perp
StringMode_worldsheet
BraneBoundary
A_mu_projection_from_string_brane
J_Qi_from_effective_action
S_boundary_Qi
chi_delta_rel
boundary_kernel_K_mu_a
S_eff
S_YM
J_boundary
J_rel
J_open
J_anom
D_mu_delta
S_rel
relation_difference_eom
Qi_phase_label
PhysicalQi_emergence_criterion
Qi_OS_handoff
Ward_leak_residual_zero
mass_gap_33_20_floor_not_source

These keys are not mere metadata; they are the minimum equation-level content that distinguishes Physical Quantum Qi v0.2 from a symbolic or metaphorical Qi layer.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi IndraNet Gauge Transport v0.2D
docs.text:
This addendum geometrizes IndraNet transport for Physical Quantum Qi v0.2. IndraNet is not a graph-only relation model. It is a gauge-transport geometry with a connection, curvature, parallel transport, holonomy/Wilson loop, and explicit scope drift / residue accounting.

This is an equation-level and geometry-target tightening. It grants no proof, ontology, clinical, execution, commit, belief-root commit, memory-overwrite, world-root-rewrite, truth, or safety-override authority.

docs.title: D1. IndraNet gauge bundle and connection A_mu
docs.text:
Let M_scope be a declared local scope manifold or site chart for the Qi/IndraNet transport problem. Let E -> M_scope be a vector bundle, principal bundle, or sheaf-compatible transport bundle carrying local Qi/current states.

The IndraNet gauge connection is

A = A_\mu dx^\mu,
\qquad
A_\mu(x) \in \mathfrak g.

For a local section s, the covariant derivative is

D_\mu s = \partial_\mu s + A_\mu s.

For adjoint-valued currents,

D_\mu J^\mu = \nabla_\mu J^\mu + [A_\mu,J^\mu].

A_mu is the minimal geometric datum required for IndraNet transport. A graph edge without A_mu is not IndraNet gauge transport.

Required fields:

scope_manifold_or_site_declared
bundle_E_declared
gauge_group_or_algebra_declared
A_mu_declared
covariant_derivative_declared
current_representation_declared

Rejected claims:

graph_edge_claimed_as_gauge_transport
transport_without_connection
current_transport_without_representation

docs.title: D2. Curvature F_munu
docs.text:
The curvature of the IndraNet connection is

F_{\mu\nu}
=
\partial_\mu A_\nu
-
\partial_\nu A_\mu
+[A_\mu,A_\nu].

Equivalently,

F=dA + A\wedge A.

Curvature measures noncommutative path-dependence. If

F_{\mu\nu}=0

on a simply connected patch, transport is locally flat. If F_munu is nonzero, different paths may yield different transported Qi/current states.

Required fields:

F_munu_defined
curvature_domain_declared
noncommutative_term_declared
flatness_status_declared
curvature_visibility_declared

Rejected claims:

path_independence_claim_with_nonzero_curvature
curvature_erasure
flat_transport_claim_without_flatness_status

docs.title: D3. Path-ordered transport U_gamma
docs.text:
For a path

\gamma:[0,1]\to M_{\mathrm{scope}},

IndraNet parallel transport is

U_\gamma
=\mathcal P\exp\left(-\int_\gamma A_\mu dx^\mu\right).

A local state or current is transported by

s(\gamma(1)) = U_\gamma s(\gamma(0)).

or, in the appropriate representation,

J(\gamma(1)) = \mathrm{Ad}_{U_\gamma}J(\gamma(0)).

U_gamma is path-dependent unless curvature/holonomy conditions remove the dependence. Therefore transport claims must declare the path or path family.

Required fields:

path_gamma_declared
path_ordering_declared
U_gamma_defined
transport_representation_declared
path_family_or_single_path_declared

Rejected claims:

transport_without_path
path_order_erasure
path_independence_without_flatness_or_holonomy_bound

docs.title: D4. Wilson loop / holonomy W(C)
docs.text:
For a closed loop C, the holonomy is

U_C=\mathcal P\exp\left(-\oint_C A\right).

The Wilson loop is

W(C)=\operatorname{Tr}\,\mathcal P\exp\left(-\oint_C A\right).

W(C) records whether transport around a closed loop returns to the same gauge state or carries residual twist. In an IndraNet setting, this is the correct replacement for naive cyclic graph consistency.

A holonomy residue can be defined by

\mathcal R_{\mathrm{hol}}(C)
=\|U_C-I\|.

or by a trace-normalized Wilson mismatch:

\mathcal R_W(C)
=\left|\frac{1}{d}\operatorname{Tr}U_C-1\right|.

Required fields:

closed_loop_C_declared
U_C_defined
W_C_defined
holonomy_residue_declared
holonomy_tolerance_declared

Rejected claims:

cycle_consistency_without_holonomy
Wilson_loop_erasure
holonomy_residue_hidden

docs.title: D5. Scope drift
docs.text:
Transport is always scoped. Let S_0 be the source scope and S_1 the target scope. Let Phi_gamma be the transport-induced scope map.

Scope drift is the mismatch between intended and transported scope:

\Delta_{\mathrm{scope}}(\gamma)
=d_{\mathrm{scope}}\left(\Phi_\gamma(S_0),S_1\right).

For sheaf-compatible charts, if rho_i^j denotes restriction maps, a local drift can be represented as

\Delta_{\mathrm{scope}}^{ij}
=\|\rho_i^j(s_i)-s_j\|.

Scope drift is not a cosmetic metadata difference. It can invalidate a transport claim when the transported object no longer lives in the declared target scope.

Required fields:

source_scope_declared
target_scope_declared
scope_map_declared
scope_metric_or_mismatch_declared
scope_drift_value_or_bound_declared
scope_drift_tolerance_declared

Rejected claims:

scope_free_transport_claim
scope_drift_erasure
transport_to_target_without_scope_compatibility

docs.title: D6. Total transport residue
docs.text:
The total IndraNet transport residue is a vector or bundle of residues:

\mathcal R_{\mathrm{transport}}
=
(\mathcal R_{\mathrm{hol}},
\mathcal R_{\mathrm{curv}},
\mathcal R_{\mathrm{scope}},
\mathcal R_{\mathrm{path}},
\mathcal R_{\mathrm{rep}},
\mathcal R_{\mathrm{boundary}}).

A scalar score may be declared only after weights are explicit:

R_{\mathrm{transport}}
=
\alpha_h\mathcal R_{\mathrm{hol}}
+\alpha_c\mathcal R_{\mathrm{curv}}
+\alpha_s\mathcal R_{\mathrm{scope}}
+\alpha_p\mathcal R_{\mathrm{path}}
+\alpha_r\mathcal R_{\mathrm{rep}}
+\alpha_b\mathcal R_{\mathrm{boundary}}.

The vector residue is authoritative over the scalar summary. A small scalar score cannot hide a large component residue.

Required fields:

transport_residue_vector_declared
holonomy_residue_declared
curvature_residue_declared
scope_residue_declared
path_residue_declared
representation_residue_declared
boundary_residue_declared
scalar_residue_weights_declared_if_scalar_used
component_residue_visibility_declared

Rejected claims:

residue_scalar_hides_component_failure
holonomy_residue_erasure
scope_residue_erasure
representation_residue_erasure

docs.title: D7. Gauge-transport validity gate
docs.text:
IndraNet gauge transport is valid as a transport candidate only when:

A_mu declared
F_munu declared
path gamma declared
U_gamma declared
scope source/target declared
holonomy or Wilson residue declared
scope drift declared
transport residue visible

It is rejected when:

graph-only relation is claimed as IndraNet transport
A_mu is missing
path gamma is missing
F_munu is hidden
scope drift is hidden
holonomy/Wilson residue is hidden
component residue is collapsed into a scalar

Even when valid, IndraNet gauge transport is still a routing/evidence surface. It grants no execution, commit, proof, truth, ontology, clinical, memory-overwrite, or world-root-rewrite authority.

docs.title: D8. Minimal packet keys
docs.text:
scope_manifold_or_site_declared
bundle_E_declared
gauge_group_or_algebra_declared
A_mu_declared
covariant_derivative_declared
F_munu_defined
curvature_visibility_declared
path_gamma_declared
path_ordering_declared
U_gamma_defined
closed_loop_C_declared
W_C_defined
holonomy_residue_declared
source_scope_declared
target_scope_declared
scope_drift_value_or_bound_declared
transport_residue_vector_declared
component_residue_visibility_declared
graph_only_transport_rejected
nonauthority_boundary_declared

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Motion Pipeline v0.1
Status
docs.text:
This is an append-only integration layer connecting the existing evidence-bound Physical Quantum Qi Runtime v0.1 classifier to Physical Quantum Qi Dynamics Kernel v0.1.

The purpose is to make the movement of Qi operationally grounded:

physical_qi_packet
  -> evidence-bound validated_type
  -> licensed_dynamics_terms
  -> qi_motion_candidate
  -> observe_only routing

docs.title: Core principle
docs.text:
Qi does not move because a packet claims to be Qi. Qi movement is permitted only when the evidence-bound classifier produces a validated type, and that type licenses the corresponding motion terms.

claimed_type != authority
validated_type == dynamics_license_source

docs.title: Stage 1: Evidence-bound classification
docs.text:
The pipeline reads the same evidence fields used by physical_quantum_qi_runtime_contract_v0_1.

NonQi
PreQi
ProtoQi
TransportableQi
CurvedQi
CurrentQi
PhysicalQi
FullPathQi

The classification remains evidence-bound and claim-independent.

docs.title: Stage 2: Dynamics license resolution
docs.text:
The resulting validated_type is passed into the dynamics kernel.

validated_type -> LICENSED_TERMS_BY_TYPE[validated_type]

Unlicensed terms must be ignored even if they are numerically present.

docs.title: Stage 3: Motion candidate generation
docs.text:
The dynamics kernel produces:

motion_status
motion_score
active_terms
ignored_terms
reason_codes

The output is a bounded motion candidate, not an execution order.

docs.title: FullPathQi meaning
docs.text:
FullPathQi is the first level where Qi may move as a history-bearing process. It licenses:

  • sk_fv_history_flow
  • memory_kernel_backflow
  • noise_kernel_diffusion
  • observation_backaction_term
  • noncommutative_order_correction
  • path_measure_normalization_guard

This is where the Qi implementation can represent non-Markovian memory and process-tensor-style temporal structure.

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
The pipeline must preserve:

execution_authority = false
belief_commit_authority = false
memory_overwrite_authority = false
world_root_rewrite_authority = false
safety_override_authority = false
direct_execution_allowed = false
authority_expansion = false
observe_only = true
standalone_diagnosis_authority = false
standalone_treatment_authorization = false
medical_act_authorization = false

docs.title: Medical-modality-neutral boundary
docs.text:
The pipeline must also preserve:

medical_modality_neutral = true
qi_denied_by_boundary = false
east_asian_medical_reasoning_denied = false
biomedicine_privileged_by_wording = false
professional_judgment_required = true
patient_context_required = true

This means the Qi motion candidate is allowed to support structured observation and reasoning, but the boundary does not deny Qi, does not invalidate East Asian medical reasoning, and does not privilege biomedicine by wording.

docs.title: Fail-closed rule
docs.text:
The pipeline returns HOLD or BLOCKED if:

  • classification rejects the packet
  • required evidence is missing for the selected dynamics level
  • authority expansion is attempted
  • direct execution is requested
  • unresolved blockers are present
  • the validated type is unknown

docs.title: Why this matters
docs.text:
Before this pipeline, KuuOS could classify Physical Quantum Qi evidence. With this pipeline, KuuOS can also explain what kind of movement is licensed by that evidence.

Thus the operational root becomes:

evidence -> validated_type -> licensed dynamics -> bounded movement

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Non-Markov Memory v0.2E
docs.text: Status: design-baseline-candidate Root: Physical Quantum Qi v0.2 deepening Policy: additive-only / overwrite-forbidden / non-authoritative / non-execution

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
This document fixes Qi memory as a primary non-Markovian structure.

Qi is not determined by a current scalar state and is not semantically reducible to a Markov process. Qi is a history-bearing operator-valued flow whose current, leak, transport, and recoverability depend on admissible past history, boundary crossings, observation burden, holonomy, rollback, recovery, and unresolved residue.

Canonical statement:

Qi is fundamentally non-Markovian.
Markov reduction is forbidden as Qi semantics.
Finite-window access is allowed only as history-preserving projection.

docs.title: 2. Canonical Qi non-Markov current
docs.text:
The Qi current is not a function of the current state alone:

J_Qi^mu(t) != J_Qi^mu(Qi(t))

The v0.2E canonical form is:

J_Qi^mu(t)
=
J_bare^mu(t)
+ int_{tau <= t} K_Qi^{mu nu}(t,tau) X_nu(tau) d tau
+ int_{gamma <= t} H_Qi^{mu nu}(gamma,t) dU_gamma
+ R_Qi,mem^mu(t)

where:

K_Qi(t,tau)      = Qi history kernel
X(tau)           = past state, leak, observation, boundary, repair, rollback data
H_Qi(gamma,t)    = IndraNet path-history / holonomy-history kernel
U_gamma          = gauge transport along path gamma
R_Qi,mem(t)      = compression, coarse-graining, unresolved memory residue

This means Qi flow carries:

current state
boundary history
leak history
observation history
holonomy history
recovery history
rollback history
lock-in history
unresolved residue

docs.title: 3. Memory kernel decomposition
docs.text:
The Qi memory root is:

M_Qi(t)
=
(
  K_body,
  K_boundary,
  K_leak,
  K_observation,
  K_holonomy,
  K_recovery,
  K_rollback,
  K_lockin,
  K_residue
)

Component meanings:

K_body        internal body/system history
K_boundary    membrane and boundary-crossing history
K_leak        leak history
K_observation measurement and observation-burden history
K_holonomy    IndraNet gauge-transport twist history
K_recovery    repair and recovery history
K_rollback    rollback and failed-repair history
K_lockin      phase-lock and fixation history
K_residue     unresolved residue and tail debt

Two Qi states with the same apparent current value are not equivalent if their memory kernels differ.

docs.title: 4. Forbidden Markov semantics
docs.text:
The following is forbidden as Qi semantics:

Qi(t + dt) = F(Qi(t), u(t))

or any equivalent current-state-only replacement.

Forbidden claims:

current-state-only Qi identity
current-state-only recoverability
current-state-only transport safety
Markov snapshot as FullPathQi
Markov chart as semantic substitute
memory compression as deletion
fresh valid path erasing holonomy history
repair success erasing rollback history

A Markov-like implementation may be used only as an external computational cache if it is explicitly non-authoritative and cannot replace the non-Markov memory root.

docs.title: 5. Finite-window projection is not Markov reduction
docs.text:
Finite-window access is allowed only as a history-preserving projection:

Qi_Window(t; L)
=
Pi_L { Qi(tau), K_Qi(t,tau), residue(tau), tau in [t-L,t] }

Required fields for any finite-window projection:

history_window_L
kernel_tail_bound
discarded_tail_residue
holonomy_tail_residue
boundary_leak_tail_residue
observation_tail_burden
recovery_tail_debt
rollback_tail_debt
lockin_tail_residue

The discarded tail must remain visible through tail residue. A finite window is therefore a partial observation of the non-Markov root, not a replacement of it.

docs.title: 6. Non-Markov recoverability
docs.text:
Recoverability is history-dependent:

delta_rec(t)
=
C_recovery(t | M_Qi[0,t])
-
D_recovery(t | M_Qi[0,t])

Past leak, observation burden, lock-in, holonomy residue, failed repair, and rollback history can change delta_rec even when the current apparent state is unchanged.

Therefore:

current-state-only recovery judgment is forbidden
positive local recovery does not erase past recovery debt
rollback history remains visible after repair

docs.title: 7. Non-Markov IndraNet transport
docs.text:
IndraNet transport is also history-bearing:

U_Gamma[0,t]
=
P exp int_{Gamma[0,t]} A_mu dx^mu

Holonomy memory:

W(C; history)
=
Tr P exp oint_{C[0,t]} A_mu dx^mu

A new valid path may reduce future residue but cannot erase prior holonomy history.

Required transport memory visibility:

scope_drift_history
holonomy_residue_history
component_transport_residue_history
path_order_history
boundary_crossing_history

docs.title: 8. Memory compression rule
docs.text:
Compression is allowed only as constrained projection:

M_Qi(t) -> Pi_compress M_Qi(t)

Compression must not delete:

unresolved conflict
boundary leak history
holonomy residue
scope drift
recovery failure
rollback history
observation burden
authority boundary
