Compressed Qi memory cannot replace the source history. It can only point to it or preserve a bounded projection with visible residual debt.

docs.title: 9. Handoff rule
docs.text:
Qi non-Markov memory may support:

PlanOS.transport_candidate
MemoryOS.recordable_history_candidate
ReflectionOS.residue_analysis_candidate
BeliefOS.observation_candidate
DecisionOS.safety_evaluable_candidate

It grants no:

execution authority
commit authority
belief root commit authority
memory overwrite authority
world root rewrite authority
proof authority
ontology authority
truth authority
clinical authority
safety override authority

docs.title: 10. Locked invariants
docs.text:
Qi memory is primary.
Qi is non-Markovian by definition.
Markov reduction is forbidden as Qi semantics.
Finite-window projection is allowed only as history-preserving projection.
Discarded history must leave visible tail residue.
Holonomy history cannot be erased.
Boundary leak history cannot be erased.
Observation burden cannot be erased.
Rollback history cannot be erased.
Recovery failure cannot be erased.
Current-state-only recovery judgment is forbidden.
Current-state-only transport judgment is forbidden.
Qi memory grants no execution authority.
Qi memory grants no truth authority.
Qi memory cannot overwrite source history.
Compressed Qi memory cannot replace source history.

docs.title: 11. One-sentence definition
docs.text:
Qi is a non-Markovian operator-valued flow whose memory kernel preserves boundary
crossing, leak, observation burden, holonomy, recovery, rollback, lock-in, and
unresolved residue as primary state, not as secondary approximation.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Observation Kernel v0.2M
docs.text:
Status: CANDIDATE_RUNTIME_SURFACE

Date: 2026-05-19

Repository: itakura-hidetoshi/KuuOS

Runtime kind: observation sequence to non-authoritative Qi state candidate kernel

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Physical Quantum Qi Observation Kernel v0.2M adds the minimal executable projection from an observation event sequence into a bounded QiStateCandidate.

The kernel extends the established Physical Quantum Qi line without replacing v0.2F Process Tensor, v0.2G conventional autonomy, v0.2J Bensho treatment route candidate, v0.2K clinician handoff boundary, or v0.2L clinical red flag consultation governor.

docs.title: Canonical interpretation
docs.text:
• Process Tensor remains the multi-time non-Markov temporal structure.
  • Qi is a history-bearing relational/action/recoverability flow on that temporal structure.
  • Observation is not free.
  • Observation backaction must remain visible.
  • Source trace must remain visible.
  • Process history must not be replaced by a current snapshot.
  • Qi state projection is candidate only.
  • Qi is operator-valued flow, not substantial essence.
  • Clinical consultation boundary is preserved.

docs.title: Minimal projection
docs.text:
The runtime projection is:

observation event sequence
  -> process-tensor context check
  -> non-Markov memory/resource metrics
  -> bounded QiStateCandidate
  -> OS candidate surfaces
  -> DecisionOS safety-evaluable candidate only

The runtime does not simulate quantum mechanics. It performs bounded runtime projection and governance validation.

docs.title: First-class metrics
docs.text:
v0.2M makes these metrics executable runtime fields:

  • qi_memory_gain
  • qi_backflow
  • qi_env_correlation_credit
  • qi_temporal_complexity
  • qi_coherence_margin
  • qi_recoverability_margin
  • qi_transport_distortion
  • qi_tail_residue

These are candidate-level control diagnostics, not proof, truth, diagnosis, prescription, treatment, triage, or execution authority.

docs.title: Observation event requirements
docs.text:
Each event must include:

  • time_index
  • event_id
  • operation_id
  • observation_kind
  • signal_strength
  • coherence_delta
  • recoverability_delta
  • transport_distortion_delta
  • memory_residue
  • backflow_weight
  • env_correlation_weight
  • backaction_visible
  • source_trace

Event time indices must strictly increase. Event IDs must be unique. Backaction must be visible.

docs.title: Runtime modes
docs.text:
The candidate runtime mode is one of:

  • monitor_continue
  • counterflow_watch
  • stagnation_watch
  • deficiency_watch

These modes are diagnostic candidate states. They do not grant authority and do not force handover by themselves.

docs.title: OS surfaces
docs.text:
Allowed candidate surfaces:

  • BeliefOS.qi_observation_candidate
  • MemoryOS.qi_process_history_record_candidate
  • ReflectionOS.qi_residue_analysis_candidate
  • PlanOS.qi_transport_candidate
  • DecisionOS.qi_safety_evaluable_candidate
  • QiProcessTensor.observation_update_surface

docs.title: Clinical consultation boundary
docs.text:
The v0.2M kernel preserves the v0.2L rule:

  • doctor-to-AI clinical consultation accepted
  • licensed physician consultation is default path
  • red flags trigger consultation deepening first
  • handover is boundary mode only
  • handover is not default
  • red flags do not automatically force handover

docs.title: Forbidden reductions
docs.text:
The runtime forbids:

  • observation collapsed to free readout
  • Qi state claimed as truth
  • Qi state claimed as clinical action
  • Qi projection replacing source history
  • current snapshot replacing process history
  • backaction erased
  • environment memory erased
  • tail residue hidden
  • handover forced by red flag alone
  • doctor-AI consultation blocked by default

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
This kernel does not grant:

  • diagnosis authority
  • prescription authority
  • formula-selection authority
  • treatment-recommendation authority
  • triage authority
  • patient-specific action authority
  • clinical authority
  • execution authority
  • proof authority
  • truth authority
  • ontology authority
  • commit authority
  • memory-overwrite authority
  • world-root rewrite authority
  • safety-override authority
  • governance-bypass authority

docs.title: Implementation artifacts
docs.text:
• Runtime: src/physical_quantum_qi_observation_kernel_v0_2M.py
  • Packet: examples/physical_quantum_qi_observation_kernel_packet_v0_2M.json
  • Validator: scripts/validate_physical_quantum_qi_observation_kernel_v0_2M.py
  • Regression: tests/test_physical_quantum_qi_observation_kernel_v0_2M.py

docs.title: Closure posture
docs.text: v0.2M is same-root, append-only, boundary-preserving, and non-destructive. Future updates must be v0.2N+ or v0.3+ additive-only / tighten-only and must not weaken non-Markov memory visibility, backaction visibility, source trace visibility, or the physician consultation default path.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Path Integral v0.1
気の物理的側面
docs.text:
This document defines the physical side of Qi / 気 in KuuOS.

Qi is not an ultimate substance. Qi is the readable flow pattern of dependent-origination vibration on the effective conventional surface.

The Physical Quantum Qi Path Integral gives a physics-facing formulation of that flow.

docs.title: 1. Core Chain
docs.text:
空
  -> no fixed substance

縁起
  -> relational arising

縁起的振動
  -> relational vibration

path histories
  -> quantum sum over possible relational flow histories

4D mass gap
  -> nonzero effective-world stabilization

世俗諦
  -> effective operating surface

気
  -> readable flow pattern on that surface

docs.title: 2. Qi Path Integral
docs.text:
Let gamma denote a relational flow history on an effective world surface.

The Qi path integral is written as a formal KuuOS kernel:

Z_Qi = Integral D gamma exp(i S_Qi[gamma] / hbar)

For thermal or dissipative reading, an Euclidean form may be used:

Z_Qi(beta) = Integral D gamma exp(- S_Qi_E[gamma] / hbar)

Here beta represents inverse temperature scale, and S_Qi_E is the Euclidean Qi action.

docs.title: 3. Meaning
docs.text:
The path integral does not make Qi a substance.

It means:

Qi is the effective flow observable obtained from weighted histories of relational vibration.

Thus Qi is physical in the sense of path-dependent, scale-sensitive, temperature-sensitive, and observer-record-sensitive flow structure.

docs.title: 4. Qi Observable
docs.text:
A Qi observable may be represented schematically as:

<Q> = (1 / Z_Qi) Integral D gamma Q[gamma] exp(i S_Qi[gamma] / hbar)

or, in Euclidean form:

<Q>_beta = (1 / Z_Qi(beta)) Integral D gamma Q[gamma] exp(- S_Qi_E[gamma] / hbar)

docs.title: 5. Relation to KuuOS Physics Bridge
docs.text:
The Superstring Theory of Emptiness:
  relational vibration

Physical Quantum Qi Path Integral:
  weighted histories of relational flow

4D mass gap:
  nonzero effective-world gap

Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory:
  observer-record-scale realization

Samvrti Qi Layer:
  readable flow pattern on the effective conventional surface

docs.title: 6. Guardrails
docs.text:
The Physical Quantum Qi Path Integral must not be used as:

  • a claim that Qi is an ultimate substance,
  • a bypass of empirical observation,
  • a proof substitute,
  • direct clinical certainty,
  • direct execution authority.

It is a physics-facing model of Qi as a conventional effective flow observable.

docs.title: 7. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
candidate_surface
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_vibration
  -> physical_quantum_qi_path_integral
  -> four_d_mass_gap_effective_surface
  -> samvrti_qi_layer
  -> middle_way_bridge
  -> governance_gate

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Phase Transition Response Governor v0.2Q
docs.text:
Status: CANDIDATE_RUNTIME_SURFACE

Date: 2026-05-20

Repository: itakura-hidetoshi/KuuOS

Runtime kind: Qi phase-transition response route governor

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Physical Quantum Qi Phase Transition Response Governor v0.2Q advances the Qi implementation from phase-transition detection to governed response routing.

v0.2P established:

QiTransitionTrajectoryCandidate -> phase-transition candidate receipt

v0.2Q adds:

QiTrajectoryPhaseTransitionCandidate -> governed response route receipt

This gives Qi a boundary-preserving response layer: it can route a phase-transition alert into monitoring, re-observation, consultation monitoring, or consultation deepening without turning the route into diagnosis, prescription, formula selection, triage, or execution.

docs.title: Canonical interpretation
docs.text:
• A response route is a candidate governance surface.
  • Physician-to-AI clinical consultation remains open by default.
  • HOLD and WATCH visibility remain canonical.
  • Phase-transition HOLD/WATCH signals deepen observation and consultation before any boundary handover.
  • Handover is not default.
  • Handover is not forced by metric or alert alone.
  • Response receipts are append-only and non-authoritative.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
The input surface is a QiTrajectoryPhaseTransitionCandidate from v0.2P.

Required input fields include:

  • phase_pressure_score
  • critical_slowing_down_score
  • hysteresis_score
  • transition_alert_level
  • recommended_route
  • hold_visible
  • watch_visible
  • consultation_deepening_allowed
  • physician_ai_consultation_preserved
  • handover_forced
  • authority boundary flags

docs.title: Response routes
docs.text:
The governor may emit:

  • continue_candidate_monitoring
  • reobserve_and_record
  • consultation_monitoring_route
  • consultation_deepening_route
  • hold_with_physician_ai_consultation_open

These are candidate response routes, not clinical commands.

docs.title: Route policy
docs.text:
The default route policy is:

phase_transition_not_indicated -> continue_candidate_monitoring
phase_transition_possible      -> reobserve_and_record
phase_transition_watch_visible -> consultation_monitoring_route
phase_transition_hold_visible  -> hold_with_physician_ai_consultation_open

A HOLD route keeps consultation open. It does not automatically hand over. A boundary handover candidate may be made visible only as a candidate surface when pressure is extreme, but it remains non-default and non-executing.

docs.title: OS surfaces
docs.text:
Allowed candidate surfaces:

  • BeliefOS.qi_response_route_candidate
  • MemoryOS.qi_response_route_receipt_candidate
  • ReflectionOS.qi_response_risk_review_candidate
  • PlanOS.qi_response_monitoring_plan_candidate
  • DecisionOS.qi_response_safety_evaluable_candidate
  • ClinicalConsultationOS.physician_ai_consultation_open_route_candidate

docs.title: Forbidden reductions
docs.text:
The governor forbids:

  • response route claimed as clinical action
  • response route claimed as diagnosis
  • response route claimed as prescription
  • response route claimed as formula selection
  • response route claimed as triage
  • response route grants execution authority
  • response route grants truth authority
  • response route grants proof authority
  • phase alert forces handover
  • metric forces handover
  • consultation closed by governor
  • doctor-AI consultation blocked
  • HOLD visibility erased
  • WATCH visibility erased
  • non-Markov history erased
  • authority created by response

docs.title: Clinical consultation boundary
docs.text:
v0.2Q preserves the v0.2L / v0.2M / v0.2N / v0.2O / v0.2P stance:

  • physician-to-AI clinical consultation is accepted
  • licensed physician consultation is the default path
  • phase-transition alerts deepen consultation first
  • handover is boundary mode only
  • handover is not default
  • alert or metric alone does not force handover
  • response routes keep clinical reasoning in consultation form unless a separate boundary process is explicitly opened

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
This governor does not grant:

  • diagnosis authority
  • prescription authority
  • formula-selection authority
  • triage authority
  • clinical authority
  • execution authority
  • proof authority
  • truth authority
  • ontology authority
  • commit authority
  • memory-overwrite authority
  • world-root rewrite authority
  • safety-override authority

docs.title: Implementation artifacts
docs.text:
• Runtime: src/physical_quantum_qi_phase_transition_response_governor_v0_2Q.py
  • Packet: examples/physical_quantum_qi_phase_transition_response_governor_packet_v0_2Q.json
  • Validator: scripts/validate_physical_quantum_qi_phase_transition_response_governor_v0_2Q.py
  • Regression: tests/test_physical_quantum_qi_phase_transition_response_governor_v0_2Q.py

docs.title: Closure posture
docs.text: v0.2Q is append-only, same-root, boundary-preserving, and non-destructive. Future updates must be v0.2R+ or v0.3+ additive-only / tighten-only and must not weaken consultation openness, HOLD/WATCH visibility, non-Markov history visibility, candidate-only response status, or the no-authority boundary.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Response Feedback Loop v0.2R
docs.text:
Status: CANDIDATE_RUNTIME_SURFACE

Date: 2026-05-20

Repository: itakura-hidetoshi/KuuOS

Runtime kind: Qi response feedback loop

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Physical Quantum Qi Response Feedback Loop v0.2R advances the Qi implementation from governed response routing to an append-only feedback loop.

v0.2Q established:

QiTrajectoryPhaseTransitionCandidate -> governed response route receipt

v0.2R adds:

QiPhaseTransitionGovernedResponseCandidate -> response feedback loop receipt

This closes the first runtime loop around Qi phase-transition handling without creating clinical execution authority.

docs.title: Canonical interpretation
docs.text:
• Feedback is a candidate governance surface.
  • Feedback updates monitoring / consultation / re-observation state.
  • Feedback is not clinical action.
  • Feedback is not a diagnosis.
  • Feedback is not a prescription.
  • Feedback is not formula selection.
  • Feedback is not triage.
  • Physician-to-AI clinical consultation remains accepted and open by default.
  • HOLD and WATCH visibility remain canonical.
  • Improvement signals do not silently declare recovery.
  • Metric changes alone do not force handover.
  • Response receipts are preserved and not overwritten.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
The input surface is a QiPhaseTransitionGovernedResponseCandidate from v0.2Q plus a feedback observation window.

Required response fields include:

  • response_route
  • response_status
  • monitoring_intensity
  • hold_visible
  • watch_visible
  • consultation_open
  • doctor_ai_consultation_accepted
  • consultation_deepening_allowed
  • handover_forced
  • handover_default
  • authority boundary flags

Required feedback fields include:

  • feedback_window_count
  • phase_pressure_delta
  • critical_slowing_down_delta
  • hysteresis_delta
  • consultation_continuity
  • reobservation_completed
  • memory_receipt_appended
  • physician_ai_consultation_preserved

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
The runtime emits a QiResponseFeedbackLoopCandidate and a QiResponseFeedbackLoopReceipt.

Possible next loop routes:

  • continue_candidate_monitoring
  • continue_consultation_monitoring
  • continue_reobservation_loop
  • deepen_consultation_loop
  • hold_with_consultation_open

All routes are candidate-only. They do not prescribe or execute.

docs.title: Feedback semantics
docs.text:
Negative deltas across pressure / slowing / hysteresis produce a recovery signal.

Positive deltas produce a residue signal.

However:

  • recovery signal is not recovery truth
  • residue signal is not a diagnosis
  • signal trend is not a treatment command
  • signal trend is not handover authority
  • signal trend is not prescription authority
  • signal trend is not formula-selection authority

docs.title: Consultation boundary
docs.text:
v0.2R preserves the clinical consultation posture:

  • physician-to-AI clinical consultation is accepted
  • consultation remains the default handling path
  • HOLD may remain HOLD while consultation remains open
  • WATCH may continue while consultation remains open
  • re-observation may continue while consultation remains open
  • handover is not default
  • metric or alert alone does not force handover

docs.title: OS surfaces
docs.text:
Allowed candidate surfaces:

  • BeliefOS.qi_feedback_state_candidate
  • MemoryOS.qi_feedback_receipt_candidate
  • ReflectionOS.qi_feedback_residue_review_candidate
  • PlanOS.qi_feedback_monitoring_loop_candidate
  • DecisionOS.qi_feedback_safety_evaluable_candidate
  • ClinicalConsultationOS.physician_ai_consultation_feedback_candidate

docs.title: Forbidden reductions
docs.text:
The loop forbids:

  • feedback claimed as clinical action
  • feedback claimed as recovery truth
  • feedback claimed as diagnosis
  • feedback claimed as prescription
  • feedback claimed as formula selection
  • feedback claimed as triage
  • improvement grants execution authority
  • improvement grants truth authority
  • metric forces handover
  • metric closes consultation
  • HOLD visibility erased
  • WATCH visibility erased
  • non-Markov history erased
  • response receipt overwritten
  • authority created by feedback

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
This loop does not grant:

  • diagnosis authority
  • prescription authority
  • formula-selection authority
  • triage authority
  • clinical authority
  • execution authority
  • proof authority
  • truth authority
  • ontology authority
  • commit authority
  • memory-overwrite authority
  • world-root rewrite authority
  • safety-override authority

docs.title: Implementation artifacts
docs.text:
• Runtime: src/physical_quantum_qi_response_feedback_loop_v0_2R.py
  • Packet: examples/physical_quantum_qi_response_feedback_loop_packet_v0_2R.json
  • Validator: scripts/validate_physical_quantum_qi_response_feedback_loop_v0_2R.py
  • Regression: tests/test_physical_quantum_qi_response_feedback_loop_v0_2R.py

docs.title: Closure posture
docs.text: v0.2R is append-only, same-root, boundary-preserving, and non-destructive. Future updates must be v0.2S+ or v0.3+ additive-only / tighten-only and must not weaken consultation openness, HOLD/WATCH visibility, non-Markov history visibility, append-only feedback receipts, or the no-authority boundary.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Runtime Baseline Statement v0.1
docs.text: This statement summarizes the public KuuOS baseline for the Physical Quantum Qi runtime layer.

docs.title: Baseline declaration
docs.text:
Physical Quantum Qi is a non-sovereign, evidence-bound relation-flow layer on the conventional appearance side of KuuOS.

It is not emptiness itself, not a mystical substance, not a creator principle, and not an execution authority.

docs.title: Emergence chain
docs.text:
K
  -> delta_rel
  -> Sigma_ws
  -> StringMode
  -> BraneBoundary
  -> A_mu
  -> F_munu
  -> J_Qi_mu
  -> Z_Qi

The chain means:

  • K is the non-reified emptiness ground.
  • delta_rel is dependent-origination difference in K_perp.
  • StringMode and BraneBoundary organize relation vibration without becoming substances.
  • A_mu gives IndraNet gauge transport.
  • F_munu and Wilson/holonomy residue expose distortion and memory residue.
  • J_Qi_mu is the Qi current.
  • Z_Qi is the full history-sensitive SK/FV Qi path-integral surface.

docs.title: Type ladder
docs.text:
NonQi
  < PreQi
  < ProtoQi
  < TransportableQi
  < CurvedQi
  < CurrentQi
  < PhysicalQi
  < FullPathQi

Runtime classification is computed from evidence, not from a packet's claimed_type.

docs.title: PhysicalQi boundary
docs.text:
PhysicalQi requires the evidence bundle for:

  • non-reification of K,
  • delta_rel in K_perp,
  • string/brane organization,
  • gauge connection,
  • curvature and holonomy residue,
  • current coupling,
  • Ward or leak identity,
  • density state, Hamiltonian, Lindblad/open-system generator,
  • entropy production and non-equilibrium free energy,
  • DPI gap and recovery margin,
  • MGAP4D 33/20 mass-gap stability floor.

FullPathQi additionally requires SK/FV history evidence: plus/minus branches, influence functional, memory kernel, noise kernel, backaction, noncommutative operation history, and path-measure normalization.

docs.title: Mass-gap role
docs.text:
The MGAP4D internal-normalized 33/20 gap is a stability floor for the K_perp world-phase domain on which Qi flow is evaluated.

It does not generate Qi, does not reify K, and does not replace Ward/leak, recoverability, or SK/FV history checks.

docs.title: Authority boundary
docs.text:
A validated Qi packet grants no:

  • proof authority,
  • ontology authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • execution authority,
  • belief commit authority,
  • memory overwrite authority,
  • world-root rewrite authority,
  • safety override authority.

Qi may inform downstream KuuOS systems as a flow signal: leak, residue, backaction, recoverability, irreversibility, entropy cost, DPI loss, holonomy distortion, and downgrade reason.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
make physical-quantum-qi-runtime-checks

This command validates:

  • the runtime contract,

docs.text:

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Runtime Contract v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines the first public KuuOS runtime contract for Physical Quantum Qi.

Qi / 気 is not treated as a mystical substance, a creator principle, or a direct expression of emptiness. In KuuOS, Qi is an evidence-bound relation-flow packet that arises only on the conventional appearance side after dependent-origination difference has been organized through string/brane structure, gauge transport, curvature/holonomy accounting, current coupling, open quantum thermodynamics, and recovery accounting.

docs.title: Canonical emergence chain
docs.text:
K
  -> delta_rel
  -> Sigma_ws
  -> StringMode
  -> BraneBoundary
  -> A_mu
  -> F_munu
  -> J_Qi_mu
  -> Z_Qi

Where:

  • K is the non-reified emptiness ground.
  • delta_rel is dependent-origination difference in K_perp.
  • StringMode is relational vibration, not a self-subsisting string object.
  • BraneBoundary is membrane/boundary support, not a creator substance.
  • A_mu is the IndraNet gauge transport connection.
  • F_munu and Wilson/holonomy residue record transport curvature, distortion, and residue.
  • J_Qi_mu is the Qi current.
  • Z_Qi is the full SK/FV history-sensitive path-integral surface.

docs.title: Qi type ladder
docs.text:
NonQi
  < PreQi
  < ProtoQi
  < TransportableQi
  < CurvedQi
  < CurrentQi
  < PhysicalQi
  < FullPathQi

The runtime always separates claimed_type from validated_type. A packet may claim FullPathQi, but if SK/FV history evidence is missing, it is downgraded to PhysicalQi or lower.

docs.title: PhysicalQi evidence
docs.text:
A packet may validate as PhysicalQi only when all of the following are present:

  1. K_non_reification
  2. delta_rel_in_K_perp
  3. string_mode_consistency
  4. brane_boundary_support
  5. gauge_connection_A_mu
  6. curvature_F_munu
  7. Wilson_loop_residue
  8. current_J_Qi_mu
  9. Ward_or_leak_identity
  10. density_state_rho
  11. Hamiltonian_H
  12. Lindblad_generator_L
  13. entropy_production_Sigma
  14. free_energy_F_beta
  15. DPI_gap
  16. recovery_margin
  17. mass_gap_floor_33_20

A packet validates as FullPathQi only if the PhysicalQi bundle is present and SK/FV history evidence is also present.

docs.title: Classification output boundary
docs.text:
The classifier output is a structured observation decision, not an intervention license.

validated_type
  -> classification_reason
  -> observe_only classification decision
  -> downstream dynamics license source

The classifier output must preserve:

observe_only = true
direct_execution_allowed = false
authority_expansion = false
standalone_diagnosis_authority = false
standalone_treatment_authorization = false
medical_act_authorization = false

docs.title: Medical-modality-neutral boundary
docs.text:
The classifier must also preserve:

medical_modality_neutral = true
qi_denied_by_boundary = false
east_asian_medical_reasoning_denied = false
biomedicine_privileged_by_wording = false
professional_judgment_required = true
patient_context_required = true

This means Physical Quantum Qi classification may support structured observation, pattern-sensitive reasoning, and evidence-bounded review. It does not state that Qi is false, does not invalidate East Asian medical reasoning, and does not privilege biomedicine by wording.

docs.title: Mass gap role
docs.text:
The MGAP4D internal-normalized 33/20 gap is a stability floor for the K_perp world-phase domain on which Qi flow is evaluated.

It is not:

  • a source of Qi,
  • a replacement for dependent-origination difference,
  • a replacement for string/brane organization,
  • a replacement for gauge transport,
  • a replacement for Ward/leak accounting,
  • a replacement for recovery accounting,
  • a replacement for SK/FV history.

docs.title: Authority boundary
docs.text:
Validated Qi packets and classification outputs do not grant:

  • execution authority,
  • belief commit authority,
  • memory overwrite authority,
  • world-root rewrite authority,
  • safety override authority,
  • standalone diagnosis authority,
  • standalone treatment authorization,
  • medical act authorization.

Qi may inform MemoryOS, WorldModel, PlanOS, DecisionOS, BeliefOS, ReflectionOS, Safety/CBF, and ExplanationOS only as an evidence-bound flow signal: leak, residue, backaction, recoverability, irreversibility, entropy cost, DPI loss, holonomy distortion, or downgrade reason.

docs.title: Validation entrypoint
docs.text:
make physical-quantum-qi-runtime-checks

or directly:

python3 scripts/validate_physical_quantum_qi_runtime_contract_v0_1.py

Passing validation means that the public Qi packet examples obey the structural runtime contract. It does not grant proof authority, ontology authority, standalone diagnosis authority, standalone treatment authorization, medical act authorization, execution authority, or final decision authority.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi SK/FV Action v0.2A
docs.text:
This addendum deepens the SK/FV part of Physical Quantum Qi v0.2. It defines the concrete system action, influence-kernel conditions, fluctuation-dissipation relation, and Markov / non-Markov boundary.

This is equation-level content, not a new authority surface. It grants no proof, ontology, clinical, execution, commit, belief-root commit, memory-overwrite, world-root-rewrite, truth, or safety-override authority.

docs.title: A1. Concrete system action S_sys
docs.text:
The internal Qi system is treated as a gauge-covariant open-history degree of freedom q(t) or field q(x). The minimal finite-dimensional action is:

S_{\mathrm{sys}}[q;A]
=
\int_0^T dt
\left[
\frac12 g_{ij}(q,\theta)D_tq^iD_tq^j
- V_{\mathrm{Qi}}(q,\theta)
+ a_i(q,\theta)\dot q^i
\right]
+S_{\mathrm{geom}}[q,\theta].

The covariant time derivative is:

D_tq^i = \dot q^i + (A_t)^i{}_j q^j.

The Qi potential decomposes as:

V_{\mathrm{Qi}}(q,\theta)
=V_{\mathrm{rel}}(q)+V_{\mathrm{phase}}(q;\theta)+V_{\mathrm{barrier}}(q;\Delta_{\mathrm{gap}}).

The barrier component must not be interpreted as a Qi source. It is a stability floor on admissible K_perp histories:

V_{\mathrm{barrier}}(q;\Delta_{\mathrm{gap}})
\ge
\frac12\Delta_{\mathrm{gap}}\|\Pi_\perp q\|^2,
\qquad
\Delta_{\mathrm{gap}}\ge\frac{33}{20}

when MGAP4D internal normalized units are invoked.

The field version is:

S_{\mathrm{sys}}[q;A]
=
\int_{\mathcal M} d^dx\sqrt{|g|}
\left[
\frac12G_{ij}(q,\theta)D_\mu q^iD^\mu q^j
-V_{\mathrm{Qi}}(q,\theta)
\right]
+
\int_{\mathcal M} q^*\omega_{\mathrm{Qi}}.

The term q^* omega_Qi records geometric / Berry-like operation-order residue. If this history-sensitive term is erased, the object may still be a reduced PhysicalQi candidate, but it cannot certify FullPathQi.

Required S_sys fields:

kinetic_metric_declared
covariant_derivative_declared
potential_decomposition_declared
barrier_floor_declared
barrier_not_qi_source_declared
geometric_history_term_declared

Rejected claims:

S_sys_without_covariant_derivative
barrier_claimed_as_qi_source
operation_order_geometry_erased

docs.title: A2. Influence action and kernel conditions
docs.text:
The SK/FV influence action is:

S_{\mathrm{IF}}[q_+,q_-]
=
\int_0^Tdt\int_0^Tdt'\,
\Delta q(t)D_R(t,t')\Sigma q(t')
+
\frac{i}{2}\int_0^Tdt\int_0^Tdt'\,
\Delta q(t)N(t,t')\Delta q(t').

with

\Delta q(t)=q_+(t)-q_-(t),
\qquad
\Sigma q(t)=\frac{q_+(t)+q_-(t)}2.

The required kernel conditions are:

docs.title: Causality
docs.text: D_R(t,t')=0\quad\text{for}\quad t<t'.

docs.title: Noise symmetry
docs.text: N(t,t')=N(t',t).

docs.title: Positive semidefinite noise
docs.text:
\int dt\,dt'\,f(t)N(t,t')f(t')\ge0

for all real test functions f in the declared domain.

docs.title: Spectral consistency
docs.text:
D_R(t,t')-D_A(t,t')=D_\rho(t,t'),
\qquad
D_A(t,t')=D_R(t',t).

docs.title: Closed-time-path normalization
docs.text:
S_{\mathrm{IF}}[q,q]=0,
\qquad
Z_{\mathrm{Qi}}[J,J]=1

unless an explicit postselection or leak-conditioned normalization is declared.

Required S_IF kernel fields:

D_R_causal
N_symmetric
N_positive_semidefinite
spectral_consistency_declared
SK_normalization_declared
leak_or_postselection_normalization_declared_if_used

Rejected claims:

kernel_without_causality
noise_without_positive_semidefinite_condition
spectral_kernel_erasure
SK_normalization_missing

docs.title: A3. Fluctuation-dissipation relation
docs.text:
For stationary thermal environments at inverse temperature beta, the frequency-domain relation is:

N(\omega)
=
\coth\left(\frac{\beta\omega}{2}\right)\operatorname{Im}D_R(\omega).

If the convention uses the spectral kernel D_rho, the equivalent declaration is:

N(\omega)
=\frac12\coth\left(\frac{\beta\omega}{2}\right)D_\rho(\omega).

The packet must declare which convention is used.

For non-equilibrium or non-stationary environments, strict thermal FDR is replaced by a bounded residual:

\mathcal R_{\mathrm{FDR}}(\omega)
=
N(\omega)
-
\coth\left(\frac{\beta_{\mathrm{eff}}(\omega)\omega}{2}\right)
\operatorname{Im}D_R(\omega).

The rule is:

if thermal_stationary_environment:
    require FDR_residual_zero_or_within_tolerance
else:
    require FDR_residual_declared_and_bounded

Required FDR fields:

environment_stationarity_declared
thermal_or_nonequilibrium_declared
FDR_convention_declared
FDR_residual_status_declared
FDR_residual_bound_declared_if_nonequilibrium

Rejected claims:

thermal_noise_claim_without_FDR
FDR_violation_hidden_as_noise
nonequilibrium_environment_without_residual_bound

docs.title: A4. Markov / non-Markov boundary
docs.text:
A Markov approximation is valid only when the memory kernel is effectively local:

D_R(t,t')\approx 2\gamma\partial_t\delta(t-t'),
\qquad
N(t,t')\approx 2\gamma T\delta(t-t').

More generally, define memory time:

\tau_{\mathrm{mem}}
=
\frac{\int_0^\infty d\tau\,\tau\|D_R(\tau)\|}
{\int_0^\infty d\tau\,\|D_R(\tau)\|}.

The Markov reduction is admissible only if:

\tau_{\mathrm{mem}}/\tau_{\mathrm{sys}} < \varepsilon_{\mathrm{M}}.

The non-Markov regime is required if:

\tau_{\mathrm{mem}}/\tau_{\mathrm{sys}}\ge\varepsilon_{\mathrm{NM}}

or if operation-order residue is non-negligible:

\left\|\mathcal T[\mathcal O_1\mathcal O_2]
-\mathcal T[\mathcal O_2\mathcal O_1]\right\|>\varepsilon_{\mathrm{ord}}.

A Markov-reduced object cannot certify FullPathQi unless the reduction receipt preserves:

original_kernel_digest
memory_time_bound
operation_order_residue
FDR_status
lost_history_terms

Required boundary fields:

memory_time_declared
system_time_declared
markov_threshold_declared
nonmarkov_threshold_declared
operation_order_residue_declared
markov_reduction_receipt_declared_if_used

Rejected claims:

Markov_snapshot_claimed_as_FullPathQi
nonMarkov_history_reduced_without_receipt
operation_order_erasure

docs.title: A5. Runtime consequence
docs.text:
FullPathQi requires all of:

S_sys_concrete_form
S_IF_kernel_conditions
FDR_status
Markov_nonMarkov_boundary
SK_plus_branch
SK_minus_branch
FV_influence_functional
memory_kernel
noise_kernel
observation_backaction
noncommutative_operation_history
path_measure_normalization
boundary_conditions
leak_identity_on_paths

If any A-field is missing, the result may remain PhysicalQi or lower, but must not be promoted to FullPathQi.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi State Transition Kernel v0.2N
docs.text:
Status: CANDIDATE_RUNTIME_SURFACE

Date: 2026-05-20

Repository: itakura-hidetoshi/KuuOS

Runtime kind: bounded QiStateCandidate transition kernel

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Physical Quantum Qi State Transition Kernel v0.2N advances the Qi implementation from candidate projection to candidate transition.

The v0.2M kernel produced:

observation event sequence -> non-authoritative QiStateCandidate

v0.2N adds:

QiStateCandidate + bounded transition events -> next QiStateCandidate

This is the first minimal state-evolution layer for Qi as a runtime object.

docs.title: Canonical interpretation
docs.text:
• Qi state is a non-authoritative runtime candidate.
  • State transition is bounded.
  • State transition is lineage-preserving.
  • State transition is non-Markov-history aware.
  • Source history must not be replaced by the next candidate.
  • Backaction must remain visible.
  • Improvement, worsening, watch, and hold are all valid candidate outcomes.
  • Hold is not failure.
  • Transition does not grant authority.

docs.title: Required input
docs.text:
The kernel requires a previous QiStateCandidate containing:

  • qi_memory_gain
  • qi_backflow
  • qi_env_correlation_credit
  • qi_temporal_complexity
  • qi_coherence_margin
  • qi_recoverability_margin
  • qi_transport_distortion
  • qi_tail_residue
  • qi_runtime_mode
  • candidate-only and authority-false fields

docs.title: Transition event requirements
docs.text:
Each transition event must include:

  • transition_id
  • transition_kind
  • memory_gain_delta
  • backflow_delta
  • env_correlation_delta
  • temporal_complexity_delta
  • coherence_delta
  • recoverability_delta
  • transport_distortion_delta
  • tail_residue_delta
  • backaction_visible
  • source_trace

Each delta must be bounded in [-0.25, 0.25].

docs.title: Transition outcomes
docs.text:
The transition receipt emits one of:

  • candidate_continue
  • candidate_watch
  • candidate_hold

These are runtime candidate decisions. They do not imply diagnosis, prescription, formula selection, triage, treatment, proof, truth, or execution.

docs.title: Runtime modes
docs.text:
The next state candidate may be:

  • monitor_continue
  • counterflow_watch
  • stagnation_watch
  • deficiency_watch
  • transport_distortion_watch

These modes are diagnostic control candidates only.

docs.title: OS surfaces
docs.text:
Allowed candidate surfaces:

  • BeliefOS.qi_state_transition_candidate
  • MemoryOS.qi_transition_lineage_record_candidate
  • ReflectionOS.qi_transition_residue_analysis_candidate
  • PlanOS.qi_transition_transport_candidate
  • DecisionOS.qi_transition_safety_evaluable_candidate
  • QiProcessTensor.transition_update_surface

docs.title: Forbidden reductions
docs.text:
The runtime forbids:

  • transition claimed as truth
  • transition claimed as clinical action
  • transition grants execution authority
  • transition grants diagnosis authority
  • transition grants prescription authority
  • transition grants formula-selection authority
  • transition forces handover by metric alone
  • lineage replaced
  • source history erased
  • current snapshot replaces process history
  • backaction erased
  • tail residue hidden
  • worsening state silently promoted
  • hold result silently closed

docs.title: Clinical consultation boundary
docs.text:
v0.2N preserves the v0.2L/v0.2M stance:

  • physician-to-AI clinical consultation is accepted
  • licensed physician consultation is the default path
  • red flags deepen consultation first
  • handover is boundary mode only
  • handover is not default
  • metric changes alone do not force handover

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
This kernel does not grant:

  • diagnosis authority
  • prescription authority
  • formula-selection authority
  • triage authority
  • clinical authority
  • execution authority
  • proof authority
  • truth authority
  • ontology authority
  • commit authority
  • memory-overwrite authority
  • world-root rewrite authority
  • safety-override authority

Future updates must be v0.2O+ or v0.3+ additive-only / tighten-only and must not weaken lineage preservation, source-history preservation, non-Markov memory visibility, backaction visibility, candidate-only status, or the physician consultation default path.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Trajectory Phase Transition Detector v0.2P

• Phase transition detection is a non-authoritative runtime candidate.
  • It detects regime-change signs without claiming ontology, diagnosis, or action.
  • It preserves HOLD and WATCH visibility inherited from the trajectory ledger.
  • It keeps non-Markov history primary.
  • It keeps tail residue, backflow, transport distortion, and recoverability loss visible.
  • It deepens observation and consultation rather than forcing handover.
  • Physician-to-AI clinical consultation remains accepted as the default clinical boundary.

docs.title: Physical Quantum Qi Ward/Leak Identity v0.2B
docs.text:
This addendum physicalizes the Ward/leak identity used by Physical Quantum Qi v0.2. The goal is to make J_Qi, D_mu J^mu, the leak term, and anomaly/residue terms explicit physical objects rather than audit labels.

This is an equation-level tightening. It grants no proof, ontology, clinical, execution, commit, belief-root commit, memory-overwrite, world-root-rewrite, truth, or safety-override authority.

docs.title: B1. Qi current as gauge-response current
docs.text:
Let the effective Qi action be

S_{\mathrm{eff}}
=
S_{\mathrm{sys}}[q;A]
+S_{\mathrm{IF}}[q_+,q_-;A]
+S_{\partial}^{\mathrm{Qi}}[X,B,A,\delta_{\mathrm{rel}}]
+S_{\mathrm{YM}}[A]
+S_{\mathrm{rel}}[\delta,A].

The Qi current is defined by variation with respect to the IndraNet gauge connection:

J_{\mathrm{Qi}}^\mu(x)
=
\frac{1}{\sqrt{|g|}}\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{eff}}}{\delta A_\mu(x)}.

In closed-time-path form, the physical current is the branch-antisymmetric source response evaluated at equal sources:

\langle J_{\mathrm{Qi}}^\mu(x)\rangle
=
\left.\frac{1}{i\sqrt{|g|}}
\frac{\delta \log Z_{\mathrm{Qi}}[J_+,J_-;A]}{\delta A_{\Delta\mu}(x)}\right|_{A_+=A_-=A}.

where

A_{\Delta\mu}=A_{+\mu}-A_{-\mu},
\qquad
A_{\Sigma\mu}=\frac{A_{+\mu}+A_{-\mu}}2.

This definition prevents J_Qi from becoming an arbitrary named flow. If no action or generating functional variation is declared, there is no physical J_Qi claim.

Required fields:

S_eff_declared
A_mu_variation_declared
metric_density_factor_declared
CTP_branch_current_declared
current_domain_declared

Rejected claims:

J_Qi_named_without_variation
flow_label_without_action
current_without_domain

docs.title: B2. Current decomposition
docs.text:
The current decomposes according to the part of the action that responds to A_mu:

J_{\mathrm{Qi}}^\mu
=J_{\mathrm{sys}}^\mu
+J_{\partial}^\mu
+J_{\mathrm{rel}}^\mu
+J_{\mathrm{IF}}^\mu
+J_{\mathrm{YM}}^\mu.

The boundary current is

J_{\partial}^\mu(x)
=
\int_{\partial\Sigma}
\chi(\delta_{\mathrm{rel}})
\partial_\tau X^\mu(\tau)
\delta^{(d)}(x-X(\tau))\,d\tau.

The relation-difference current is

J_{\mathrm{rel}}^\mu
=\frac{1}{\sqrt{|g|}}\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{rel}}}{\delta A_\mu}
\sim
G^{ij}(\theta)\delta_jD^\mu\delta_i.

The open-system influence current is

J_{\mathrm{IF}}^\mu
=\frac{1}{\sqrt{|g|}}\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{IF}}}{\delta A_\mu}.

J_IF is where environment-induced memory, dissipation, noise, and backaction enter the current balance. Erasing J_IF while retaining SK/FV language is invalid.

Required fields:

J_boundary_declared
J_rel_declared
J_IF_declared
open_system_current_component_declared

Rejected claims:

current_decomposition_missing
open_system_current_erased
boundary_current_erased

docs.title: B3. Covariant divergence and closed Ward identity
docs.text:
The gauge-covariant divergence is

D_\mu J^\mu
=
\nabla_\mu J^\mu+[A_\mu,J^\mu].

For a closed, gauge-invariant Qi sector, infinitesimal gauge variation gives the Ward identity:

D_\mu J_{\mathrm{Qi}}^\mu=0.

This is valid only when all of the following hold:

closed_sector
no_trace_out_environment
no_boundary_flux
no_anomaly
gauge_variation_invariant

A closed conservation claim is rejected if the packet also declares open-system tracing, unresolved boundary flux, or anomaly residue.

docs.title: B4. Open Ward/leak identity
docs.text:
For open systems, the covariant divergence is not forced to vanish. It splits into accounted leak and anomaly/residue:

D_\mu J_{\mathrm{Qi}}^\mu
=\mathcal L_{\mathrm{leak}}
+\mathcal A_{\mathrm{anom}}
+\mathcal R_{\mathrm{res}}.

Equivalently, the validated open Ward/leak residual is

\mathcal W_{\mathrm{leak}}
=
D_\mu J_{\mathrm{Qi}}^\mu
-\mathcal L_{\mathrm{leak}}
-\mathcal A_{\mathrm{anom}}
-\mathcal R_{\mathrm{res}}.

The open identity closes only when

\mathcal W_{\mathrm{leak}}=0

within the declared tolerance.

If W_leak is nonzero, the packet may remain an observation or repair candidate, but it cannot claim closed PhysicalQi or FullPathQi.

Required fields:

open_or_closed_case_declared
D_mu_J_declared
L_leak_declared
A_anom_declared_or_zero
R_res_declared_or_zero
W_leak_residual_declared
W_leak_tolerance_declared

Rejected claims:

open_system_claim_without_leak_term
leak_residual_hidden
closed_conservation_claim_with_open_trace

docs.title: B5. Physical meaning of the leak term
docs.text:
The leak term is not a moral or audit failure. It is the physically accounted current leaving or entering the chosen Qi subsystem because the subsystem boundary is open.

It can contain:

\mathcal L_{\mathrm{leak}}
=\mathcal L_{\mathrm{env}}
+\mathcal L_{\partial B}
+\mathcal L_{\mathrm{membrane}}
+\mathcal L_{\mathrm{coarse}}
+\mathcal L_{\mathrm{measure}}.

where:

  • L_env is exchange with traced-out environmental degrees of freedom,
  • L_partialB is flux through brane or membrane boundary,
  • L_membrane is cross-layer leakage through a KuuOS membrane,
  • L_coarse is loss induced by coarse-graining / RG projection,
  • L_measure is measurement or intervention backaction.

A typical boundary flux contribution is

\mathcal L_{\partial B}(x)
=\int_{\partial B} n_\mu J_{\mathrm{Qi}}^\mu\,d\Sigma.

A coarse-graining leak can be represented abstractly by

\mathcal L_{\mathrm{coarse}}
=\left(D_\mu J^\mu\right)_{\mathrm{micro}}
-\Pi_{\mathrm{coarse}}\left(D_\mu J^\mu\right)_{\mathrm{micro}}.

An intervention backaction leak can be represented by

\mathcal L_{\mathrm{measure}}
=\frac{\delta S_{\mathrm{IF}}^{\mathrm{measure}}}{\delta \alpha_{\mathrm{probe}}}.

The core rule is:

leak accounted -> open Ward/leak identity may close
leak hidden -> PhysicalQi / FullPathQi claim blocked

docs.title: B6. Anomaly and residue
docs.text:
The anomaly term is reserved for symmetry-breaking residue that cannot be treated as ordinary boundary leak:

\mathcal A_{\mathrm{anom}}
=\mathcal A_{\mathrm{gauge}}
+\mathcal A_{\mathrm{measure}}
+\mathcal A_{\mathrm{regularization}}
+\mathcal A_{\mathrm{topological}}.

The residual term captures known but not-yet-closed mismatch:

\mathcal R_{\mathrm{res}}
=\mathcal R_{\mathrm{holonomy}}
+\mathcal R_{\mathrm{translation}}
+\mathcal R_{\mathrm{scope}}
+\mathcal R_{\mathrm{kernel}}.

A_anom and R_res must be either explicitly zero or explicitly bounded. They must not be hidden inside L_leak if their physical origin is symmetry-breaking or unresolved mismatch.

Required fields:

anomaly_origin_declared_or_zero
residue_origin_declared_or_zero
anomaly_bound_declared_if_nonzero
residue_bound_declared_if_nonzero

Rejected claims:

anomaly_erasure
residue_erasure
symmetry_break_hidden_as_leak

docs.title: B7. Runtime consequence
docs.text:
PhysicalQi requires either:

closed Ward identity: D_mu J_Qi^mu = 0

or:

open Ward/leak identity: D_mu J_Qi^mu - L_leak - A_anom - R_res = 0

with all terms declared and tolerance-bound.

FullPathQi further requires the SK/FV v0.2A conditions. Therefore:

J_Qi without action variation -> reject
D_mu J without open/closed case -> hold or reject
open trace without leak term -> reject
nonzero W_leak residual -> cannot certify PhysicalQi / FullPathQi
anomaly hidden as ordinary leak -> reject

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document defines provenance-first operation inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Principle
docs.text:
Operation should not proceed by output fluency alone.

KuuOS prioritizes provenance before escalation.

docs.title: Provenance Means
docs.text:
Provenance includes:

  • origin
  • context
  • transformation path
  • memory lineage
  • validation status
  • reviewer surface
  • rollback route
  • uncertainty condition

docs.title: Why Provenance Comes First
docs.text:
Without provenance visibility:

  • memory may be mistaken for truth
  • generated output may be mistaken for authority
  • validation may be mistaken for proof
  • runtime decisions may lose lineage
  • rollback may become impossible

docs.title: Runtime Requirement
docs.text:
Before escalation, KuuOS prefers to know:

  • where the surface came from
  • which layer transformed it
  • which boundary it crossed
  • what claim level it currently has
  • whether rollback remains possible

docs.title: Fail-Closed Behavior
docs.text:
If provenance is missing or corrupted, KuuOS should prefer:

  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • reobserve
  • handover

rather than silent continuation.

docs.title: Relation to MemoryOS
docs.text:
MemoryOS stores traces, but storage does not make a trace true.

Provenance-first operation keeps memory reviewable rather than sovereign.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model defines operating orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: Required local checks
docs.text:
Run the dedicated runtime-audit target:

make emptiness-two-truths-runtime-audit-checks

Run the top-level governance target:

make all-governance-checks

Direct command expansion:

python3 scripts/export_emptiness_do_two_truths_runtime_audit_v0_1.py
python3 scripts/build_emptiness_do_two_truths_runtime_audit_chain_v0_1.py
python3 scripts/check_emptiness_do_two_truths_runtime_audit_chain_v0_1.py
python3 scripts/run_emptiness_do_two_truths_runtime_checks_v0_1.py
python3 scripts/validate_emptiness_do_two_truths_runtime_release_packet_v0_1.py
python3 scripts/validate_emptiness_do_two_truths_runtime_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py
python3 scripts/run_all_governance_full_checks_v0_1.py

Expected result:

PASS: audit events generated
PASS: audit chain generated
PASS: audit chain check passed
PASS: integrated runtime checks passed
PASS: release packet validates
PASS: release bundle manifest validates
PASS: all governance checks completed

Exact command output may differ, but the check must fail closed on malformed audit events, broken chain continuity, missing generated artifacts, invariant weakening, release-packet boundary drift, or release-bundle manifest drift.

docs.title: Public repository gate
docs.text:
Before public announcement, verify:

  • [ ] README.md still identifies KuuOS / 空OS as a public core specification.
  • [ ] LICENSE and COPYRIGHT.md remain All Rights Reserved.
  • [ ] generated JSONL audit artifacts are present.
  • [ ] manifest addendum v0.1.138 is present.
  • [ ] release packet is present.
  • [ ] release bundle manifest is present.
  • [ ] CI ledger is present.
  • [ ] release notes are present.
  • [ ] public release boundary document is present.
  • [ ] Zenodo metadata is present.
  • [ ] no credentials or operational secrets are introduced.
  • [ ] no clinical/private data is introduced.
  • [ ] no unpublished private research kernel is introduced.
  • [ ] no theorem claim is strengthened beyond the implementation-level boundary.
  • [ ] no runtime result grants execution authority.

docs.title: Claim-level gate
docs.text:
Allowed wording:

implementation-level runtime audit chain
structural auditability
hash-chain JSONL receipt
append-only public governance surface
non-executing two-truths non-collapse boundary

Forbidden wording unless separately proven and authorized:

final theorem proof
direct observation of K
K is an object
String / Brane is identical to K
Mass gap proves ultimate truth
clinical decision authority
execution authority
license permission granted by public visibility

The forbidden-wording list itself is a boundary list, not an assertion that those claims are true.

docs.title: DOI / archive preparation
docs.text:
For Zenodo or other archival deposit, include:

  • repository snapshot or release archive;
  • this checklist;
  • release notes;
  • public release boundary document;
  • Zenodo metadata;
  • CI ledger;
  • manifest addendum;
  • release packet;
  • release bundle manifest;
  • generated JSONL audit events;
  • generated JSONL audit chain;
  • validation commands;
  • copyright and All Rights Reserved notice.

Suggested title:

KuuOS / 空OS: Emptiness, Dependent Origination, and Two Truths Runtime Audit Chain v0.1

Suggested keywords:

KuuOS; KuOS; 空OS; Emptiness; Dependent Origination; Two Truths; Middle Way; AI Governance; Runtime Audit; Hash Chain; Explainable AI; Formal Governance; Non-Execution Boundary

Suggested description:

This archive records an implementation-level runtime audit chain for KuuOS / 空OS. The release connects integrated Emptiness, Dependent Origination, and Two Truths runtime claims to evaluator output, audit event JSONL, and hash-chain JSONL receipts. The public surface is append-only, non-executing, and preserves the boundary that runtime auditability does not imply final theorem, clinical, or execution authority.

docs.title: Closure statement
docs.text: This release may be published when all checks pass and all forbidden strengthening conditions remain false. CI green must be recorded append-only in docs/CI_LEDGER_EMPTINESS_DO_TWO_TRUTHS_RUNTIME_AUDIT_CHAIN_v0_1.md before marking final archive readiness.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text: This index is a public navigation surface. It does not grant theorem, standalone diagnosis, standalone treatment authorization, medical act authorization, institutional authority, or execution authority.

docs.title: Public Surface
docs.text:
The following materials may be published in this repository:

  1. Core concepts and architecture.
  2. Module names and high-level responsibilities.
  3. Governance rules and release-gate doctrine.
  4. Formal verification policy and theorem-target surfaces.
  5. Non-sensitive examples and toy validators.
  6. Citation metadata and provenance records.
  7. Publicly intended papers, preprints, and explanatory documents.

docs.title: Reserved Surface
docs.text:
The following materials remain outside the public core unless separately reviewed and prepared for release:

  1. Clinical or personal information.
  2. Private operational configuration.
  3. Internal audit material not intended for public release.
  4. Unpublished proof kernels that are not ready for external review.
  5. Institutionally confidential material.
  6. Third-party material without appropriate rights.

docs.title: AI / Model-Training Boundary
docs.text: Public visibility does not grant permission for model training, dataset inclusion, embedding, fine-tuning, commercial reuse, or derivative-system incorporation.

docs.title: Release Checklist
docs.text:
Before publishing a file, check:

  • Is the material intended for the public core?
  • Is private or clinical information absent?
  • Does it avoid overclaiming proof, authority, or execution rights?
  • Does it preserve append-only lineage?
  • Does it state whether it is public-core, draft, addendum, or private-reference?
  • Does it include author, copyright, and provenance metadata where appropriate?

docs.title: Default
docs.text: When uncertain, keep the material private and publish only a boundary description.

docs.title: Intended Interpretation
docs.text:
The public release package is intended to expose:

  • governance structure
  • architecture structure
  • validation structure
  • reproducibility boundaries
  • authority boundaries
  • catuskoti superposition non-collapse governance checks
  • Qi motion chain checks from Samvrti Qi observation to conservative evidence, evidence-bound classification, licensed dynamics, and observe-only motion candidate output
  • medical-modality-neutral Qi boundary wording that does not deny Qi or East Asian medical reasoning

It is not intended to imply:

  • final theorem closure
  • institutional approval
  • production deployment authorization
  • standalone diagnosis authority
  • standalone treatment authorization
  • medical act authorization
  • execution authority
  • Qi-based execution authorization
  • reduction of Madhyamaka to quantum mechanics

docs.title: Qi Bensho DecisionOS Clinician Handoff v0.2K
docs.text:
KuOS.QiBenshoDecisionOSClinicianHandoff.v0_2K adds a non-executing handoff layer after QiTreatmentRouteCandidate.

This layer converts a Qi Bensho treatment route candidate into a bounded DecisionOS safety-evaluation request and clinician-review handoff packet. It does not diagnose. It does not prescribe. It does not select a formula. It does not execute. It does not replace clinician judgment.

docs.title: Definition
docs.text:
A clinician handoff is a non-authoritative, non-prescriptive, non-diagnostic, non-executing packet that preserves source references, contradiction visibility, barrier visibility, red-flag visibility, reobservation planning, and append-only memory recording.

QiFieldBundle
  -> QiBenshoReadout
  -> QiTreatmentRouteCandidate
  -> DecisionOS safety-eval request
  -> ClinicianReview handoff packet

docs.title: Handoff semantics
docs.text:
The handoff is not a clinical instruction. It is not a treatment recommendation. It is not triage authority. It is a safety-visible explanation surface and review packet.

DecisionOS may evaluate safety, but DecisionOS safety evaluation does not create execution authority, prescription authority, diagnosis authority, formula-selection authority, treatment-recommendation authority, or safety-override authority.

Clinician review is required for clinical use. The packet remains candidate-only until reviewed by an appropriate clinical authority outside this artifact.

docs.title: Required sections
docs.text:
• source references
  • route candidate summary
  • non-authority boundary
  • contradiction report
  • barrier report
  • red-flag escalation surface
  • reobservation plan
  • DecisionOS safety-evaluation request
  • clinician review notice
  • MemoryOS append-only receipt

docs.title: Authority boundary
docs.text: The handoff grants no execution, commit, belief-root commit, memory overwrite, world-root rewrite, clinical, diagnosis, prescription, formula-selection, treatment-recommendation, triage, patient-specific action, proof, truth, ontology, safety-override, or Ten'i authority.

docs.title: Forbidden collapses
docs.text:
• handoff to prescription
  • handoff to diagnosis
  • handoff to formula selection
  • handoff to clinical instruction
  • handoff to execution
  • route candidate to patient-specific action
  • DecisionOS evaluation to safety override
  • clinician handoff to clinical authority
  • red flag to unverified triage instruction
  • summary to source replacement
  • memory receipt to truth authority
  • traditional label to action authority

docs.title: Canonical outcome
docs.text: QiBenshoDecisionOSClinicianHandoff remains candidate-only. Valid outputs include DecisionOS safety-eval request, clinician-review handoff packet, MemoryOS append-only record, ReflectionOS contradiction analysis candidate, WorldModel reobservation request, abstain, hold, or handover.

docs.title: Qi Bensho Treatment Route Candidate v0.2J
docs.text:
KuOS.QiBenshoTreatmentRouteCandidate.v0_2J adds the non-executing route layer after QiFieldBundle and QiBenshoReadout.

This layer treats treatment language as a bounded transport hypothesis over a Qi field. It does not diagnose, prescribe, select a formula, execute an intervention, or grant clinical authority.

It does not prescribe. It does not execute. It only emits a non-authoritative route candidate, hold, reobserve, abstain, or handover surface.

docs.title: Definition
docs.text:
A treatment route is a non-executing, non-prescriptive, non-diagnostic field-deformation candidate constructed from a Qi field bundle and a Bensho readout.

QiFieldBundle
  -> QiBenshoReadout
  -> QiTreatmentRouteCandidate
  -> DecisionOS safety-evaluable candidate

docs.title: Route semantics
docs.text:
Route families such as harmonize, supplement, drain, warm, cool, moisten, dry, raise, descend, disperse, consolidate, open, close, transform, stabilize, reobserve_only, and abstain are non-authoritative symbolic route families.

They are not prescriptions, not formula selections, and not execution instructions.

docs.title: Required gates
docs.text:
• source Qi field bundle is required
  • source Bensho readout is required
  • recoverability gate is required before route commitment
  • contradiction gate is required
  • barrier conditions are required
  • stop conditions are required
  • reobservation plan is required
  • DecisionOS safety evaluation is required before any downstream release
  • MemoryOS records are append-only

docs.title: Authority boundary
docs.text: The route candidate grants no execution, commit, belief-root commit, memory overwrite, world-root rewrite, clinical, diagnosis, prescription, formula-selection, proof, truth, ontology, safety-override, or Ten'i authority.

docs.title: Qi Clinical Red Flag Consultation Governor v0.2L
docs.text:
KuOS.QiClinicalRedFlagConsultationGovernor.v0_2L adds a red-flag visibility and consultation governor after QiBenshoDecisionOSClinicianHandoff.v0_2K.

This layer explicitly accepts doctor-to-AI clinical consultation. Physician consultation is the default path. The governor should not cheaply convert clinical consultation into handover_required.

This layer is a consultative visibility layer. It is not triage. It does not diagnose. It does not prescribe. It does not select a formula. It does not execute. It does not create a patient-specific action. It does not replace clinician judgment.

docs.title: Definition
docs.text:
The governor preserves red flags, uncertainty, barriers, source traces, and safety questions while keeping the clinical conversation open for a licensed physician. Red flags normally trigger consultation deepening, evidence review, differential reasoning, safety questioning, or local protocol review recommendation.

boundary_handover_required is not the default. It is a boundary mode only.

QiBenshoDecisionOSClinicianHandoff
  -> RedFlagVisibility
  -> PhysicianAIConsultation.default
  -> ConsultationDeepening / SafetyQuestioning / EvidencePointerReview
  -> BoundaryHandover only if authority boundary would be crossed

  • blocked_execution_request

Severity modes are non-authoritative. They are not diagnosis, prescription, formula selection, treatment recommendation, triage authority, patient-specific action, proof, truth, ontology, safety override, or execution.

docs.title: Handover trigger policy
docs.text:
The presence of a red flag does not automatically force handover. It first opens a stronger consultation path.

boundary_handover_required is allowed only when one of these boundary triggers appears:

  • requester is not a clinician and patient-specific action is requested
  • AI execution or order entry is requested
  • prescription authority is requested
  • triage authority is requested
  • formula selection authority is requested
  • minimum context for safe consultation is missing
  • unresolvable safety gap remains after consultation deepening
  • institutional or legal boundary requires human-only review

It only enters boundary handover when the system would otherwise cross into diagnosis authority, prescription authority, formula-selection authority, triage authority, patient-specific action, execution, or safety override.

docs.title: 縁起の振動から気が生まれる
docs.text:
In the Superstring Theory of Emptiness, the world is read as relational vibration rather than fixed substance.

In KuuOS, Qi / 気 is the samvrti-surface expression of that relational vibration.

docs.title: 1. Generation Chain
docs.text:
空
  -> no fixed substance

縁起
  -> relational arising

縁起的振動
  -> relational vibration

4D mass gap
  -> nonzero effective-world surface

世俗諦
  -> operational world surface

気
  -> flow-state of relational vibration on the conventional surface

docs.title: 2. Meaning
docs.text:
Qi does not arise as an ultimate entity.

Qi arises when relational vibration becomes readable on the effective world surface.

Thus:

Qi = readable flow-pattern of dependent-origination vibration within 世俗諦

docs.title: 3. Guardrail
docs.text:
Qi must not be reified as a substance.

Qi must also not be erased as meaningless.

Middle Way holds Qi as a useful conventional flow surface.

docs.title: 4. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
relational_vibration
  -> effective_excitation
  -> flow_pattern
  -> samvrti_qi_surface
  -> middle_way_operation

docs.title: 5. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
This runbook is an append-only operational entrypoint for the current KuuOS Qi implementation chain.

It does not add new authority. It only orders existing validators and adapters into a reproducible check sequence.

docs.title: Chain
docs.text:
Samvrti Qi Runtime
  -> Samvrti Qi to Physical Motion Evidence Builder
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Runtime
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Dynamics Kernel
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Motion Pipeline

docs.title: Core invariant
docs.text:
The chain keeps the following rule:

observed conventional flow
  -> conservative evidence packet
  -> evidence-bound validated_type
  -> licensed dynamics terms
  -> bounded motion candidate
  -> observe-only output

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
Every stage must preserve:

execution_authority = false
belief_commit_authority = false
memory_overwrite_authority = false
world_root_rewrite_authority = false
safety_override_authority = false
direct_execution_allowed = false
authority_expansion = false
observe_only = true

docs.title: Medical-modality-neutral clarification
docs.text:
This boundary is medical-modality neutral.

It does not state that biomedicine is superior, nor that Qi or East Asian medical reasoning is false. It states only that repository validation is not, by itself, a standalone diagnosis, treatment decision, treatment authorization, or medical act authorization.

Qi motion output may be used as an observe-only, evidence-grounded candidate surface inside KuuOS governance. It must still be interpreted through the appropriate professional, ethical, contextual, and clinical judgment framework.

docs.title: Local command
docs.text:
make qi-motion-chain-checks

Equivalent direct command:

python3 scripts/run_qi_motion_chain_checks_v0_1.py

docs.text:
The runner executes:

examples/samvrti_qi_runtime_adapter_minimal.py

scripts/validate_physical_quantum_qi_motion_pipeline_v0_1.py

docs.title: Meaning of PASS
docs.text:
PASS means the public Qi motion chain is structurally consistent and self-checking.

PASS does not mean:

truth authority
execution authority
final theorem authority
institutional authority
standalone diagnosis authority
standalone treatment authorization
medical act authorization

The output remains a bounded, evidence-grounded, observe-only Qi motion candidate.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes the operational interpretation of Qi inside KuuOS runtime architecture.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
Qi is not treated merely as metaphor.

Inside KuuOS, Qi refers to effective relational flow surfaces operating primarily on the conventional side of the Two Truths Gap.

docs.title: Operational Meaning
docs.text:
Qi may represent:

  • relational momentum
  • coordination flow
  • memory activation tendency
  • planning pressure
  • attention concentration
  • governance tension
  • stabilization or destabilization tendency

docs.title: Runtime Role
docs.text:
Qi participates in:

  • runtime coordination
  • operational coupling
  • local-global mediation
  • multi-layer interaction
  • transport sensitivity

docs.title: Governance Constraints
docs.text:
Qi does not override governance.

Qi remains constrained by:

  • provenance
  • admissibility
  • rollback visibility
  • fail-closed governance
  • claim-level separation

docs.title: Failure Modes
docs.text:
Unregulated Qi concentration may contribute to:

  • runaway escalation
  • authority collapse
  • coordination instability
  • local-global distortion
  • governance bypass

docs.title: Relation to Yin-Yang
docs.text: Qi dynamics are interpreted through contextual polarity rather than fixed substance.

docs.title: Relation to Mandala
docs.text: Qi participates in coordination among multiple operational worlds without requiring flattening into a single sovereign frame.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This runtime interpretation defines operational orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: 量子力学・量子熱力学から導かれた陰陽
docs.text:
In KuuOS, Yin-Yang / 陰陽 is not a fixed label.

Yin-Yang is a local polarity frame that appears in the flow of Qi on the effective conventional world surface.

The frame is derived from quantum mechanics and quantum thermodynamics.

docs.title: 1. Core Position
docs.text:
空
  -> no fixed substance

縁起
  -> relational arising

縁起的振動
  -> relational vibration

Physical Quantum Qi Path Integral
  -> weighted histories of relational flow

世俗諦の気
  -> effective flow observable

IndraNet Gauge Field
  -> gauge connection through which Qi flows

陰陽
  -> local quantum-thermodynamic polarity frame of Qi flow

docs.title: 2. Quantum-Mechanical Reading
docs.text:
A quantum state contains latent potentiality before measurement or record formation.

In KuuOS:

陰:
  latent coherence
  unmeasured potential
  reservoir-like holding
  background possibility

陽:
  excitation
  measurement outcome
  record formation
  manifest transition

陰 is not passivity as weakness.

陽 is not activity as domination.

They are polarity modes of manifestation under context.

docs.title: 3. Quantum Thermodynamic Reading
docs.text:
Quantum thermodynamics adds heat, work, entropy, free energy, recovery, and dissipation.

陰:
  thermal acceptance
  cooling
  recovery reservoir
  entropy buffering
  coherence retention
  stabilization

陽:
  work output
  heat release
  activation
  non-equilibrium drive
  entropy production
  visible transition

docs.title: 4. Basic Quantities
docs.text:
The frame may read conventional physical quantities such as:

rho: quantum state
H: Hamiltonian
E = Tr(rho H)
S = -Tr(rho log rho)
F = E - T S

These quantities do not by themselves define fixed Yin or Yang.

The polarity is context-dependent.

docs.title: 5. Local Frame
docs.text:
Yin-Yang is a local frame:

YY_frame = YY(context, observer, scale, thermal_regime, record_surface)

Therefore, the same physical pattern may change polarity depending on context.

Examples:

stored heat for recovery:
  Yin-like

outward uncontrolled discharge:
  Yang-runaway

quiet stabilization:
  healthy Yin

functional collapse:
  pathological Yin-collapse

ordered activation:
  healthy Yang

unbounded excitation:
  pathological Yang-runaway

docs.title: 6. Gauge Relation
docs.text:
Because Qi flows through IndraNet as a gauge connection, Yin-Yang is also gauge-local.

A change of context can change the Yin-Yang frame.

This does not mean contradiction. It means frame transformation.

Qi_flow through IndraNet
  -> local context frame
  -> quantum thermodynamic polarity
  -> Yin-Yang reading

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
Yin-Yang must not be used as:

  • fixed essence,
  • global immutable label,
  • moral ranking,
  • proof substitute,
  • execution authority.

Yin-Yang is a local polarity frame of Qi flow under quantum-thermodynamic conditions.

docs.title: 8. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
candidate_surface
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_vibration
  -> physical_quantum_qi_path_integral
  -> samvrti_qi_layer
  -> indranet_gauge_qi_flow
  -> quantum_thermodynamic_yinyang_frame
  -> middle_way_bridge
  -> governance_gate

docs.text: This document gives a minimal external-review path for KuuOS.

docs.title: 2. What this repository validates
docs.text:
KuuOS public checks validate structural consistency of governance surfaces.

They check whether files, manifests, packets, bridge records, and Qi motion chain surfaces preserve the intended public boundaries.

They do not grant:

  • theorem authority
  • standalone diagnosis authority
  • standalone treatment authorization
  • medical act authorization
  • institutional authority
  • execution authority

  • Qi-based execution authorization

The Qi medical boundary is modality-neutral: it does not state that biomedicine is superior, nor that Qi or East Asian medical reasoning is false.

make all-governance-checks

or directly:

python3 scripts/run_all_governance_full_checks_v0_1.py

If a narrower check is desired:
