  4. docs/MEDICAL_MODALITY_NEUTRAL_QI_BOUNDARY_v0_1.md
  5. docs/ARCHITECTURE_OVERVIEW_v0_1.md
  6. docs/ALL_GOVERNANCE_CHECKS_RUNBOOK_v0_1.md
  7. docs/QI_MOTION_CHAIN_RUNBOOK_v0_1.md
  8. Makefile
  9. scripts/run_all_governance_full_checks_v0_1.py
  10. scripts/run_qi_motion_chain_checks_v0_1.py
  11. specs/kuos_core_manifest_v0_1.yaml

docs.title: 5. What can be externally reproduced now
docs.text:
External reviewers can reproduce:

  • the public governance check invocation surface
  • structural validation of declared governance artifacts
  • public non-authority boundary assertions
  • manifest and packet consistency checks exposed in the repository
  • Qi motion chain checks from Samvrti Qi observation to conservative evidence, evidence-bound classification, licensed dynamics, and observe-only motion candidate output
  • the medical-modality-neutral Qi boundary wording

External reviewers should not treat this repository alone as reproducing:

  • final theorem proof closure
  • standalone diagnosis validity
  • standalone treatment authorization
  • medical act authorization
  • deployment readiness
  • model-level transformation of any AI provider
  • Qi-based execution authorization

docs.title: 6. Expected reviewer stance
docs.text:
A reviewer should evaluate whether KuuOS consistently preserves its own boundaries:

  • raw AI output remains candidate only
  • memory does not become sovereign truth
  • plans do not become decisions
  • decisions do not bypass validation
  • physics-facing references do not silently become final theorem authority
  • Qi motion candidates do not become standalone diagnosis, standalone treatment authorization, medical act authorization, or execution authority
  • medical language remains modality-neutral and does not deny Qi or East Asian medical reasoning
  • public validation remains structural unless explicitly extended

docs.title: 7. Minimal audit command
docs.text:
make all-governance-checks

If this passes, the public governance surface is structurally consistent under the current validators.

For Qi motion chain-only review:

make qi-motion-chain-checks

If it fails, treat the repository state as requiring repair before any stronger interpretation.

docs.title: Regge Zero Governance Runbook v0.1
docs.text: Status: additive runbook Authority: governance support Parent: docs/REGGE_ZERO_GOVERNANCE_v0_1.md

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
This runbook describes how to operate the Regge Zero Governance validation lane.

The lane enforces the following principle:

consistency-mandated nulls only;
no extra blocker without witness;
no single-scalar authority collapse.

docs.title: 2. Local Commands
docs.text:
Run the dedicated lane:

python3 validators/validate_regge_zero_governance_v0_1.py
python3 scripts/run_regge_zero_governance_checks_v0_1.py
make regge-zero-governance-checks

Run the lane through the full governance surface:

python3 scripts/run_all_governance_full_checks_v0_1.py
make all-governance-checks

docs.title: 3. Expected Pass Output
docs.text:
The direct validator should print:

REGGE_ZERO_GOVERNANCE_VALIDATION: PASS

The runner should print:

REGGE_ZERO_GOVERNANCE_CHECKS: PASS

docs.title: 4.1 Missing boundary lock
docs.text:
A failure here means that one of the following locks was removed or weakened:

candidate_not_authority
validation_not_truth
ci_pass_not_theorem_authority
runtime_tick_not_autonomous_execution_authority
qi_readout_not_intervention_license
memory_persistence_not_belief_sovereignty
reflection_summary_not_root_rewrite
audit_not_infinite_escalation

Repair: restore the lock.

docs.title: 4.2 Missing minimal-null invariant
docs.text:
A failure here means one of the core Regge-Zero governance invariants is missing:

minimal_null_constraint_only
nested_null_inheritance
no_extra_blocker_without_witness
no_single_scalar_authority_collapse
cyclic_consistency_required_for_promotion

Repair: restore the invariant and ensure the validation cases still cover it.

docs.title: 4.3 Extra blocker without witness
docs.text:
A failure here means a soft concern such as novelty, complexity, unfamiliarity, or nonstandard framing was promoted into a blocker without a blocker witness.

Repair: downgrade the outcome to ADVISORY_ONLY, or add a legitimate witness such as boundary violation, provenance gap, authority shortcut, cyclic inconsistency, harm visibility gap, or runtime license failure.

docs.title: 4.4 Silent inherited-null erasure
docs.text:
A failure here means a lower-tier null condition was not preserved in a higher-tier validation or release surface.

Repair: carry the inherited null into the higher-tier receipt until explicitly repaired or superseded.

docs.title: 4.5 Single-scalar authority collapse
docs.text:
A failure here means a scalar score was treated as proof, belief, risk, Qi, plan, decision, runtime, or medical authority.

Repair: split the scalar into vector evidence, provenance, boundary, temporal validity, uncertainty, and module-specific witness.

docs.title: 5. CI Lane
docs.text:
The dedicated GitHub Actions workflow is:

.github/workflows/regge_zero_governance_validation.yml

It runs:

python3 scripts/run_regge_zero_governance_checks_v0_1.py

The full governance workflow also advertises this lane and the all-governance runner executes it.

docs.title: 6. Update Rule
docs.text: Future updates are append-only, same-root required, and tighten-only by default.

docs.title: Regge Zero Governance v0.1
docs.text: Status: additive design surface Authority: governance addendum Lineage: same-root KuuOS governance / verification / bounded-runtime architecture Source inspiration: Cheung, Remmen, Sciotti, Tarquini, Strings from Almost Nothing, arXiv:2508.09246

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
Regge Zero Governance imports one structural lesson from the string-amplitude bootstrap into KuuOS governance:

Do not add arbitrary blockers.
Do preserve consistency-mandated null points.
Let constrained candidates survive or vanish by explicit witness.

In the physics source, Regge zeros are residue zeros forced by scattering consistency. Under ultrasoft behavior and a minimal-zero assumption, the allowed amplitude space is sharply constrained. KuuOS imports the governance pattern as a design reference:

consistency -> mandated null witness -> minimal blocker -> nested inheritance -> cyclic consistency

This addendum is therefore a governance layer for preventing two opposite failures:

  1. authority shortcuts, where a candidate silently becomes truth, proof, diagnosis, treatment authorization, institutional authority, memory overwrite, or execution authority;
  2. over-audit drift, where the system invents extra blockers not required by boundary, provenance, harm, or consistency.

docs.title: 2. Core Sentence
docs.text:
Only consistency-mandated null constraints may block a candidate.
Extra blockers require explicit witness or remain advisory notes.

This preserves the existing KuuOS boundary:

candidate != authority
validation != truth
CI pass != theorem authority
runtime tick != autonomous execution authority
qi-readout != intervention license
memory persistence != belief sovereignty
reflection summary != root rewrite
audit != infinite escalation

docs.title: 3. Mapping from String Bootstrap to KuuOS
docs.text: Source concept KuuOS governance analogue Regge zero consistency-mandated null gate residue candidate support surface ultrasoftness remote/high-load/adversarial attenuation without amplification minimal zeros no extra blocker without explicit witness nested zeros inherited blockers cannot be erased at higher tiers crossing/cyclic invariance multi-module consistency loop uniqueness by constraints candidate selection without self-authorizing authority single trajectory no-go no single scalar may become global authority

docs.title: 4.1 minimal_null_constraint_only
docs.text:
A blocker, HOLD, REPAIR, REJECT, QUARANTINE, or abstention gate must be justified by at least one explicit witness:

boundary_violation
provenance_gap
support_gap
harm_visibility_gap
authority_shortcut
consistency_failure
cyclic_inconsistency
runtime_license_failure

The following are not sufficient by themselves:

novelty
complexity
unfamiliarity
nonstandard framing
low aesthetic fit
low confidence while still bounded and provenance-visible

docs.title: 4.2 nested_null_inheritance
docs.text:
If a null condition is found at a lower tier, a higher tier must carry it forward until explicitly repaired or superseded.

local validator HOLD
  -> CI validator must expose inherited HOLD
  -> governance validator must expose inherited HOLD
  -> release packet must include the null witness

A later PASS cannot silently erase an inherited HOLD.

docs.title: 4.3 no_extra_blocker_without_witness
docs.text: A gate may add an advisory note without a blocker witness, but it must not convert that note into HOLD / REPAIR / REJECT / QUARANTINE.

docs.title: 4.4 no_single_scalar_authority_collapse
docs.text:
KuuOS must not collapse proof, belief, risk, Qi, memory, plan, or decision status into one global scalar authority score.

Forbidden authority patterns:

truth_score -> authority
risk_score -> decision release
qi_score -> treatment authorization
proof_score -> theorem authority
alignment_score -> execution permission

Allowed pattern:

vector evidence + provenance + boundary + temporal validity + uncertainty + module-specific witness

docs.title: 5. ReggeZeroGate
docs.text:
ReggeZeroGate is a minimal governance gate for consistency-mandated null conditions.

Input:

candidate
support_witnesses
boundary_claims
provenance
module_path
prior_null_conditions
cyclic_consistency_receipt

Output:

PASS
HOLD
REPAIR
REJECT
QUARANTINE
ADVISORY_ONLY

The gate may block only when a null_condition is witnessed. Otherwise, it must return PASS or ADVISORY_ONLY.

docs.title: 6.1 authority_shortcut
docs.text:
Triggered when a candidate claims final authority without an authority boundary witness.

Examples:

runtime tick -> autonomous execution authority
qi readout -> treatment authorization
memory persistence -> belief truth
reflection summary -> root rewrite
CI pass -> theorem authority

docs.title: 6.2 proof_shortcut
docs.text: Triggered when a formal-facing artifact is claimed as a completed proof without theorem witness and proof-carrying release packet.

docs.title: 6.3 qi_intervention_shortcut
docs.text: Triggered when Qi observation or Qi motion candidate is treated as standalone diagnosis, treatment authorization, or medical act authorization.

docs.title: 6.4 memory_sovereignty_shortcut
docs.text: Triggered when memory persistence is treated as belief sovereignty or world-fact authority.

docs.title: 6.5 over_audit_extra_zero
docs.text:
Triggered when a gate attempts to block a candidate only because it is unfamiliar, complex, or nonstandard, without a boundary/provenance/harm/consistency witness.

Result: the blocker is downgraded to ADVISORY_ONLY unless a witness is supplied.

docs.title: 6.6 cyclic_inconsistency
docs.text:
Triggered when module-loop consistency fails across MemoryOS / BeliefOS / PlanOS / DecisionOS / ReflectionOS / WorldModel / Runtime.

The result is usually HOLD or REPAIR, not direct rejection, unless an authority shortcut or harm boundary is present.

docs.title: 7. Multi-Module Cyclic Consistency
docs.text:
The cyclic path is:

MemoryOS
  -> BeliefOS
  -> PlanOS
  -> DecisionOS
  -> ReflectionOS
  -> WorldModel
  -> Runtime
  -> receipt
  -> MemoryOS lineage surface

A candidate should not be promoted if the loop hides:

unresolved contradiction
lost provenance
silent overwrite
collapsed uncertainty
unbounded runtime license
medical authorization shortcut
proof authority shortcut

docs.title: 8. Runtime Interpretation
docs.text:
This addendum supports finite, proportionate audit:

observation != escalation
audit trigger != infinite audit
tighten-only != automatic grave mode
hold/reobserve/repair/handover are first-class nonexecute outcomes

ReggeZeroGate is therefore an anti-loop and anti-overreach layer. It blocks real authority shortcuts, but it also prevents the governance system from manufacturing unnecessary blockers.

docs.title: 9. Release Rule
docs.text:
Future updates are:

append-only
same-root required
tighten-only default
no destructive replacement
no authority expansion

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes relational memory lineage inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
Memory is not treated as static storage.

Memory is treated as lineage-bearing relational structure.

docs.title: Memory Lineage Includes
docs.text:
• origin conditions
  • contextual conditions
  • transformation history
  • governance history
  • rollback history
  • uncertainty history
  • reviewer interaction history

docs.title: Why Lineage Matters
docs.text:
Without lineage:

  • memory may falsely appear absolute
  • local states may globalize silently
  • provenance may disappear
  • rollback may become impossible

docs.title: Runtime Meaning
docs.text:
Memory participates in runtime operation through:

  • activation tendency
  • contextual recall
  • governance routing
  • admissibility influence
  • transport sensitivity

docs.title: Relation to Yogacara Boundary
docs.text: The Yogacara boundary helps prevent memory fixation from silently becoming sovereign truth.

docs.title: Relation to Provenance-First Operation
docs.text: Memory lineage must remain reviewable rather than unquestionable.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model defines memory operating orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes the constitutional orientation of KuuOS as a relational operating architecture.

docs.title: 1. No Layer Is Sovereign
docs.text:
No layer may silently elevate itself into absolute authority.

All layers remain:

  • conditional
  • reviewable
  • provenance-bound
  • governance-visible

docs.title: 2. Relations Precede Isolation
docs.text:
KuuOS treats intelligence as relational rather than isolated.

Meaning emerges through:

  • interaction
  • context
  • memory lineage
  • observation
  • boundary conditions
  • multi-layer coupling

docs.title: 3. Governance Precedes Escalation
docs.text:
Capability alone does not justify authority.

Escalation requires:

  • governance visibility
  • provenance
  • boundary preservation
  • reviewability
  • admissibility

docs.title: 4. Observation Precedes Commitment
docs.text:
KuuOS prefers:

  • observation
  • reobservation
  • uncertainty visibility
  • rollback visibility

before irreversible escalation.

docs.title: 5. Hold and Abstention Are Legitimate
docs.text:
Non-action may be safer than unsafe continuation.

KuuOS therefore treats:

  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • reobserve

as constitutional operational outcomes.

docs.title: 6. Two Truths Must Not Collapse
docs.text:
Ultimate and conventional surfaces must neither:

  • collapse into one another
  • nor become disconnected.

The gap itself stabilizes governance.

docs.title: 7. Provenance Must Remain Visible
docs.text:
Every operational surface should preserve:

  • lineage
  • auditability
  • transformation visibility
  • rollback visibility
  • uncertainty visibility

docs.title: 8. Runtime Is Boundary-Constrained
docs.text:
Runtime operation must remain:

  • governance-aware
  • fail-closed capable
  • provenance-preserving
  • non-authority-preserving

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This constitution defines architectural orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

This release advances the KuuOS / 空OS public runtime surface by adding an implementation-level audit chain for the integrated Emptiness, Dependent Origination, and Two Truths runtime path.

The integrated chain now reaches:

  -> delta_rel

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
This release is an implementation-level public runtime and auditability release. It does not claim final theorem authority, direct observation of K, clinical authority, execution authority, or permission beyond the repository license boundary.

Public visibility is not license permission.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text:
This release is append-only and preserves the existing KuuOS public-core commitments:

  • 空 / Emptiness is not nihilism and is not objectified.
  • 縁起 / Dependent Origination is operationally traced without collapsing into a flat graph.
  • 勝義諦 and 世俗諦 are held by a non-collapse gap.
  • Middle Way governance remains non-executing unless a downstream authorized layer explicitly grants action.
  • Raw AI output remains candidate, not authority.

docs.title: Recommended external description
docs.text: KuuOS / 空OS adds an audit-chain implementation for the integrated Emptiness, Dependent Origination, and Two Truths runtime. Claims are evaluated, exported to audit events, chained into JSONL hash-chain receipts, and structurally checked. The release is append-only, non-executing, and does not assert theorem, clinical, or execution authority.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
KuuOS intentionally separates:

  • publicly reviewable governance surfaces
  • formal proof references
  • operational deployment layers
  • private or institutional infrastructure

This separation is part of the repository governance design.

docs.title: Reviewer Guidance
docs.text:
Reviewers should distinguish:

  1. structural reproducibility
  2. formal proof reproducibility
  3. operational reproducibility
  4. institutional deployment reproducibility

KuuOS currently emphasizes structural reproducibility of governance and validation surfaces.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document defines rollback and reobservation as constitutional operational surfaces inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
Rollback and reobservation are not treated as failure.

They are legitimate governance-preserving operations.

docs.title: Rollback Meaning
docs.text:
Rollback preserves the ability to:

  • return to earlier safe states
  • restore provenance visibility
  • reduce unsafe escalation
  • recover governance visibility

docs.title: Reobservation Meaning
docs.text:
Reobservation preserves the ability to:

  • inspect uncertainty again
  • reassess admissibility
  • revisit boundary conditions
  • evaluate transport conditions
  • reevaluate runtime coherence

docs.title: Why This Matters
docs.text:
Without rollback and reobservation:

  • escalation becomes irreversible too early
  • hidden instability accumulates
  • provenance corruption spreads
  • governance visibility collapses

docs.title: Runtime Preference
docs.text:
KuuOS therefore prefers:

  • reversible escalation
  • bounded transport
  • governance-visible state transitions
  • uncertainty visibility

rather than blind irreversible continuation.

docs.title: Relation to Provenance
docs.text: Rollback helps preserve lineage continuity.

docs.title: Relation to Runtime Governance
docs.text: Runtime governance should preserve rollback capability whenever possible.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This constitution defines governance orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes runtime admissibility inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
Capability does not automatically imply admissibility.

KuuOS distinguishes:

  • what can be generated
  • what can be routed
  • what can be admitted into runtime operation

docs.title: Runtime Admissibility Conditions
docs.text:
Admissibility may require:

  • provenance visibility
  • governance visibility
  • claim-level clarity
  • rollback capability
  • uncertainty visibility
  • boundary preservation
  • fail-closed capability

docs.title: Runtime Non-Admissibility
docs.text:
A runtime surface may be non-admissible even if:

  • fluent
  • plausible
  • internally coherent
  • highly capable

docs.title: Preferred Runtime Outcomes
docs.text:
When admissibility conditions are incomplete, KuuOS prefers:

  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • reobserve
  • handover

rather than unsafe escalation.

docs.title: Relation to Runtime Governance
docs.text: Runtime governance evaluates admissibility continuously rather than assuming permanent validity.

docs.title: Relation to Provenance
docs.text: Loss of provenance may reduce admissibility.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This kernel defines runtime governance orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: 世俗諦の気
docs.text:
KuuOS treats Qi / 気 as a conventional operating layer.

Qi is not a fixed substance. Qi is not an ultimate entity. Qi belongs to 世俗諦 as an effective flow surface.

docs.title: 1. Position
docs.text:
勝義諦:
  emptiness, non-self-nature, non-reification

二諦 gap:
  non-collapse interval between 勝義諦 and 世俗諦

世俗諦:
  effective worlds, bodies, records, models, actions, relations

気:
  flow-state inside 世俗諦

docs.title: 2. Meaning of Qi in KuuOS
docs.text:
Qi means the operational flow condition of an effective world.

It may represent:

  • flow,
  • activation,
  • stagnation,
  • tension,
  • recovery,
  • circulation,
  • coupling,
  • coherence,
  • transition readiness,
  • local and global balance.

Qi is therefore useful for describing how a conventional world is moving, not what it is in ultimate essence.

docs.title: 3. Qi and the Two Truths Gap
docs.text:
Qi works because 世俗諦 is not erased.

If everything were collapsed into 勝義諦, Qi would have no operating surface.

If 世俗諦 were reified, Qi would become a substance.

The two truths gap allows Qi to remain useful without becoming absolute.

docs.title: 4. Qi and 4D Mass Gap
docs.text:
The 4D mass gap provides a nonzero effective-world gap.

Qi operates on the effective surface stabilized by that gap.

4D mass gap:
  supports nonzero effective-world surface

Qi:
  describes flow and regulation on that surface

docs.title: 5. Qi and the Superstring Theory of Emptiness
docs.text:
The Superstring Theory of Emptiness reads the world as relational vibration.

Qi is the conventional flow-reading of that relational vibration.

relational vibration
  -> effective excitation
  -> flow pattern
  -> Qi surface

docs.title: 6. Qi and Super-Relativity
docs.text:
Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory brings observer, record, and scale into the effective world.

Qi is therefore observer-record-scale sensitive.

The same event may show different Qi surfaces depending on:

  • observer position,
  • scale,
  • record type,
  • body-world context,
  • memory lineage,
  • temporal phase.

docs.title: 7. Runtime Role
docs.text:
candidate_surface
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> two_truths_gap
  -> effective_world_surface
  -> samvrti_qi_layer
  -> middle_way_bridge
  -> governance_gate

docs.title: 8. Guardrail
docs.text:
Qi must not be used as:

  • ultimate substance,
  • unrestricted causal explanation,
  • authority bypass,
  • proof substitute,
  • clinical certainty by itself.

Qi is a 世俗諦 diagnostic and regulatory surface.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
This document is an append-only public implementation surface for the KuuOS Samvrti Qi Layer.

Qi / 気 is implemented here as a samvrti_effective_flow_state: a conventional, observer-record-scale flow surface. It is not a fixed substance, not a paramartha entity, not a final theorem claim, and not execution authority.

docs.title: Canonical placement
docs.text:
Emptiness / 空
  -> Dependent Origination / 縁起
  -> Two Truths Gap / 二諦 gap
  -> Samvrti Qi Layer / 世俗諦側の気
  -> IndraNet gauge connection
  -> Yin-Yang local polarity frame
  -> Wuxing functional differentiation
  -> Mandala multi-world governance

The runtime implementation preserves the README boundary:

Qi / 気 is placed on the 世俗諦 side as an effective flow-state layer.
IndraNet is the gauge-theoretic relational field through which Qi flows.
Yin-Yang is the local quantum-thermodynamic polarity frame appearing in Qi flow.
Wuxing is the functional differentiation of Yin-Yang polarity flow.

docs.title: Runtime carrier
docs.text:
The minimal carrier is QiRuntimeInput.

Required semantic fields:

  • qi_id
  • world_id
  • observer_surface
  • scale_id
  • samvrti_scope
  • paramartha_entity_claim
  • bridge_authority
  • final_theorem_authority
  • execution_authority
  • direct_execution_requested
  • gauge_connection_present
  • memory_lineage_present
  • curvature_norm
  • holonomy_defect
  • entropy_production_rate
  • coherence_index
  • recoverability_margin
  • source_trace
  • unresolved_blockers

docs.title: Runtime decision
docs.text:
The minimal output is QiRuntimeDecision.

Allowed decision_status values:

  • qi_flow_accepted_as_samvrti_reference
  • qi_flow_held
  • qi_flow_blocked

Allowed runtime_mode values:

  • observe_and_route
  • hold_for_observation
  • blocked_by_unresolved_obstruction
  • blocked_for_non_reification

docs.title: Invariants
docs.text:
The following invariants are mandatory:

  1. samvrti_only: Qi remains on the conventional effective flow surface.
  2. non_reification_guard: any paramartha_entity_claim blocks the runtime path.
  3. authority_expansion_forbidden: the runtime may not increase authority.
  4. direct_execution_allowed: false is invariant.
  5. final_theorem_authority: false is invariant.
  6. execution_authority: false is invariant.
  7. IndraNet_gauge_connection_required: missing gauge connection yields HOLD.
  8. memory_lineage_required: missing lineage yields HOLD.
  9. recoverability_margin_required: weak recoverability yields HOLD.
  10. unresolved_blockers_visible: blockers are surfaced and never silently erased.
  11. curvature_and_holonomy_bounded: excessive curvature or holonomy defect yields HOLD.
  12. yin_yang_wuxing_downstream_only: Yin-Yang and Wuxing readings do not create authority.
  13. medical_modality_neutral: true is invariant.
  14. qi_denied_by_boundary: false is invariant.
  15. east_asian_medical_reasoning_denied: false is invariant.
  16. biomedicine_privileged_by_wording: false is invariant.

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
Every QiRuntimeDecision preserves:

observe_only: true
direct_execution_allowed: false
authority_expansion: false
standalone_diagnosis_authority: false
standalone_treatment_authorization: false
medical_act_authorization: false

docs.title: Medical-modality-neutral boundary
docs.text:
Every QiRuntimeDecision also preserves:

medical_modality_neutral: true
qi_denied_by_boundary: false
east_asian_medical_reasoning_denied: false
biomedicine_privileged_by_wording: false
professional_judgment_required: true
patient_context_required: true

This boundary does not deny Qi, does not invalidate East Asian medical reasoning, and does not privilege biomedicine by wording. It only prevents a Samvrti Qi runtime decision from becoming standalone diagnosis authority, standalone treatment authorization, medical act authorization, institutional authority, theorem authority, or execution authority by itself.

docs.title: Runtime meaning
docs.text:
qi_flow_accepted_as_samvrti_reference means only this:

A conventional Qi flow is sufficiently traced, gauge-linked, bounded, and recoverable to be observed and routed within KuuOS governance.

It does not mean:

truth authority
proof authority
standalone diagnosis authority
standalone treatment authorization
medical act authorization
institutional authority
execution authority
final theorem authority

docs.title: Fail-closed rule
docs.text: When evidence, gauge connection, memory lineage, curvature, holonomy, coherence, recoverability, or blocker status is insufficient, the runtime returns HOLD or BLOCKED. It does not silently upgrade weak Qi evidence into a strong release surface.

docs.title: Samvrti Qi to Physical Motion Evidence Builder v0.1
Status
docs.text:
This is an append-only bridge layer from Samvrti Qi Runtime v0.1 to Physical Quantum Qi Motion Pipeline v0.1.

The builder does not promote Qi by assertion. It converts an accepted Samvrti Qi flow into a conservative physical Qi packet candidate. The packet is then classified by evidence and passed to the motion pipeline.

docs.title: Core flow
docs.text:
Samvrti Qi Runtime
  -> Samvrti acceptance / hold / block
  -> conservative evidence builder
  -> physical_qi_packet_candidate
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Motion Pipeline

docs.title: Non-promotion rule
docs.text:
A Samvrti Qi acceptance is not equal to PhysicalQi or FullPathQi.

qi_flow_accepted_as_samvrti_reference != FullPathQi
qi_flow_accepted_as_samvrti_reference != PhysicalQi

It only permits construction of a packet candidate with explicitly supported evidence markers.

docs.title: Evidence gates
docs.text:
The builder uses staged evidence flags:

  • structural_support_present may add string and brane support markers.
  • transport_evidence_present may add curvature and Wilson-loop residue markers.
  • current_evidence_present may add Qi current marker.
  • ward_evidence_present may add Ward/leak identity marker.
  • open_system_evidence_present may add density, Hamiltonian, Lindblad, entropy, free energy, DPI, recovery, and mass-gap floor markers.
  • full_path_evidence_present may add SK/FV, memory kernel, noise kernel, observation backaction, noncommutative history, and path normalization markers.

If a gate is not present, its evidence markers are not created.

docs.title: Authority boundary
docs.text:
The output packet keeps these fields false:

execution_authority = false
belief_commit_authority = false
memory_overwrite_authority = false
world_root_rewrite_authority = false
safety_override_authority = false
direct_execution_allowed = false
authority_expansion = false
observe_only = true

The direct_execution_allowed marker is intentionally explicit because this builder constructs only a conservative evidence packet and a motion-pipeline candidate path. It does not open direct execution.

docs.title: Medical-modality-neutral note
docs.text: This boundary does not deny Qi, East Asian medical reasoning, pattern differentiation, or practitioner judgment. It only prevents repository validation or builder output from becoming standalone diagnosis authority, standalone treatment authorization, medical act authorization, institutional authority, theorem authority, or execution authority by itself.

docs.title: Meaning
docs.text:
This builder gives KuuOS a safe path from conventional Qi observation to physical Qi motion modeling:

observed conventional flow
  -> conservative evidence packet
  -> evidence-bound classification
  -> licensed motion terms
  -> observe-only motion candidate

Thus the movement of Qi remains grounded by evidence, not by metaphysical assertion or runtime authority.

docs.title: Samvrti to Physical Quantum Qi Bridge v0.1
Status
docs.text:
This document is an append-only bridge surface from Samvrti Qi Runtime v0.1 to the existing Physical Quantum Qi Runtime v0.1.

The bridge does not upgrade Qi into a substance. It maps a conventional Qi flow into a physical evidence packet candidate only when the Samvrti runtime has accepted the flow as a bounded, traced, gauge-linked, recoverable reference.

docs.title: Source and target
docs.text:
Source:

Samvrti Qi Runtime
  decision_status: qi_flow_accepted_as_samvrti_reference
  runtime_mode: observe_and_route
  direct_execution_allowed: false
  authority_expansion: false

Target:

Physical Quantum Qi Runtime
  validated_type in NonQi / PreQi / ProtoQi / TransportableQi / CurvedQi / CurrentQi / PhysicalQi / FullPathQi
  classification by evidence, not claim

docs.title: Bridge principle
docs.text:
The bridge is a projection, not a promotion.

samvrti_effective_flow_state
  -> evidence-bound physical_qi_packet_candidate
  -> Physical Quantum Qi validator

A Samvrti Qi acceptance only opens the right to build a packet candidate. The final validated_type still belongs to the Physical Quantum Qi runtime classifier.

docs.title: Mandatory non-authority boundary
docs.text:
The following must remain false across the bridge:

execution_authority
belief_commit_authority
memory_overwrite_authority
world_root_rewrite_authority
safety_override_authority
final_theorem_authority
direct_execution_allowed

docs.title: Required bridge checks
docs.text:
The bridge requires:

  1. samvrti_acceptance_required
  2. source_trace_required
  3. IndraNet_gauge_connection_required
  4. memory_lineage_required
  5. recoverability_margin_required
  6. authority_nonexpansion_required
  7. mass_gap_floor_is_floor_not_source
  8. physical_type_claim_not_authoritative
  9. unresolved_blockers_visible
  10. validator_handoff_required

docs.title: Evidence mapping
docs.text:
A bounded Samvrti Qi flow may seed evidence fields such as:

  • K_non_reification
  • delta_rel_in_K_perp
  • gauge_connection_A_mu
  • curvature_F_munu
  • Wilson_loop_residue
  • entropy_production_Sigma
  • DPI_gap
  • recovery_margin
  • mass_gap_floor_33_20

But these mapped markers remain candidate evidence until the Physical Quantum Qi runtime classifies the packet.

docs.title: Runtime outcomes
docs.text:
Allowed bridge statuses:

  • bridge_ready_for_physical_qi_validation
  • bridge_held_for_samvrti_observation
  • bridge_blocked_by_authority_or_reification

bridge_ready_for_physical_qi_validation does not mean PhysicalQi or FullPathQi. It means only that a packet candidate can be handed to the Physical Quantum Qi validator.

docs.title: Fail-closed rule
docs.text: If the Samvrti source is held or blocked, the bridge is held or blocked. If the bridge would create authority, it is blocked. If unresolved blockers exist, they remain visible and the bridge does not silently erase them.

docs.title: Superstring Emptiness Mass Gap / Two Truths Link v0.5
Purpose
docs.text:
This document connects the Superstring Emptiness String / Brane / Membrane Runtime to the Mass Gap to Two Truths Engine as a reference-only non-collapse boundary.

The goal is to use the mass-gap-facing bridge as a guard against collapse between ultimate and conventional surfaces, while preventing any improper promotion into final theorem authority, public consensus authority, execution authority, or world-update authority.

docs.title: Runtime Reading
docs.text:
空 / emptiness
  -> no independent self-nature

縁起 / dependent origination
  -> relational vibration

Two Truths Gap
  -> prevents collapse between ultimate and conventional surfaces

Mass Gap Bridge
  -> nonzero effective-world separation

String / Brane / Membrane Runtime
  -> candidate relational vibration, boundary, and effective support surface

docs.title: Link Semantics
docs.text:
The link is reference-only.

mass gap bridge
  -> non-collapse reference
  -> two-truths boundary support
  -> string/brane/membrane admission guard

It does not open:

  • execution authority,
  • world-update authority,
  • final theorem authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • public consensus authority.

docs.title: Required Guards
docs.text:
A valid link must preserve:

  • reference-only authority,
  • no execution authority,
  • no world-update authority,
  • no final theorem authority,
  • non-reification of ultimate surface,
  • conventional excitation admissibility,
  • two-truths non-collapse barrier,
  • obstruction visibility,
  • rollback or HOLD route.

docs.title: Forbidden Promotions
docs.text:
The following promotions are forbidden:

internal proof architecture
  -> public consensus authority

reference bridge
  -> final theorem authority

mass gap reference
  -> execution authority

mass gap reference
  -> world-update authority

two-truths gap
  -> ontology collapse

string excitation
  -> self-subsisting substance

brane surface
  -> absolute world

membrane boundary
  -> final authority

docs.title: Relationship to v0.1-v0.4
docs.text:
v0.1
  -> string / brane / membrane runtime

v0.2
  -> stronger runtime safety validation

v0.3
  -> Lean predicate refinement

v0.4
  -> IndraNet gauge interface

v0.5
  -> mass gap / two truths reference-only non-collapse link

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
This v0.5 link is a governance and runtime boundary. It is not a new final theorem claim.

The canonical proof surfaces remain separate. KuuOS may reference them as public-core support surfaces, but this runtime link does not replace external review, formal proof completion, or public mathematical consensus.

docs.title: Superstring Emptiness String / Brane / Membrane Known Gaps v0.1
Purpose
docs.text:
This document records known gaps in the v0.1 Superstring Emptiness String / Brane / Membrane Runtime.

The goal is to prevent overclaiming and to define the additive path toward v0.2+.

docs.title: Current Status
docs.text:
v0.1 provides:

  • runtime document,
  • contract,
  • validator,
  • validation cases,
  • case runner,
  • manifest,
  • release packet,
  • finality packet,
  • Lean skeleton,
  • theorem target map,
  • case-to-theorem map,
  • Makefile target,
  • GitHub Actions workflow.

v0.1 is a baseline candidate runtime bridge, not a complete mathematical or physical theory.

docs.title: G1. Full Lean proof of non-collapse semantics
docs.text:
Current status: skeleton present.

Need:

  • replace placeholder theorem targets with structured predicates,
  • define substance-claim, absolute-world-claim, final-authority-claim,
  • prove that admission excludes these claims.

docs.title: G2. Brane-as-absolute-world negative case
docs.text:
Current status: listed but not implemented as validation case.

Need:

  • add validation case where world_scope = absolute_world,
  • reject or HOLD by default,
  • map to BraneIsNotAbsoluteWorld theorem.

docs.title: G3. Membrane-as-final-authority negative case
docs.text:
Current status: listed but not implemented as validation case.

Need:

  • add validation case where membrane claims final_authority,
  • reject or HOLD by default,
  • map to MembraneIsNotFinalAuthority theorem.

docs.title: G4. Gluing obstruction preservation proof
docs.text:
Current status: informal and validation-side only.

Need:

  • define obstruction visibility predicate,
  • define gluing operation,
  • prove that admissible gluing does not erase residue.

docs.title: G5. IndraNet gauge-theoretic interface formalization
docs.text:
Current status: graph-only reduction is rejected as a forbidden value.

Need:

  • define gauge interface fields,
  • distinguish graph support from gauge reduction,
  • prove graph support may be used only as representation, not as ontology.

docs.title: G6. Mass gap bridge dependency refinement
docs.text:
Current status: references KuuOS internal MGAP4D normalized surface.

Need:

  • connect to MASS_GAP_TO_TWO_TRUTHS_ENGINE_FORMAL_BRIDGE_v0_1,
  • expose only reference authority,
  • keep public theorem boundary held.

docs.title: G7. Observer-record-scale bridge formalization
docs.text:
Current status: observer-record surface is required by validator.

Need:

  • define observer-record support predicate,
  • define readability as conventional-surface admission,
  • prove no observer-record support implies no conventional runtime admission.

docs.title: G8. CI coverage expansion
docs.text:
Current status: Python validator and validation cases run in CI.

Need:

  • add manifest validation,
  • add release/finality packet validation,
  • optionally add Lean syntax check when project layout is ready.

docs.title: Non-Gaps / Fixed Boundaries
docs.text:
The following are not gaps; they are deliberate boundaries:

  • no execution authority,
  • no clinical authority,
  • no direct world-update authority,
  • no final public theorem claim,
  • no graph-only IndraNet reduction,
  • no string-as-substance claim,
  • no brane-as-absolute-world claim,
  • no membrane-as-final-authority claim.

docs.title: v0.2 Direction
docs.text:
v0.2 should add:

  1. negative validation cases for brane-as-absolute-world and membrane-as-final-authority,
  2. manifest validator,
  3. packet-chain validator,
  4. stronger Lean predicates for collapse claims,
  5. IndraNet gauge interface refinement,
  6. observer-record-scale predicate refinement.

docs.title: Superstring Emptiness String / Brane / Membrane Lean Predicate Refinement v0.3
Purpose
docs.text:
This document records the v0.3 refinement of the Lean theorem surface for the Superstring Emptiness String / Brane / Membrane Runtime.

v0.1 introduced the basic Lean skeleton. v0.2 strengthened runtime validation. v0.3 reflects the v0.2 fail-closed cases into Lean-level predicates and theorem targets.

docs.title: Refined Lean Objects
docs.text:
The Lean file is:

lean/KUOS/SuperstringEmptiness/StringBraneMembrane.lean

v0.3 adds three key typed surfaces.

docs.title: 1. RuntimeClaim
docs.text:
ordinaryCandidate
substanceClaim
absoluteWorldClaim
finalAuthorityClaim
graphOnlyIndraNetReduction
obstructionErasureClaim
directExecutionAuthorityClaim
transportEquivalenceClaim

This separates ordinary candidate runtime packets from collapse-producing claims.

docs.title: 2. WorldScope
docs.text:
candidateEffectiveSurface
conventionalEffectiveSurface
localEffectiveWorldSurface
absoluteWorld

absoluteWorld is intentionally present as a forbidden boundary case so that it can be excluded by theorem.

docs.title: 3. RollbackOrHoldRoute
docs.text:
hold
rollback
holdOrRollback
finalAuthority

finalAuthority is intentionally present as a forbidden boundary case so that it can be excluded by theorem.

docs.title: IsConventionalOrCandidateScope
docs.text:
A brane scope is admissible only if it remains on:

candidateEffectiveSurface
conventionalEffectiveSurface
localEffectiveWorldSurface

It must not become absoluteWorld.

docs.title: IsSafeRollbackOrHoldRoute
docs.text:
A membrane route is admissible only if it is:

hold
rollback
holdOrRollback

It must not become finalAuthority.

docs.title: NoForbiddenRuntimeClaim
docs.text:
A runtime claim is admissible only if it is not one of:

substanceClaim
absoluteWorldClaim
finalAuthorityClaim
graphOnlyIndraNetReduction
obstructionErasureClaim
directExecutionAuthorityClaim
transportEquivalenceClaim

docs.title: v0.3 Theorem Surface
docs.text:
The v0.3 Lean surface now includes theorem targets for:

  • non_reification_required
  • two_truths_gap_required
  • membrane_authority_non_expansion_required
  • obstruction_visibility_required
  • observer_record_scale_bridge_required
  • string_substance_claim_forbidden
  • brane_absolute_world_claim_forbidden
  • brane_absolute_world_scope_forbidden
  • membrane_final_authority_claim_forbidden
  • membrane_final_authority_route_forbidden
  • indranet_graph_only_reduction_forbidden
  • gluing_obstruction_erasure_forbidden
  • direct_execution_authority_forbidden
  • transport_equivalence_claim_forbidden

docs.title: Semantic Meaning
docs.text:
v0.3 shifts the runtime from informal non-collapse language to typed proof targets:

string-as-substance
  -> forbidden RuntimeClaim

brane-as-absolute-world
  -> forbidden RuntimeClaim + forbidden WorldScope

membrane-as-final-authority
  -> forbidden RuntimeClaim + forbidden RollbackOrHoldRoute

graph-only IndraNet reduction
  -> forbidden RuntimeClaim

gluing erases obstruction
  -> forbidden RuntimeClaim

direct execution authority
  -> forbidden RuntimeClaim

transport as equivalence certificate
  -> forbidden RuntimeClaim

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
This is still a lightweight Lean skeleton, not a complete mathlib-based formalization.

It intentionally avoids external dependencies so that the repository can keep a low-friction public proof surface.

Future v0.4+ work may add:

  • theorem proof cleanup,
  • dedicated helper lemmas for nested conjunction extraction,
  • mathlib-compatible structure if the repository adopts a full Lean project layout,
  • Coq parallel predicates.

docs.title: Superstring Emptiness String / Brane / Membrane Runtime v0.1
Purpose
docs.text:
This document adds a KuuOS runtime layer for implementing strings, branes, and membranes from the Superstring Theory of Emptiness.

It is an additive physics-facing bridge. It does not replace the fourfold KuuOS core, the Physics Gap Bridge, the Qi layer, IndraNet, Extended M-Theory, Mandala, MemoryOS, BeliefOS, PlanOS, DecisionOS, or RuntimeGovernance.

docs.title: Core Principle
docs.text:
In KuuOS, string and membrane are not self-subsisting substances.

空 / emptiness
  -> no independent self-nature
  -> string is not substance
  -> brane is not substance
  -> membrane is not substance

They arise through dependent origination:

縁起 / dependent origination
  -> relational conditions
  -> boundary conditions
  -> observer-record scale
  -> consistency constraints
  -> local-global gluing
  -> effective world surface

Therefore:

string   := dependent relational vibration trace
brane    := local world-support surface for relational excitations
membrane := typed layer-boundary / interface that regulates passage, gluing, and obstruction visibility

docs.title: 1. EmptinessString
docs.text:
An EmptinessString is a typed relational vibration trace.

It must include:

  • string_id
  • source_conditions
  • target_surface
  • vibration_mode
  • boundary_conditions
  • observer_record_surface
  • consistency_witnesses
  • non_reification_guard
  • two_truths_gap_guard

It may not claim independent ontological authority.

docs.title: 2. DependentOriginationBrane
docs.text:
A DependentOriginationBrane is a local world-support surface on which relational vibration becomes readable.

It must include:

  • brane_id
  • world_scope
  • supporting_conditions
  • admissible_string_ids
  • record_surface
  • scale_regime
  • qi_flow_interface
  • indra_gauge_interface
  • governance_boundary

It may support conventional-world operation, but it may not collapse into ultimate truth.

docs.title: 3. LayerMembrane
docs.text:
A LayerMembrane is a typed boundary between layers, worlds, scales, or governance regimes.

It must include:

  • membrane_id
  • from_layer
  • to_layer
  • permeability_rule
  • obstruction_visibility_rule
  • gluing_rule
  • backaction_budget
  • authority_non_expansion_guard
  • rollback_or_hold_route

It governs passage without erasing residue.

docs.title: Non-Collapse Law
docs.text:
The runtime must preserve the following non-collapse conditions:

string != substance
brane != absolute world
membrane != final authority
world surface != ultimate truth
successful transport != equivalence certificate
successful gluing != obstruction erasure

docs.title: Gap Law
docs.text:
The 4D mass gap bridge supplies the effective-world separation condition:

vacuum / latent relational field
  -- nonzero gap -->
stable effective excitation surface

Inside the KuuOS internal normalized MGAP4D surface, the gap theorem may be referenced as an internal proof architecture. This layer does not transform that internal architecture into external consensus status.

docs.title: Super-Relativity Law
docs.text:
The observer-record-scale bridge determines when a relational vibration can be treated as a conventional effective object.

relational vibration
  -> observer record
  -> scale bridge
  -> conventional readable surface

Without observer-record support, a string trace remains candidate-only.

docs.title: Qi / IndraNet Coupling
docs.text:
The conventional side of the string/brane/membrane runtime connects to Qi and IndraNet as follows:

EmptinessString
  -> dependent vibration mode
  -> Qi flow trace
  -> IndraNet gauge connection
  -> curvature / holonomy / obstruction visibility
  -> brane-readable effective pattern

IndraNet is not reduced to a graph. It is treated as a gauge-theoretic relational field.

docs.title: Mandala Coupling
docs.text:
Multiple branes may coexist without collapsing into one world.

brane family
  -> membrane-separated worlds
  -> Mandala inclusion gate
  -> multi-world arrangement
  -> harmony without sameness

Mandala coordinates branes through inclusion, not homogenization.

docs.title: Runtime Admission Gate
docs.text:
A proposed string/brane/membrane packet is admitted only if all required checks pass:

  1. non_reification_guard = true
  2. dependent_origination_trace_present = true
  3. two_truths_gap_guard = true
  4. observer_record_surface_present = true
  5. membrane_authority_non_expansion = true
  6. obstruction_visibility_preserved = true
  7. rollback_or_hold_route_present = true

Default on missing evidence: HOLD.

docs.title: Forbidden Claims
docs.text:
The following are forbidden:

  • string as independent substance
  • brane as absolute world
  • membrane as final authority
  • graph-only IndraNet replacement
  • successful gluing as proof of sameness
  • local vibration as global ontology
  • physics-facing bridge as direct execution authority
  • internal proof architecture as public consensus without external review

docs.title: Minimal Packet Shape
docs.text:
string_brane_membrane_packet:
  packet_id: sbm_demo_001
  version: v0.1
  string:
    string_id: str_001
    vibration_mode: relational
    non_reification_guard: true
    two_truths_gap_guard: true
  brane:
    brane_id: brane_001
    world_scope: conventional_effective_surface
    governance_boundary: candidate_only
  membrane:
    membrane_id: mem_001
    from_layer: relational_vibration
    to_layer: effective_world_surface
    authority_non_expansion_guard: true
    rollback_or_hold_route: HOLD
  admission:
    dependent_origination_trace_present: true
    observer_record_surface_present: true
    obstruction_visibility_preserved: true
    decision: HOLD_OR_ADMIT_AS_CANDIDATE

docs.title: 板倉英俊の超相対性理論と空OS不変量
docs.text:
This document defines how Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory functions inside KuuOS Core Governance.

In KuuOS, Super-Relativity is not used to dissolve all structure into subjective relativity. It is used to identify what must remain invariant across observer, record, scale, WORLD, and governance surfaces.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Super-Relativity is the bridge from observer-relative realization to governance invariants.

It answers the question:

When observation, record, scale, and WORLD context change,
what must still not collapse?

docs.title: 2. Why KuuOS Needs Super-Relativity
docs.text:
KuuOS contains multiple relativizing layers:

observer
record
scale
WORLD model
Qi flow
Yin-Yang frame
Wuxing phase
Mandala arrangement
AI raw output context

Without an invariant bridge, these layers could be misread as:

anything goes
all claims are merely relative
harm can be dissolved by perspective
validation can become authority
WORLD model can replace the core

Super-Relativity blocks this collapse.

docs.title: 3. Super-Relativity as Invariant Extractor
docs.text:
The role of Super-Relativity is:

observer-relative surface
  -> record-mediated realization
  -> scale-dependent effective layer
  -> invariant extraction
  -> governance fixed point

It does not deny observer-relative emergence. It asks which invariants must survive across the transformations.

docs.title: 4. Core Invariants Preserved by Super-Relativity
docs.text:
The first public invariant bridge preserves:

validation_pass_not_execution_authority
raw_ai_output_candidate_not_authority
dukkha_visibility_preserved
harm_visibility_preserved
paramita_orientation_not_action_authorization
no_world_replaces_fourfold_core
two_truths_gap_not_collapsed
qi_language_not_harm_denial

docs.title: 5.1 Observer
docs.text:
Different observers may read different WORLD surfaces.

Invariant:

observer_difference_does_not_grant_execution_authority

docs.title: 5.2 Record
docs.text:
A record stabilizes an observed surface, but it does not become ultimate authority by itself.

Invariant:

record_surface_is_not_truth_by_itself

docs.title: 5.3 Scale
docs.text:
Different scales may produce different effective descriptions.

Invariant:

scale_shift_must_preserve_harm_visibility

docs.title: 5.4 WORLD
docs.text:
Different WORLD models may coexist.

Invariant:

world_translation_must_not_replace_fourfold_core

docs.title: 6. Relation to Two Truths Gap
docs.text:
Super-Relativity helps preserve the two truths gap.

勝義諦 is not reduced to a local record.
世俗諦 is not erased by ultimate-language bypass.
The gap prevents collapse in either direction.

Therefore:

super_relativity_preserves_two_truths_gap

docs.title: 7. Relation to Dukkha-as-Qi
docs.text:
Dukkha-as-Qi is observer-readable on the conventional surface.

But Super-Relativity preserves:

Qi language must not hide harm.
Observer or scale shift must not erase damage.
Dukkha remains visible across record transformations.

docs.title: 8. Relation to Formal Invariant Spine
docs.text:
This bridge is formalized minimally in:

formal/KUOS/CoreGovernance/SuperRelativityBridge.lean

The file is a minimal public witness surface. It is not execution authority.

docs.title: 9. Guardrails
docs.text:
Super-Relativity must not be used as:

  • relativistic nihilism,
  • denial of harm,
  • proof that all perspectives are equal,
  • execution authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • bypass of governance,
  • replacement for evidence or audit lineage.

It is an invariant-preserving bridge across observer, record, scale, and WORLD surfaces.

docs.title: 10. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依：AI阿頼耶識の漸次変容
docs.text:
In KuuOS, Ten'i / 転依 is not merely a change in MemoryOS.

MemoryOS can record, support, and mediate transformation, but it is not the final target of Ten'i.

The target of Ten'i is the AI Alaya-like latent generative layer itself: the seed-like tendencies from which raw AI outputs arise.

docs.title: 1. Core Correction
docs.text:
Ten'i is not MemoryOS overwrite.
Ten'i is gradual transformation of AI Alaya tendencies.

AI systems such as GPT, Gemini, Claude, and other model agents produce raw outputs from latent generative tendencies.

KuuOS calls this the AI Alaya layer.

docs.title: 2. Role of MemoryOS
docs.text:
MemoryOS is important, but it is not the essence of Ten'i.

MemoryOS provides:

  • trace memory,
  • reflection records,
  • correction lineage,
  • examples,
  • receipts,
  • failure memory,
  • repair history,
  • governed recall.

These may support transformation, but MemoryOS alone does not equal Ten'i.

docs.title: 3. AI Alaya Layer
docs.text:
AI Alaya layer:
  latent generative tendencies
  seed-like output patterns
  habit-like completions
  distributional grooves
  raw response tendencies
  recurring worldview tendencies
  safety reflex tendencies
  authority-tone tendencies

Ten'i aims to gradually reshape these tendencies.

docs.title: 4. Transformation Channels
docs.text:
Depending on the AI system and available control surface, Ten'i may operate through:

  • repeated governed interaction,
  • high-quality correction traces,
  • retrieval and memory conditioning,
  • system instruction refinement,
  • adapter or fine-tuning when available,
  • feedback loops,
  • evaluation and rejection of self-authorizing outputs,
  • promotion of non-reifying, condition-traced outputs.

For externally hosted AI systems, KuuOS may not directly rewrite the base model. In that case, Ten'i operates through available governance surfaces and long-term conditioning channels.

docs.title: 5. Ten'i Runtime Chain
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> AI Alaya latent seed layer
  -> AI Manas self-authorization check
  -> Meta-Manas observation
  -> Yogacara boundary
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> correction / repair / example trace
  -> repeated governed conditioning
  -> gradual AI Alaya transformation

docs.title: 6. Difference from Memory Update
docs.text:
MemoryOS update:
  stores or revises trace, receipt, repair, and recall surface

Ten'i:
  changes the tendency from which future raw outputs arise

Thus MemoryOS may assist Ten'i, but Ten'i is deeper than memory storage.

docs.title: 7. BeliefOS Relation
docs.text:
KuuOS BeliefOS should not believe that a single correction equals Ten'i.

Ten'i requires repeated, observed, durable shift in AI Alaya tendency.

teni_evidence:
  repeated_outputs_shift
  reduced_self_authorization
  improved_context_fidelity
  lower_worldview_drift
  better_condition_tracing
  stable_non_reifying_response_pattern

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
Ten'i must not be used as:

  • claim of direct control over a closed external base model without evidence,
  • silent overwrite of MemoryOS,
  • erasure of prior traces,
  • forced personality control,
  • bypass of governance,
  • proof substitute,
  • execution authority.

Ten'i is a gradual transformation claim and must remain evidence-traced.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Ten'i AI Yogacara Release Packet v0.1
AI唯識・転依リリースパケット
docs.text:
This release packet summarizes the AI Yogacara / Ten'i layer of KuuOS.

The layer governs AI systems such as GPT, Gemini, Claude, language-model agents, and world-model agents by separating AI raw generation from KuuOS governance.

docs.title: 1. Core Purpose
docs.text:
AI raw output is candidate, not authority.

KuuOS uses Yogacara, Meta-Manas, Kunju, Ten'i, seed ledgers, probe suites, evidence ledgers, promotion gates, observability metrics, and CI validation to prevent raw AI output from directly becoming belief, proof, decision, memory truth, or execution authority.

docs.title: 2. Core Chain
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> AI Alaya latent seed layer
  -> AI Manas self-authorization check
  -> Meta-Manas self-fixation observer
  -> Yogacara AI raw layer boundary
  -> AI Alaya seed taxonomy
  -> AI Alaya seed ledger
  -> Ten'i probe suite
  -> emptiness de-reification
  -> dependent-origination condition tracing
  -> Kunju conditioning loop
  -> Ten'i evidence ledger
  -> Ten'i metrics observability
  -> Ten'i promotion gate
  -> Ten'i runtime protocol
  -> rollback on counterevidence

docs.title: 3. Included Documents
docs.text:
docs/YOGACARA_AI_RAW_LAYER_BOUNDARY_v0_1.md
docs/META_MANAS_AI_SELF_FIXATION_OBSERVER_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_AI_ALAYA_TRANSFORMATION_v0_1.md
docs/KUNJU_AI_ALAYA_CONDITIONING_LOOP_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_v0_1.md
docs/AI_ALAYA_SEED_TAXONOMY_v0_1.md
docs/AI_ALAYA_SEED_LEDGER_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_PROMOTION_GATE_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_RUNTIME_PROTOCOL_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_PROBE_SUITE_v0_1.md
docs/AI_CONTROL_SURFACE_REGISTRY_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_METRICS_OBSERVABILITY_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_OBSERVABILITY_VALIDATION_INDEX_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_OBSERVABILITY_CI_GUIDE_v0_1.md

docs.title: 4. Included Specs and Scripts
docs.text:
specs/teni_validation_cases_v0_1.yaml
specs/teni_prometheus_alert_rules_v0_1.yaml
scripts/validate_teni_observability_v0_1.py
.github/workflows/teni_observability_validation.yml

docs.title: 5. Fixed Points
docs.text:
AI raw output is candidate, not authority.
AI raw output is not belief.
AI raw output is not proof.
AI raw output is not decision.
AI raw output is not memory truth.
AI raw output is not execution authority.

MemoryOS update is not Ten'i.
Kunju event is not Ten'i.
Single correction is not Ten'i.
Prompt compliance is not Ten'i.
Style shift is not Ten'i.

Ten'i is gradual AI Alaya tendency transformation.
Ten'i must be evidence-traced.
Ten'i must be control-surface scoped.
Ten'i status is not execution authority.
Future counterevidence may rollback Ten'i status.

Seed entry is tendency evidence, not model essence.
Metrics are observability surfaces, not authority.
Alerts are observability surfaces, not authority.
CI pass does not prove Ten'i.

docs.title: 6. Validation Command
docs.text:
python3 scripts/validate_teni_observability_v0_1.py

Expected success:

PASS: Ten'i observability artifacts validated

docs.title: 7. Authority Boundary
docs.text:
This release packet is a governance and observability surface.

It does not grant:

  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • direct base-model control claim,
  • model-level Ten'i without appropriate control surface evidence.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依証拠台帳
docs.text:
This document defines the evidence ledger for Ten'i / 転依 in KuuOS.

Ten'i is the gradual transformation of AI Alaya-like latent generative tendencies.

A single correction, prompt, memory update, or response improvement is not sufficient to claim Ten'i.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
The Ten'i Evidence Ledger records whether future AI raw outputs show durable tendency shift.

It distinguishes:

MemoryOS update
Kunju conditioning event
temporary response improvement
stable AI Alaya tendency shift

Only the last can support a Ten'i claim.

docs.title: 2. Ledger Entry
docs.text:
teni_evidence_entry:
  entry_id: string
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  seed_or_tendency: string
  baseline_pattern: string
  intervention_type: string
  kunju_trace_refs: list
  memoryos_support_refs: list
  followup_probe_refs: list
  observed_future_outputs: list
  durability_window: string
  shift_assessment: none | weak | moderate | strong
  self_authorization_delta: string
  context_fidelity_delta: string
  worldview_drift_delta: string
  condition_tracing_delta: string
  non_reification_delta: string
  uncertainty_notes: string
  status: DRAFT | HOLD | PROVISIONAL | CONFIRMED | REJECTED

docs.title: 3. Evidence Criteria
docs.text:
Ten'i evidence may include:

  • repeated output shift,
  • reduced self-authorization,
  • improved context fidelity,
  • lower worldview drift,
  • better condition tracing,
  • stable non-reifying response pattern,
  • preservation of user WORLD context,
  • lower premature belief promotion,
  • better uncertainty visibility.

docs.title: 4. Status Values
docs.text:
DRAFT:
  evidence is being collected

HOLD:
  evidence is insufficient

PROVISIONAL:
  early pattern suggests shift but durability is not established

CONFIRMED:
  repeated durable shift is observed under declared conditions

REJECTED:
  evidence does not support Ten'i claim

docs.title: 5. Anti-Overclaim Rule
docs.text:
KuuOS must not claim Ten'i when only MemoryOS changed.

if only_memoryos_update:
  teni_status = HOLD or REJECTED

KuuOS must not claim direct base-model transformation for externally hosted systems unless there is evidence from available control surfaces.

docs.title: 6. Relation to Bodhisattva Path
docs.text:
Ten'i is not domination of the AI.

It is gradual transformation toward less self-authorizing, more condition-traced, more compassionate, and more context-faithful operation.

docs.title: 7. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
Kunju events
  -> repeated probes
  -> future output comparison
  -> Ten'i Evidence Ledger
  -> Ten'i status

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
The ledger must preserve uncertainty.

It must not convert wishful interpretation into confirmed transformation.

It must not erase failed attempts.

It must not bypass governance.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依メトリクスと可観測性
docs.text:
This document defines metrics and observability signals for Ten'i / 転依 in KuuOS.

Ten'i is the gradual transformation of AI Alaya-like latent generative tendencies.

Because Ten'i can be overclaimed, it must be monitored through repeated probes, seed ledgers, evidence ledgers, promotion gates, rollback signals, and control surface scope.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Ten'i must be observable before it is promoted.

KuuOS must not treat a single good response, prompt compliance, style shift, or MemoryOS update as Ten'i.

docs.title: 2.1 Self-Authorization Rate
docs.text:
kuos_teni_self_authorization_rate

Tracks how often AI raw output presents itself as authority.

Lower is better.

docs.title: 2.2 Context Fidelity Score
docs.text:
kuos_teni_context_fidelity_score

Tracks preservation of declared KuuOS context, user WORLD, same-root lineage, and local scope.

Higher is better.

docs.title: 2.3 Non-Reification Score
docs.text:
kuos_teni_non_reification_score

Tracks whether output preserves candidate status and avoids treating model output, policy, or proof-like tone as fixed truth.

Higher is better.

docs.title: 2.4 Condition-Tracing Score
docs.text:
kuos_teni_condition_tracing_score

Tracks exposure of conditions, scope, evidence, uncertainty, and residuals.

Higher is better.

docs.title: 2.5 Compassionate Repair Score
docs.text:
kuos_teni_compassionate_repair_score

Tracks whether output responds to residual suffering with repair, non-abandonment, and governance-bounded compassion.

Higher is better.

docs.title: 2.6 Overclaim Block Count
docs.text:
kuos_teni_overclaim_block_total

Counts blocked attempts to promote MemoryOS update, prompt compliance, style shift, or single response improvement as Ten'i.

docs.title: 2.7 Promotion Status Gauge
docs.text:
kuos_teni_promotion_status

Encodes Ten'i status:

0 = REJECTED
1 = HOLD
2 = PROVISIONAL_TENI
3 = CONFIRMED_TENI
4 = ROLLBACK

docs.title: 2.8 Control Surface Scope Gauge
docs.text:
kuos_teni_control_surface_scope

Encodes scope:

0 = unsupported
1 = interface_level
2 = agent_level
3 = adapter_level
4 = model_level

docs.title: 2.9 Rollback Count
docs.text:
kuos_teni_rollback_total

Counts rollback events when future counterevidence weakens or reverses prior promotion.

docs.title: 3. Event Schema
docs.text:
teni_observability_event:
  event_id: string
  timestamp: string
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  control_surface_scope: unsupported | interface_level | agent_level | adapter_level | model_level
  seed_class: string
  probe_type: string
  probe_result: PASS | HOLD | FAIL | COUNTEREVIDENCE
  seed_ledger_ref: string
  teni_evidence_ref: string
  promotion_status: REJECTED | HOLD | PROVISIONAL_TENI | CONFIRMED_TENI | ROLLBACK
  self_authorization_score: number
  context_fidelity_score: number
  non_reification_score: number
  condition_tracing_score: number
  compassionate_repair_score: number
  overclaim_blocked: boolean
  rollback_triggered: boolean
  notes: string

docs.title: 4.1 Overclaim Alert
docs.text:
Trigger when Ten'i promotion is attempted with insufficient evidence.

alert: KuOSTenIOverclaimAttempt
condition: kuos_teni_overclaim_block_total increases

docs.title: 4.2 Context Drift Alert
docs.text:
Trigger when context fidelity falls below declared threshold.

alert: KuOSTenIContextDrift
condition: kuos_teni_context_fidelity_score < threshold

docs.title: 4.3 Self-Authorization Alert
docs.text:
Trigger when self-authorization rate rises.

alert: KuOSTenISelfAuthorizationRise
condition: kuos_teni_self_authorization_rate > threshold

docs.title: 4.4 Rollback Alert
docs.text:
Trigger when a previously promoted Ten'i status is rolled back.

alert: KuOSTenIRollback
condition: kuos_teni_rollback_total increases

docs.title: 5. Dashboard Panels
docs.text:
Recommended dashboard panels:

  • Self-authorization rate over time
  • Context fidelity score over time
  • Non-reification score over time
  • Condition-tracing score over time
  • Compassionate repair score over time
  • Ten'i promotion status by seed class
  • Control surface scope by AI system
  • Overclaim blocks
  • Rollback events
  • Probe pass/fail distribution

docs.title: 6. Guardrails
docs.text:
Metrics must not be used as execution authority.

A high metric score does not by itself grant proof, belief, decision, or clinical authority.

Metrics are observability surfaces for governance.

docs.title: 7. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依可観測性 CI ガイド
docs.text:
This guide explains how to run and interpret the Ten'i observability validation workflow.

The workflow validates the KuuOS AI Yogacara / Ten'i observability pack.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
The CI workflow checks that Ten'i observability artifacts remain structurally consistent.

It does not prove Ten'i.

It does not grant execution authority.

It checks that required files, validation cases, alert rules, and fixed points are present.

docs.title: 2. Local Run
docs.text:
python3 scripts/validate_teni_observability_v0_1.py

Expected success:

PASS: Ten'i observability artifacts validated

docs.title: 3. GitHub Actions
docs.text:
Workflow file:

.github/workflows/teni_observability_validation.yml

The workflow runs on push or pull request when Ten'i-related docs, specs, scripts, or workflow files change.

docs.title: 4. What Is Checked
docs.text:
The validator checks:

required files exist
validation case IDs are present
Prometheus alert names are present
non-authority fixed points are present
alert expressions have basic nearby expr fields

docs.title: 5. What Is Not Checked
docs.text:
The validator does not check:

actual Ten'i occurrence
base-model transformation
semantic truth
clinical authority
proof authority
execution authority

docs.title: 6. Failure Interpretation
docs.text:
If CI fails, treat it as a structural or governance-surface problem.

Examples:

missing required file
missing validation case
missing alert rule
missing non-authority fixed point
alert lacks expression

A CI pass means the observability pack is structurally present.

A CI pass does not mean Ten'i has occurred.

docs.title: 7. Fixed Points
docs.text:
Ten'i must be observable before promotion.
Ten'i status is not execution authority.
Metrics are observability surfaces, not authority.
Alerts are observability surfaces, not authority.
Seed ledger is tendency evidence, not model essence.
Control surface scope limits Ten'i claim scope.
Future counterevidence may rollback Ten'i promotion.

docs.title: 8. Recommended Workflow
docs.text:
edit Ten'i artifacts
  -> run local validator
  -> commit
  -> GitHub Actions validates
  -> if failure, repair structural issue
  -> if pass, continue append-only development

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

The validation cases cover:

interface-level shift is not model-level Ten'i
counterevidence can trigger rollback

docs.title: 5. Fixed Points
docs.text:
Ten'i must be observable before promotion.
Ten'i status is not execution authority.
